docs.title: sigc
docs.text:
A Rust-based quantitative finance research platform

Build, test, and deploy trading strategies with a type-safe DSL, deterministic backtester, and production-ready infrastructure.

Get Started View on GitHub

docs.title: Why sigc?
docs.text: Quant teams lose hours every week to ad hoc Python notebooks: mismatched calendars, fragile joins, non-deterministic backtests, and duplicated factor code. sigc is a composable, shape-aware DSL backed by a columnar runtime and deterministic backtester.

docs.title: Type-Safe DSL
docs.text: Write signals in a concise, readable language with compile-time type checking. Catch errors before running expensive backtests.

docs.title: Deterministic Backtesting
docs.text: Get reproducible results every time. Content-addressed caching ensures identical inputs produce identical outputs.

docs.title: 120+ Operators
docs.text: Time-series, cross-sectional, technical indicators, and portfolio construction operators built-in. No external dependencies needed.

docs.title: Production Ready
docs.text: Circuit breakers, position limits, Slack alerts, audit logging, and Prometheus metrics. Deploy with confidence.

docs.title: Single Binary
docs.text: No services to manage. Compile, run, and deploy with one sigc binary. Daemon mode available for long-lived services.

docs.title: IDE Support
docs.text: VS Code extension with syntax highlighting, code completion, error diagnostics, and 25+ code snippets.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "data/prices.parquet"

params:
  lookback = 20
  top_pct = 0.2

signal momentum:
  returns = ret(prices, lookback)
  score = zscore(returns)
  emit winsor(score, p=0.01)

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=top_pct, bottom=top_pct)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31
Bash
$ sigc run strategy.sig

=== Backtest Results ===
Total Return:         15.23%
Annualized Return:    15.23%
Sharpe Ratio:          1.45
Max Drawdown:          8.12%
Turnover:            312.00%

docs.title: Signal Development
docs.text:
Write trading signals using a concise DSL with 120+ built-in operators:

MomentumMean ReversionMulti-Factor Text Only
signal momentum:
  returns = ret(prices, 252)
  skip = ret(prices, 21)
  mom = returns - skip
  emit zscore(mom)
Text Only
signal mean_reversion:
  ma = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  std = rolling_std(prices, 20)
  z = (prices - ma) / std
  emit -zscore(z)
Text Only
signal combo:
  mom = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  vol = -zscore(rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 20))
  emit 0.6 * mom + 0.4 * vol

docs.title: Custom Functions and Macros
docs.text:
Create reusable components for your signal library:

Text Only
// Custom function
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

// Reusable macro
macro vol_adj_momentum(px: expr, ret_window: number = 20, vol_window: number = 60):
  let r = ret(px, ret_window)
  let vol = rolling_std(r, vol_window)
  emit zscore(r / vol)

signal my_signal:
  emit vol_adj_momentum(prices, 30, 90)

docs.title: Production Infrastructure
docs.text:
Deploy with enterprise-grade safety systems:

  • Circuit Breakers - Automatic trading halts on anomalies
  • Position Limits - Max weight, sector, and turnover constraints
  • Kill Switches - Emergency stop for all trading
  • Alerting - Slack, email, and console notifications
  • Audit Logging - Full compliance trail
  • Monitoring - Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Factor Models
docs.text:
Build multi-factor strategies:

Text Only
signal value:
  emit zscore(book_to_market)

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal quality:
  emit zscore(roe)

signal composite:
  emit 0.4 * value + 0.4 * momentum + 0.2 * quality

Learn more →

docs.title: Risk Models
docs.text:
Estimate and manage portfolio risk:

Text Only
portfolio risk_managed:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  constraints:
    target_volatility = 0.10
    max_beta = 1.2
    max_sector = 0.25

Learn more →

docs.title: Regime Detection
docs.text:
Adapt to market conditions:

Text Only
signal regime_aware:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  high_vol = vol > quantile(vol, 0.8)

  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  reversion = -zscore(ret(prices, 5))

  // Momentum in low vol, reversion in high vol
  emit where(high_vol, reversion, momentum)

Learn more →

docs.title: Factor Models
docs.text: Build sophisticated multi-factor portfolios.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Factor models decompose returns into systematic components:

\[R_i = \alpha_i + \beta_{1,i} F_1 + \beta_{2,i} F_2 + ... + \epsilon_i\]

docs.title: Fama-French Three Factor
docs.text:
Text Only
signal market:
  emit ret(prices, 1)

signal smb:  // Small Minus Big
  small = market_cap < median(market_cap)
  ret_small = where(small, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  ret_big = where(not(small), ret(prices, 1), 0)
  emit mean(ret_small) - mean(ret_big)

signal hml:  // High Minus Low
  value = book_to_market
  high = value > quantile(value, 0.7)
  low = value < quantile(value, 0.3)
  ret_high = where(high, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  ret_low = where(low, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  emit mean(ret_high) - mean(ret_low)

docs.title: Five Factor Model
docs.text:
Text Only
signal rmw:  // Robust Minus Weak (Profitability)
  robust = roe > quantile(roe, 0.7)
  weak = roe < quantile(roe, 0.3)
  ret_robust = where(robust, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  ret_weak = where(weak, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  emit mean(ret_robust) - mean(ret_weak)

signal cma:  // Conservative Minus Aggressive (Investment)
  conservative = asset_growth < quantile(asset_growth, 0.3)
  aggressive = asset_growth > quantile(asset_growth, 0.7)
  ret_cons = where(conservative, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  ret_agg = where(aggressive, ret(prices, 1), 0)
  emit mean(ret_cons) - mean(ret_agg)

docs.title: Single Factor
Composite Factor
docs.text:
Text Only
signal value:
  book_value = zscore(book_to_market)
  earnings_yield = zscore(1 / pe_ratio)
  fcf_yield = zscore(fcf / market_cap)

  // Equal weight combination
  emit (book_value + earnings_yield + fcf_yield) / 3

signal quality:
  profitability = zscore(roe)
  stability = -zscore(rolling_std(roe, 4))  // Quarterly
  leverage = -zscore(debt_to_equity)

  emit (profitability + stability + leverage) / 3

signal composite:
  // Weight factors
  emit 0.4 * value + 0.4 * momentum + 0.2 * quality

docs.title: Static Weighting
docs.text:
Text Only
signal static_blend:
  emit 0.25 * momentum + 0.25 * value + 0.25 * quality + 0.25 * low_vol

docs.title: Dynamic Weighting
docs.text:
Text Only
signal dynamic_blend:
  // Adjust weights based on regime
  vol_regime = rolling_std(ret(market, 1), 60)
  high_vol = vol_regime > quantile(vol_regime, 0.8)

  // More defensive in high vol
  mom_weight = where(high_vol, 0.2, 0.4)
  val_weight = where(high_vol, 0.3, 0.2)
  qual_weight = where(high_vol, 0.3, 0.2)
  lowvol_weight = where(high_vol, 0.2, 0.2)

  emit mom_weight * momentum +
       val_weight * value +
       qual_weight * quality +
       lowvol_weight * low_vol

docs.title: Rank-Based Blending
docs.text:
Text Only
signal rank_blend:
  // Rank each factor
  r_mom = rank(momentum)
  r_val = rank(value)
  r_qual = rank(quality)

  // Average ranks
  avg_rank = (r_mom + r_val + r_qual) / 3

  emit avg_rank

docs.title: Sector Neutral
docs.text:
Text Only
signal sector_neutral_momentum:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  emit neutralize(raw, by=sectors)

docs.title: Industry Neutral
docs.text:
Text Only
signal industry_neutral_value:
  raw = zscore(book_to_market)
  emit neutralize(raw, by=industry)

docs.title: Multi-Level Neutralization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal double_neutral:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))

  // First sector neutral
  sector_neutral = neutralize(raw, by=sectors)

  // Then size neutral
  emit neutralize(sector_neutral, by=size_bucket)

docs.title: Market Cap Weighted Neutralization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal cap_weighted_neutral:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  sector_mean = group_mean(raw, by=sectors, weights=market_cap)
  emit raw - sector_mean

docs.title: Momentum Factor Timing
docs.text:
Text Only
signal timed_momentum:
  mom = zscore(ret(prices, 60))

  // Factor momentum (trailing 12-month factor performance)
  factor_perf = rolling_mean(mom, 252)
  factor_strong = factor_perf > 0

  // Only use momentum when factor is working
  emit where(factor_strong, mom, 0)

docs.title: Value Spread Timing
docs.text:
Text Only
signal timed_value:
  value = zscore(book_to_market)

  // Value spread
  high_val = quantile(value, 0.9)
  low_val = quantile(value, 0.1)
  spread = high_val - low_val

  // Wide spread = value likely to work
  wide_spread = spread > quantile(spread, 0.7)

  emit where(wide_spread, value, 0)

docs.title: Target Exposures
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio factor_targeted:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  constraints:
    // Target factor exposures
    factor_exposure:
      market: [0.8, 1.2]    // Beta between 0.8 and 1.2
      smb: [-0.2, 0.2]      // Small size neutral
      hml: [0.0, 0.5]       // Slight value tilt
      momentum: [0.3, 0.7]  // Moderate momentum

docs.title: Factor Hedging
In Backtest
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(composite).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  backtest factors=[MKT, SMB, HML, MOM] from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

// === Factor Definitions ===

signal momentum:
  raw = ret(prices, 252) - ret(prices, 21)
  z = zscore(raw)
  clean = winsor(z, p=0.01)
  emit neutralize(clean, by=sectors)

signal value:
  bm = zscore(book_to_market)
  ey = zscore(earnings / market_cap)
  raw = 0.5 * bm + 0.5 * ey
  clean = winsor(raw, p=0.01)
  emit neutralize(clean, by=sectors)

signal quality:
  prof = zscore(roe)
  stab = -zscore(rolling_std(earnings, 4))
  lev = -zscore(debt_to_equity)
  raw = (prof + stab + lev) / 3
  clean = winsor(raw, p=0.01)
  emit neutralize(clean, by=sectors)

signal low_volatility:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 252)
  raw = -zscore(vol)  // Low vol = high signal
  emit neutralize(raw, by=sectors)

// === Composite Signal ===

signal composite:
  emit 0.30 * momentum +
       0.30 * value +
       0.25 * quality +
       0.15 * low_volatility

// === Portfolio Construction ===

portfolio multi_factor:
  weights = rank(composite).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)

  constraints:
    max_sector = 0.25
    dollar_neutral = true

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest rebal=21 factors=[MKT, SMB, HML, MOM]
           from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Incremental Computation
docs.text: Update strategies efficiently when new data arrives.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Instead of recomputing everything from scratch, sigc can incrementally update:

  • Rolling statistics
  • Signal values
  • Portfolio weights

docs.title: Traditional vs Incremental
docs.text:
Traditional (Full Recompute): Text Only

Day N: Compute everything from scratch
Day N+1: Compute everything from scratch again

Incremental: Text Only

Day N: Compute everything
Day N+1: Update only what changed

docs.title: Performance Comparison
docs.text: Operation Full Recompute Incremental Speedup Rolling mean (252) O(n × 252) O(n) 252x Rolling std (60) O(n × 60) O(n) 60x EMA (20) O(n × 20) O(n) 20x

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    enabled: true
    checkpoint_interval: 1d  # Save state daily

docs.title: CLI Flag
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --incremental

docs.title: Rolling Mean
docs.text:
Maintains running sum:

Text Only
new_mean = old_mean + (new_value - oldest_value) / window

docs.title: Rolling Standard Deviation
docs.text:
Uses Welford's online algorithm:

Text Only
new_variance = update(old_variance, new_value, oldest_value)
new_std = sqrt(new_variance)

docs.title: Exponential Moving Average
docs.text:
Naturally incremental:

Text Only
new_ema = alpha * new_value + (1 - alpha) * old_ema

docs.title: Checkpoint Files
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    enabled: true
    checkpoint_dir: "./checkpoints"
    checkpoint_interval: 1d

Checkpoint structure: Text Only

checkpoints/
├── momentum.state
├── value.state
└── portfolio.state

docs.title: Checkpoint Contents
docs.text:
Text Only
Checkpoint: momentum
  Last Date: 2024-01-15
  Rolling Buffers:
    - ret_60: [252 values per asset]
    - zscore_252: [252 values per asset]
  Computed Values:
    - AAPL: 1.234
    - GOOGL: -0.567
    ...

docs.title: Automatic Incremental Updates
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  enabled: true
  mode: incremental

  schedule:
    - cron: "0 16 * * 1-5"  # 4 PM weekdays
      action: update

docs.title: Update Flow
docs.text:
Text Only
1. Load checkpoint from previous run
2. Fetch new data since checkpoint
3. Update rolling statistics
4. Compute new signal values
5. Generate weights
6. Save new checkpoint

docs.title: Buffer Management
docs.text:
Only keeps required history:

YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    enabled: true
    max_history: 252  # Maximum lookback needed

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
If your longest lookback is 252 days: Text Only

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 252))  // Needs 252 days

sigc keeps only 252 days in memory, not full history.

docs.title: Fully Incremental
docs.text: Operation State Size rolling_mean O(window) rolling_std O(window) rolling_sum O(window) ema O(1) lag O(lag) diff O(1)

docs.title: Partially Incremental
docs.text: Operation Notes rolling_corr Requires covariance state rolling_rank Requires sorted buffer quantile Requires sorted buffer

docs.title: Non-Incremental
docs.text:
Some operations require full recompute:

Operation Reason zscore (cross-sectional) Needs all assets rank (cross-sectional) Needs all assets neutralize Needs all assets

docs.title: Dependency Graph
docs.text:
Text Only
signal a:
  emit rolling_mean(prices, 20)  // Independent

signal b:
  emit rolling_std(prices, 20)  // Independent

signal c:
  emit a / b  // Depends on a and b

Update order: Text Only

1. Update a (parallel)
2. Update b (parallel)
3. Update c (after a and b complete)

docs.title: Automatic Ordering
docs.text: sigc automatically determines update order based on dependencies.

docs.title: Checkpoint Validation
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    validate_checkpoints: true

Validates: - Checkpoint date matches expected - All assets present - Buffer sizes correct

docs.title: Recovery Options
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    on_error: recompute  # Full recompute on error
    # or: fail          # Stop and report

docs.title: Full Recompute Triggers
docs.text:
Force full recompute when:

  1. No checkpoint exists
  2. Strategy changed
  3. Data gap detected
  4. Checkpoint corrupted
  5. Manual trigger
Bash
# Force full recompute
sigc run strategy.sig --no-incremental

docs.title: 1. Set Checkpoint Interval
docs.text:
Balance between: - Too frequent: Disk I/O overhead - Too rare: Long recovery time

YAML
checkpoint_interval: 1d  # Good default

docs.title: 2. Monitor Checkpoint Size
docs.text:
Bash
sigc status --checkpoints

docs.title: 3. Validate Periodically
docs.text:
Run full recompute periodically to verify:

YAML
schedule:
  - cron: "0 0 * * 0"  # Weekly Sunday
    action: full_recompute

docs.title: 4. Handle Universe Changes
docs.text:
When assets are added/removed:

YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    on_universe_change: recompute

docs.title: Example: Daily Update Workflow
docs.text:
YAML
# config.yaml
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"

daemon:
  enabled: true

  schedule:
    - cron: "0 16 * * 1-5"
      action: update

performance:
  incremental:
    enabled: true
    checkpoint_dir: "./checkpoints"
    checkpoint_interval: 1d

# Strategy
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

Daily execution: Text Only

4:00 PM - Daemon triggers
4:00:01 - Load checkpoint
4:00:02 - Fetch today's prices
4:00:03 - Update rolling returns
4:00:04 - Compute new signals
4:00:05 - Generate weights
4:00:06 - Save checkpoint
4:00:07 - Output weights

docs.title: Slow Incremental Updates
docs.text:
Check if cross-sectional operations are the bottleneck:

Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --incremental --profile

docs.title: Checkpoint Mismatch
docs.text:
Text Only
Error: Checkpoint date mismatch
Expected: 2024-01-15
Found: 2024-01-14

Solution: Check for missing data or run full recompute.

docs.title: Memory Issues
docs.text:
Reduce checkpoint buffer sizes:

YAML
performance:
  incremental:
    max_history: 126  # Reduce if possible

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Memory Mapping - Large dataset handling
  • Parallel Execution - Multi-core processing
  • Daemon Mode - Production scheduling

docs.title: Memory Mapping
docs.text: Handle datasets larger than available RAM efficiently.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Memory mapping allows sigc to:

  • Work with datasets larger than RAM
  • Share data between processes
  • Reduce startup time for large files

docs.title: Enable Memory Mapping
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  source = "large_dataset.parquet"
  format = parquet

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true

docs.title: With Limits
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    max_mapped_size_gb: 50

  memory:
    max_memory_gb: 8  # Physical RAM limit

docs.title: Parquet (Recommended)
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true

Parquet advantages: - Columnar format - Built-in compression - Efficient for analytics

docs.title: Arrow IPC
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  source = "prices.arrow"
  format = arrow

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    zero_copy: true  # No data copying

docs.title: Memory-Mapped CSV
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  source = "prices.csv"
  format = csv

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    preprocess: true  # Convert to arrow format first

docs.title: Read-Only Access
docs.text:
Default mode, optimal for most cases:

YAML
performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    mode: read_only

docs.title: Shared Access
docs.text:
Multiple processes can read the same mapped file:

YAML
performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    mode: shared

docs.title: Physical RAM Usage
docs.text:
Memory mapping doesn't load everything into RAM:

Text Only
10 GB file, 4 GB RAM:
- Pages loaded on demand
- Unused pages evicted by OS
- Only active data in RAM

docs.title: Controlling Memory
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  memory:
    max_memory_gb: 8       # Hard limit
    target_memory_gb: 6    # Target usage

  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    prefetch: false        # Don't prefetch pages

docs.title: Historical Backtesting
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "10_years_data.parquet"  // 50 GB file
  format = parquet

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 252))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Multi-Asset Universe
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  source = "global_equities.parquet"  // 5000+ assets
  format = parquet

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true

  parallel:
    enabled: true
    workers: 8

docs.title: Column Selection
docs.text:
Only access needed columns:

YAML
data:
  source = "all_data.parquet"
  format = parquet
  columns:
    date: Date
    ticker: Symbol
    close: Numeric as prices
    volume: Numeric as volume
  # Other columns not loaded

docs.title: Row Filtering
docs.text:
Filter early to reduce data:

YAML
data:
  source = "global_data.parquet"
  filter:
    - column: country
      value: "US"
    - column: market_cap
      op: ">"
      value: 1000000000  # $1B+

docs.title: Chunked Processing
docs.text:
For very large files:

YAML
performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    chunk_size_mb: 256  # Process in chunks

docs.title: Load Time Comparison
docs.text: Dataset Size Traditional Memory Mapped 1 GB 5s 0.1s 10 GB 50s 0.5s 50 GB Out of memory 2s

docs.title: Query Performance
docs.text:
Query Traditional Memory Mapped Single asset 10ms 15ms Full scan 500ms 600ms Filtered scan 200ms 180ms

Memory mapping adds slight overhead for cached data but enables working with larger datasets.

docs.title: Convert CSV to Parquet
docs.text:
Bash
sigc convert prices.csv prices.parquet

docs.title: Optimize Parquet
docs.text:
Bash
sigc optimize prices.parquet --row-group-size 100000 --compression zstd

docs.title: Create Arrow Format
docs.text:
Bash
sigc convert prices.parquet prices.arrow --format arrow

docs.title: Multiple Backtests
docs.text:
Run parameter sweeps sharing data:

YAML
# config.yaml
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"

performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    mode: shared  # Share across processes
Bash
# Run multiple backtests sharing same mapped file
sigc run strategy.sig --param lookback=20 &
sigc run strategy.sig --param lookback=40 &
sigc run strategy.sig --param lookback=60 &
wait

All processes share the same memory-mapped file, reducing total RAM usage.

docs.title: Slow Performance
docs.text:
Symptom: Memory-mapped file slower than expected

Cause: Too much paging (disk thrashing)

Solution: YAML

performance:
  memory:
    max_memory_gb: 16  # Increase if available

docs.title: Out of Virtual Memory
docs.text:
Symptom: "Cannot map file" error

Cause: 32-bit process or system limits

Solution: Use 64-bit system, check ulimits

docs.title: Page Faults
docs.text:
Symptom: High page fault count

Cause: Random access pattern

Solution: YAML

performance:
  memory_map:
    prefetch: true      # Prefetch data
    sequential_hint: true  # Optimize for sequential access

docs.title: 1. Use Parquet for Large Files
docs.text: Parquet + memory mapping is optimal: - Columnar layout - Compression - Column pruning

docs.title: 2. Pre-filter Data
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  filter:
    - column: date
      op: ">="
      value: "2020-01-01"

docs.title: 3. Select Only Needed Columns
docs.text:
YAML
columns:
  date: Date
  ticker: Symbol
  close: Numeric as prices
  # Don't include columns you don't use

docs.title: 4. Monitor Memory Usage
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --benchmark

Shows memory statistics including mapped memory.

docs.title: 5. Use SSD Storage
docs.text: Memory mapping benefits from fast storage: - SSD: Good performance - NVMe: Best performance - HDD: Acceptable for sequential access

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
Bash
# Increase max mapped memory
sudo sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144

# Make permanent
echo "vm.max_map_count=262144" | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf

docs.title: macOS
docs.text: Generally no configuration needed for most use cases.

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
Use large pages for better performance:

YAML
performance:
  memory_map:
    enabled: true
    large_pages: true  # Requires admin rights

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Parallel Execution - Multi-core processing
  • Incremental Computation - Efficient updates
  • Configuration - Full config reference

docs.title: Parallel Execution
docs.text: Leverage multi-core processing for faster computation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
sigc automatically parallelizes:

  • Cross-sectional computations (across assets)
  • Independent signal calculations
  • Parameter optimization
  • Walk-forward windows

docs.title: Enable Parallelism
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  parallel:
    enabled: true
    workers: 8  # Number of threads

docs.title: Auto-Detection
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  parallel:
    enabled: true
    workers: auto  # Use all available cores

docs.title: Cross-Sectional Operations
docs.text:
Operations across assets run in parallel:

Text Only
signal momentum:
  // This runs in parallel across all assets
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

docs.title: Independent Signals
docs.text:
Multiple signals compute simultaneously:

Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal value:
  emit zscore(book_to_market)

signal quality:
  emit zscore(roe)

// All three signals compute in parallel
signal combined:
  emit 0.33 * momentum + 0.33 * value + 0.34 * quality

docs.title: Parameter Grid
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback: range(20, 120, 20)
  top_pct: range(0.1, 0.4, 0.1)

// Each parameter combination runs in parallel
portfolio optimized:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=top_pct, bottom=top_pct)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Walk-Forward Windows
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio validated:
  backtest walk_forward(
    train_years = 5,
    test_years = 2,
    step_years = 2,
    parallel = true  // Windows compute in parallel
  ) from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Basic Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  parallel:
    enabled: true
    workers: 8
    stack_size_mb: 2

docs.title: Advanced Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  parallel:
    enabled: true
    workers: 8

    # Thread pool settings
    thread_pool:
      name: "sigc-compute"
      stack_size_mb: 4

    # Work stealing
    work_stealing: true

    # Granularity
    min_batch_size: 100  # Min items per thread

docs.title: Optimal Worker Count
docs.text:
YAML
# CPU-bound: Use all cores
workers: auto

# Memory-bound: Use fewer
workers: 4

# I/O-bound: Can exceed core count
workers: 16

docs.title: Batch Size
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  parallel:
    min_batch_size: 100  # Minimum items per thread

Small batch = more overhead Large batch = less parallelism

docs.title: Memory Considerations
docs.text:
YAML
performance:
  parallel:
    workers: 4  # Reduce if memory-constrained
  memory:
    max_memory_gb: 8

docs.title: Benchmark Mode
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --benchmark

Output:

Text Only
Performance Benchmark:
======================
Total Time: 2.34s
  Data Loading: 0.45s (19%)
  Signal Computation: 1.52s (65%)
  Portfolio Construction: 0.25s (11%)
  Backtest Simulation: 0.12s (5%)

Parallelism:
  Workers Used: 8
  Parallel Efficiency: 85%
  Speedup vs Serial: 6.8x

Memory:
  Peak Usage: 1.2 GB
  Data Size: 850 MB

docs.title: Profiling
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --profile

Generates detailed profile report.

docs.title: CLI Options
docs.text:
Bash
# Specify worker count
sigc run strategy.sig --workers 8

# Disable parallelism
sigc run strategy.sig --workers 1

# Auto-detect
sigc run strategy.sig --workers auto

docs.title: 1. Match Workers to Cores
docs.text:
YAML
workers: auto  # Or number of physical cores

docs.title: 2. Profile Before Optimizing
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --benchmark

docs.title: 3. Consider Memory
docs.text: More workers = more memory usage.

docs.title: 4. Batch Small Operations
docs.text: Very small operations have parallelism overhead.

docs.title: 5. Use SIMD Where Available
docs.text: sigc uses SIMD for rolling statistics automatically.

docs.title: Sequential Dependencies
docs.text:
Some operations must be sequential:

Text Only
// Cumulative operations are sequential
cum_ret = cumsum(daily_return)

docs.title: Cross-Time Dependencies
docs.text:
Text Only
// Requires previous values
ema = ema(prices, 20)  // Sequential per asset

docs.title: Memory Bandwidth
docs.text: Parallelism limited by memory bandwidth for large datasets.

docs.title: High CPU, Low Speedup
docs.text:
• Too many small tasks
  • Memory bandwidth limited
  • Lock contention

docs.title: Memory Issues
docs.text:
YAML
# Reduce parallelism
workers: 4

# Or increase memory limit
memory:
  max_memory_gb: 16

docs.title: Inconsistent Results
docs.text:
Ensure operations are deterministic:

YAML
performance:
  deterministic: true
  seed: 42

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Incremental Computation - Efficient updates
  • Memory Mapping - Large datasets
  • Configuration - Full config reference

docs.title: Portfolio Optimization
docs.text: Construct optimal portfolios beyond simple equal-weighting.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Portfolio optimization finds weights that maximize objectives subject to constraints:

\[\max_w \quad f(w) \quad \text{subject to} \quad g(w) \leq 0\]

docs.title: Classic Markowitz
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio mean_variance:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("sharpe"),
    expected_returns = signal,
    covariance = rolling_cov(ret(prices, 1), 60),
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
      min_weight = 0.0
      max_weight = 0.10
  )

docs.title: With Risk Aversion
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio risk_averse:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("utility"),
    utility = expected_return - 0.5 * risk_aversion * variance,
    risk_aversion = 3.0,  // Higher = more risk averse
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
  )

docs.title: Minimum Variance
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio min_variance:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = minimize("variance"),
    covariance = rolling_cov(ret(prices, 1), 60),
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
      min_weight = 0.0
  )

docs.title: Risk Parity
docs.text:
Equal risk contribution from each asset:

Text Only
portfolio risk_parity:
  // Each asset contributes equally to portfolio risk
  weights = optimize(
    objective = equalize("risk_contribution"),
    covariance = rolling_cov(ret(prices, 1), 60),
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
      min_weight = 0.01
  )

docs.title: Simplified Risk Parity
docs.text:
Text Only
signal risk_parity_weights:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  inv_vol = 1 / vol
  weights = inv_vol / sum(inv_vol)
  emit weights

docs.title: Maximum Diversification
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio max_diversification:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("diversification_ratio"),
    // DR = weighted avg vol / portfolio vol
    covariance = rolling_cov(ret(prices, 1), 60),
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
      min_weight = 0.0
  )

docs.title: Long-Short with Constraints
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio constrained:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("expected_return"),
    expected_returns = signal,
    covariance = cov_matrix,
    constraints:
      // Exposure constraints
      gross_exposure = 2.0      // 200% gross
      net_exposure = 0.0        // Dollar neutral

      // Position constraints
      min_weight = -0.05
      max_weight = 0.05

      // Sector constraints
      max_sector_exposure = 0.25

      // Risk constraints
      max_volatility = 0.15
  )

docs.title: Tracking Error Constraint
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio tracking:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("information_ratio"),
    benchmark = spy_weights,
    constraints:
      max_tracking_error = 0.05  // 5% TE
      max_active_weight = 0.03
  )

docs.title: Black-Litterman
docs.text:
Combine market equilibrium with views:

Text Only
portfolio black_litterman:
  // Market equilibrium returns (from CAPM)
  equilibrium = market_cap_weights * risk_aversion * cov_matrix

  // Your views
  views:
    - asset: AAPL
      return: 0.15
      confidence: 0.8
    - asset: MSFT
      return: 0.12
      confidence: 0.6

  weights = optimize(
    method = "black_litterman",
    equilibrium_returns = equilibrium,
    views = views,
    covariance = cov_matrix,
    tau = 0.05  // Uncertainty in equilibrium
  )

docs.title: Hierarchical Risk Parity
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio hrp:
  weights = optimize(
    method = "hierarchical_risk_parity",
    returns = ret(prices, 1),
    lookback = 252,
    linkage = "ward"  // Clustering method
  )

docs.title: Uncertainty in Returns
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio robust:
  weights = optimize(
    method = "robust_mean_variance",
    expected_returns = signal,
    return_uncertainty = 0.02,  // ±2% uncertainty
    covariance = cov_matrix,
    constraints:
      max_weight = 0.10
  )

docs.title: Resampled Optimization
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio resampled:
  weights = optimize(
    method = "resampled_efficient_frontier",
    expected_returns = signal,
    covariance = cov_matrix,
    n_samples = 1000,
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
  )

docs.title: Transaction Cost Aware
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio turnover_penalized:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("return - turnover_cost"),
    expected_returns = signal,
    current_weights = previous_weights,
    turnover_cost = 0.001,  // 10 bps per unit turnover
    constraints:
      sum_weights = 1.0
  )

docs.title: Multi-Period Optimization
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio multi_period:
  weights = optimize(
    method = "multi_period",
    horizon = 5,  // 5 periods ahead
    expected_returns = [signal_t1, signal_t2, signal_t3, signal_t4, signal_t5],
    transaction_costs = tc.bps(10),
    constraints:
      max_turnover_per_period = 0.25
  )

docs.title: Factor-Constrained
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio factor_constrained:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("alpha"),
    alpha = signal,
    factor_exposures:
      market: [0.9, 1.1]     // Beta between 0.9 and 1.1
      size: [-0.1, 0.1]      // Size neutral
      value: [0.0, 0.3]      // Slight value tilt
    constraints:
      gross_exposure = 2.0
      net_exposure = 0.0
  )

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Alpha signal
signal alpha:
  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  value = zscore(book_to_market)
  quality = zscore(roe)
  combined = 0.4 * momentum + 0.4 * value + 0.2 * quality
  emit neutralize(combined, by=sectors)

// Covariance estimation
signal covariance:
  returns = ret(prices, 1)
  // Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage
  cov = shrunk_covariance(returns, 252, method="ledoit_wolf")
  emit cov

// Optimized portfolio
portfolio optimized:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("sharpe"),
    expected_returns = alpha,
    covariance = covariance,

    constraints:
      // Exposure
      gross_exposure = 2.0
      net_exposure = 0.0

      // Position limits
      min_weight = -0.05
      max_weight = 0.05

      // Sector limits
      max_sector = 0.25

      // Risk limits
      max_volatility = 0.12

      // Factor limits
      beta: [0.8, 1.2]

      // Turnover
      max_turnover = 0.25
  )

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest rebal=21 from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Optimization Methods
docs.text: Method Best For Complexity Mean-Variance Simple cases Low Risk Parity Equal risk Low Min Variance Risk reduction Medium Black-Litterman Incorporating views Medium HRP Robustness Medium Robust Uncertain inputs High

docs.title: 1. Use Shrinkage Estimation
docs.text:
Text Only
cov = shrunk_covariance(returns, 252)

docs.title: 2. Add Position Limits
docs.text: Unconstrained optimization produces extreme weights.

docs.title: 3. Penalize Turnover
docs.text:
Text Only
objective = return - turnover_cost * turnover

docs.title: 4. Test Robustness
docs.text: Small input changes shouldn't cause large weight changes.

docs.title: 5. Monitor Factor Exposures
docs.text: Ensure optimization doesn't create unintended bets.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Risk Models - Covariance estimation
  • Factor Models - Factor constraints
  • Constraints - Constraint types

docs.title: Regime Detection
docs.text: Identify market regimes and adapt strategies accordingly.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Markets exhibit different regimes:

  • Bull/Bear: Trending up or down
  • High/Low Volatility: Calm vs turbulent
  • Risk-On/Risk-Off: Appetite for risk
  • Correlation Regimes: Assets moving together or apart

docs.title: Simple Volatility Detection
docs.text:
Text Only
signal vol_regime:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  vol_20 = rolling_std(daily_ret, 20) * sqrt(252)
  vol_60 = rolling_std(daily_ret, 60) * sqrt(252)

  // Compare short-term to long-term vol
  vol_ratio = vol_20 / vol_60

  // High vol regime
  high_vol = vol_ratio > 1.5

  emit high_vol

docs.title: VIX-Based Detection
docs.text:
Text Only
signal vix_regime:
  // VIX levels
  low_vol = vix < 15
  normal_vol = vix >= 15 and vix < 25
  high_vol = vix >= 25
  extreme_vol = vix >= 35

  // Encode as numeric
  regime = where(extreme_vol, 4,
           where(high_vol, 3,
           where(normal_vol, 2, 1)))

  emit regime

docs.title: Regime-Adaptive Strategy
docs.text:
Text Only
signal adaptive_momentum:
  // Detect regime
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60) * sqrt(252)
  high_vol = vol > quantile(vol, 0.8)

  // Different signals for different regimes
  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  reversion = -zscore(ret(prices, 5))

  // Momentum in low vol, reversion in high vol
  signal = where(high_vol, reversion, momentum)

  emit signal

docs.title: Moving Average Regime
docs.text:
Text Only
signal trend_regime:
  ma_50 = rolling_mean(prices, 50)
  ma_200 = rolling_mean(prices, 200)

  // Trend classification
  uptrend = ma_50 > ma_200
  downtrend = ma_50 < ma_200

  emit where(uptrend, 1, where(downtrend, -1, 0))

docs.title: ADX Trend Strength
docs.text:
Text Only
signal trend_strength:
  // Simplified ADX
  high_change = high - lag(high, 1)
  low_change = lag(low, 1) - low

  plus_dm = where(high_change > low_change and high_change > 0, high_change, 0)
  minus_dm = where(low_change > high_change and low_change > 0, low_change, 0)

  atr_14 = atr(high, low, close, 14)

  plus_di = 100 * rolling_mean(plus_dm, 14) / atr_14
  minus_di = 100 * rolling_mean(minus_dm, 14) / atr_14

  dx = 100 * abs(plus_di - minus_di) / (plus_di + minus_di)
  adx = rolling_mean(dx, 14)

  // Strong trend > 25
  strong_trend = adx > 25

  emit strong_trend

docs.title: Trend-Following Adaptive
docs.text:
Text Only
signal trend_adaptive:
  // Detect trend strength
  ma_50 = rolling_mean(prices, 50)
  ma_200 = rolling_mean(prices, 200)
  trend_signal = (ma_50 - ma_200) / ma_200

  // Trend strength
  trend_strength = abs(trend_signal)
  strong_trend = trend_strength > 0.05

  // Momentum for trends, mean reversion for range
  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  reversion = -zscore((prices - ma_50) / ma_50)

  signal = where(strong_trend, momentum, reversion)

  emit signal

docs.title: Market Correlation
docs.text:
Text Only
signal corr_regime:
  // Average pairwise correlation
  ret_1d = ret(prices, 1)
  avg_corr = cross_sectional_correlation(ret_1d, 60)

  // High correlation = risk-off
  high_corr = avg_corr > 0.6

  emit high_corr

docs.title: Dispersion Trading
docs.text:
Text Only
signal dispersion:
  ret_1d = ret(prices, 1)

  // Cross-sectional dispersion
  dispersion = std(ret_1d)  // Cross-sectional std
  avg_dispersion = rolling_mean(dispersion, 60)

  // High dispersion = stock picking opportunity
  high_dispersion = dispersion > avg_dispersion * 1.5

  emit high_dispersion

docs.title: Two-State HMM
docs.text:
YAML
regime_detection:
  method: hmm
  states: 2
  features:
    - returns
    - volatility
  lookback: 252

docs.title: Using HMM Output
docs.text:
Text Only
signal hmm_adaptive:
  // hmm_state from external model (0 or 1)
  bull_regime = hmm_state == 1

  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  defensive = zscore(1 / rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60))

  // Momentum in bull, defensive in bear
  signal = where(bull_regime, momentum, defensive)

  emit signal

docs.title: Growth/Inflation Matrix
Complete Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_with_vix.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Regime detection
signal volatility_regime:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 20) * sqrt(252)
  long_vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60) * sqrt(252)
  high_vol = vol > long_vol * 1.3
  emit high_vol

signal trend_regime:
  ma_50 = rolling_mean(prices, 50)
  ma_200 = rolling_mean(prices, 200)
  uptrend = ma_50 > ma_200
  emit uptrend

// Strategy signals
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal mean_reversion:
  z = (prices - rolling_mean(prices, 20)) / rolling_std(prices, 20)
  emit -zscore(z)

signal low_volatility:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  emit -zscore(vol)

// Regime-adaptive composite
signal adaptive:
  high_vol = volatility_regime
  uptrend = trend_regime

  // Strategy weights by regime
  // Bull + Low Vol: Full momentum
  // Bull + High Vol: Momentum + Low Vol
  // Bear + Low Vol: Mean reversion
  // Bear + High Vol: Defensive (low vol)

  bull_low_vol = uptrend and not(high_vol)
  bull_high_vol = uptrend and high_vol
  bear_low_vol = not(uptrend) and not(high_vol)
  bear_high_vol = not(uptrend) and high_vol

  signal = where(bull_low_vol, momentum,
           where(bull_high_vol, 0.5 * momentum + 0.5 * low_volatility,
           where(bear_low_vol, mean_reversion,
                 low_volatility)))

  emit signal

portfolio regime_switching:
  weights = rank(adaptive).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)

  constraints:
    max_sector = 0.25

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest rebal=21 from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Smooth Transitions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal smooth_regime:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 20) * sqrt(252)
  vol_percentile = ts_rank(vol, 252) / 252

  // Smooth weight from 0 (low vol) to 1 (high vol)
  high_vol_weight = clip(vol_percentile, 0, 1)

  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  defensive = -zscore(vol)

  // Blend based on regime probability
  signal = (1 - high_vol_weight) * momentum + high_vol_weight * defensive

  emit signal

docs.title: 1. Use Multiple Indicators
docs.text:
Don't rely on single regime indicator:

Text Only
// Combine multiple signals
vol_high = vol > threshold1
trend_down = ma_50 < ma_200
corr_high = avg_corr > threshold2

defensive_regime = vol_high or trend_down or corr_high

docs.title: 2. Avoid Frequent Switching
docs.text:
Text Only
// Add persistence
regime_raw = vol > threshold
regime_smooth = rolling_mean(regime_raw, 10) > 0.5

docs.title: 3. Test Regime Stability
docs.text: Ensure regime detection is robust out-of-sample.

docs.title: 4. Consider Transaction Costs
docs.text: Regime switching increases turnover.

docs.title: Risk Models
docs.text: Estimate and manage portfolio risk.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Risk models help:

  • Estimate portfolio volatility
  • Decompose risk by factors
  • Set risk budgets
  • Monitor risk in real-time

docs.title: Historical Volatility
docs.text:
Text Only
signal hist_vol:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  vol_20d = rolling_std(daily_ret, 20) * sqrt(252)
  vol_60d = rolling_std(daily_ret, 60) * sqrt(252)

  emit vol_60d

docs.title: Exponential Weighted
docs.text:
Text Only
signal ewma_vol:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)

  // Exponentially weighted variance
  decay = 0.94  // RiskMetrics decay factor
  var = ema(daily_ret * daily_ret, 20)
  vol = sqrt(var) * sqrt(252)

  emit vol

docs.title: GARCH-Style
docs.text:
Text Only
signal garch_vol:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)

  // Simplified GARCH(1,1)
  omega = 0.00001
  alpha = 0.1
  beta = 0.85

  // Update variance
  prev_var = lag(variance, 1)
  variance = omega + alpha * daily_ret * daily_ret + beta * prev_var
  vol = sqrt(variance) * sqrt(252)

  emit vol

docs.title: Rolling Correlation
docs.text:
Text Only
signal rolling_corr:
  ret_a = ret(stock_a, 1)
  ret_b = ret(stock_b, 1)

  corr_60d = rolling_corr(ret_a, ret_b, 60)

  emit corr_60d

docs.title: Shrinkage Estimation
docs.text:
For stable correlation matrices:

Text Only
signal shrunk_corr:
  // Shrink sample correlation toward identity
  sample_corr = correlation_matrix(returns)
  shrinkage = 0.2

  shrunk = (1 - shrinkage) * sample_corr + shrinkage * identity

  emit shrunk

docs.title: Sample Covariance
docs.text:
Text Only
signal sample_cov:
  returns = ret(prices, 1)
  cov = rolling_cov(returns, 60)
  emit cov

docs.title: Ledoit-Wolf Shrinkage
docs.text:
YAML
risk:
  covariance:
    method: ledoit_wolf
    lookback: 252
    shrinkage: auto  # Optimal shrinkage

docs.title: Factor Covariance
docs.text:
YAML
risk:
  covariance:
    method: factor
    factors: [market, size, value, momentum]
    specific_risk: diagonal

docs.title: Portfolio Volatility
Value at Risk
docs.text:
Text Only
signal var_95:
  returns = ret(prices, 1)
  port_ret = sum(weights * returns)

  // Historical VaR
  var = quantile(port_ret, 0.05)

  emit var

docs.title: Conditional VaR (CVaR)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal cvar_95:
  returns = ret(prices, 1)
  port_ret = sum(weights * returns)

  // Average of worst 5%
  var = quantile(port_ret, 0.05)
  cvar = mean(where(port_ret < var, port_ret, 0))

  emit cvar

docs.title: Factor Decomposition
docs.text:
Text Only
Portfolio Risk = Factor Risk + Specific Risk

Factor Risk = Σ (βi × βj × σi × σj × ρij)
Specific Risk = Σ (wi² × σi_specific²)

docs.title: Risk Attribution
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  risk_attribution:
    factors: [market, size, value, momentum]
    output: risk_report

Output:

Text Only
Risk Attribution:
Factor      | Beta  | Factor Vol | Contribution | % Total
------------+-------+------------+--------------+--------
Market      |  0.72 |    16.5%   |     8.5%     |   56%
Size        |  0.35 |     8.2%   |     2.1%     |   14%
Value       | -0.15 |     6.5%   |     0.8%     |    5%
Momentum    |  0.45 |     9.2%   |     2.2%     |   15%
Specific    |       |            |     1.5%     |   10%
------------+-------+------------+--------------+--------
Total       |       |            |    15.1%     |  100%

docs.title: Volatility Target
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio vol_targeted:
  raw_weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  // Scale to target volatility
  port_vol = estimate_volatility(raw_weights)
  scale = target_vol / port_vol
  weights = raw_weights * scale

  constraints:
    target_volatility = 0.10  # 10% annual vol

docs.title: Risk Parity
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio risk_parity:
  // Equal risk contribution from each asset
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  inv_vol = 1 / vol

  // Weight inversely to volatility
  weights = inv_vol / sum(inv_vol)

docs.title: Maximum Drawdown
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio drawdown_controlled:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  constraints:
    max_expected_drawdown = 0.15

docs.title: Real-Time Risk
docs.text:
YAML
monitoring:
  risk:
    interval_seconds: 60
    metrics:
      - portfolio_volatility
      - var_95
      - max_position_risk
      - factor_exposures

docs.title: Risk Alerts
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  rules:
    - name: high_volatility
      condition: "portfolio_vol > 0.20"
      severity: warning

    - name: var_breach
      condition: "var_95 > 0.03"
      severity: high

    - name: drawdown
      condition: "drawdown > 0.10"
      severity: critical

docs.title: Budget by Factor
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio factor_budgeted:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("return"),
    constraints:
      market_risk_budget = 0.50    # 50% from market
      factor_risk_budget = 0.30   # 30% from factors
      specific_risk_budget = 0.20 # 20% specific
  )

docs.title: Budget by Sector
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio sector_budgeted:
  weights = optimize(
    objective = maximize("sharpe"),
    constraints:
      sector_risk:
        Technology: 0.20
        Healthcare: 0.15
        Financials: 0.15
        # ...
  )

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Risk estimation
signal volatility:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  vol = rolling_std(daily_ret, 60) * sqrt(252)
  emit vol

signal correlation:
  ret_matrix = ret(prices, 1)
  corr = rolling_corr_matrix(ret_matrix, 60)
  emit corr

// Alpha signal
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

// Risk-aware portfolio
portfolio risk_managed:
  // Raw weights from signal
  raw_weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  // Estimate portfolio risk
  port_vol = portfolio_volatility(raw_weights, volatility, correlation)

  // Scale to target volatility
  target_vol = 0.10
  scale = where(port_vol > target_vol, target_vol / port_vol, 1.0)
  weights = raw_weights * scale

  constraints:
    max_position = 0.05
    max_sector = 0.25
    dollar_neutral = true

  risk_monitoring:
    enabled: true
    metrics: [portfolio_vol, var_95, factor_exposures]

  backtest rebal=21 from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

results = pysigc.run(

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
sigc is written in Rust and can be used as a library:

Rust
use sigc::{Strategy, Backtest, Results};

fn main() -> Result<(), sigc::Error> {
    // Load strategy
    let strategy = Strategy::from_file("momentum.sig")?;

    // Run backtest
    let results = strategy.run()?;

    // Access results
    println!("Sharpe: {:.2}", results.sharpe_ratio());
    println!("Return: {:.1}%", results.annual_return() * 100.0);

    Ok(())
}

docs.title: Backtesting
docs.text: Simulate strategy performance on historical data.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Backtesting in sigc:

  1. Loads historical data from your data section
  2. Computes signals at each time point
  3. Constructs portfolios based on weights
  4. Simulates trading with realistic costs
  5. Reports metrics for analysis

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Run the backtest:

Bash
sigc run strategy.sig

docs.title: Date Range
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = ...
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Rebalancing Frequency
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = ...
  // Rebalance every 21 trading days (~monthly)
  backtest rebal=21 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Common frequencies:

Setting Frequency rebal=1 Daily rebal=5 Weekly rebal=21 Monthly rebal=63 Quarterly rebal=252 Annually

docs.title: Benchmark
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=21 benchmark=SPY from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Transaction Costs
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = ...
  costs = tc.bps(5)  # 5 basis points
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

See Cost Models for details.

docs.title: Console Output
docs.text:
Text Only

===================================

Benchmark: SPY

Performance Metrics:
  Total Return:     85.2%

  Calmar Ratio:     0.71

vs Benchmark (SPY):
  Alpha:            4.2%

Risk Metrics:

  CVaR (95%):      -3.2%
  Skewness:        -0.15
  Kurtosis:         3.8

docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --report detailed

Generates HTML report with:

  • Cumulative return chart
  • Drawdown chart
  • Monthly returns heatmap
  • Position concentration over time
  • Turnover analysis
  • Factor exposures

docs.title: Export Results
docs.text:
Bash
# CSV export
sigc run strategy.sig --output results.csv

# JSON export
sigc run strategy.sig --output results.json

# Parquet export
sigc run strategy.sig --output results.parquet

docs.title: Backtest Process
docs.text:
Text Only
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    For each rebalance date:               │
│                                                           │
│  1. Load data up to current date (no look-ahead)          │
│  2. Compute all signals                                   │
│  3. Calculate target weights                              │
│  4. Apply constraints (if any)                            │
│  5. Calculate trades needed                               │
│  6. Apply transaction costs                               │
│  7. Execute trades (update positions)                     │
│  8. Mark-to-market portfolio                              │
│  9. Record metrics                                        │
│                                                           │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Point-in-Time
docs.text:
sigc only uses data available at each historical date:

Text Only
signal no_lookahead:
  // At 2020-01-15, only data up to 2020-01-15 is visible
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

docs.title: Rebalancing
docs.text:
Trades only occur on rebalance dates:

Text Only
Rebal=21 (monthly):
Day 1:  Compute weights → Trade
Day 2-20: Hold positions
Day 21: Compute weights → Trade
Day 22-41: Hold positions
...

docs.title: Transaction Costs
docs.text:
Applied when positions change:

Text Only
Old Position: +5% AAPL
New Position: +3% AAPL
Trade: Sell 2% of portfolio
Cost: 2% × cost_rate

docs.title: Multiple Portfolios
docs.text:
Test variations:

Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio monthly:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=21 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

portfolio weekly:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=5 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

portfolio capped:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)
  backtest rebal=21 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Performance Metrics
docs.text:
Key metrics computed automatically:

Metric Description Total Return Cumulative return over period CAGR Compound annual growth rate Volatility Annualized standard deviation Sharpe Ratio Risk-adjusted return Max Drawdown Largest peak-to-trough decline Calmar Ratio CAGR / Max Drawdown

See Metrics for complete list.

docs.title: 1. Use Realistic Costs
docs.text:
Text Only
// Include trading costs
costs = tc.bps(10) + slippage.model("square-root", coef=0.1)

docs.title: 2. Match Rebalancing to Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
// Fast signal (mean reversion)
backtest rebal=5 ...  // Weekly

// Slow signal (value, momentum)
backtest rebal=21 ...  // Monthly

docs.title: 3. Test Multiple Periods
docs.text:
Text Only
// Out-of-sample testing
portfolio in_sample:
  backtest from 2010-01-01 to 2019-12-31

portfolio out_of_sample:
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 4. Include Benchmark
docs.text:
Text Only
// Compare to market
backtest benchmark=SPY from ...

docs.title: 5. Check Turnover
docs.text:
High turnover erodes returns:

Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --report turnover

docs.title: Look-Ahead Bias
docs.text:
Using future information:

Text Only
// BAD: Using same-day close to trade at close
signal bad:
  emit zscore(prices)  // Can't use today's price to trade today

// GOOD: Use previous day
signal good:
  emit zscore(lag(prices, 1))

docs.title: Survivorship Bias
docs.text:
Only testing on current stocks:

Text Only
// Use point-in-time data that includes delisted stocks
data:
  source = "pit_prices.parquet"  // Point-in-time

docs.title: Overfitting
docs.text:
Testing too many variations:

Text Only
Problem: Testing 1000 parameter combinations
         Finding "best" parameters
         Parameters don't work out-of-sample

Solution: Use walk-forward validation. See Walk-Forward.

docs.title: Return Attribution
docs.text: Decompose strategy returns to understand what's driving performance.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Attribution answers: "Where did returns come from?"

Text Only
Total Return
├── Factor Returns
│   ├── Market (Beta)
│   ├── Size (SMB)
│   ├── Value (HML)
│   └── Momentum (MOM)
├── Sector Returns
│   ├── Allocation Effect
│   └── Selection Effect
└── Idiosyncratic (Alpha)

docs.title: Single-Factor Model
docs.text:
Decompose returns vs market:

Text Only
portfolio main:
  backtest benchmark=SPY from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31
Text Only
Single-Factor Attribution:
  Total Return: 14.2%
  Market Contribution (β=0.72): 7.6%
  Alpha: 6.6%

  backtest factors=[MKT, SMB, HML, MOM] from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

SMB (Size)  |  0.35   |     2.1%      |    0.7%
HML (Value) | -0.15   |    -1.5%      |    0.2%
MOM         |  0.45   |     5.2%      |    2.3%
------------+---------+---------------+------------

Alpha       |         |               |    3.4%
Total       |         |               |   14.2%

2020Q3  0.72    0.35   -0.12    0.42
...
2024Q3  0.68    0.38   -0.18    0.52

docs.title: Attribution Effects
docs.text:
Allocation Effect: Return from over/underweighting sectors

\[\text{Allocation} = (w_p - w_b) \times (R_b^{sector} - R_b^{total})\]

Selection Effect: Return from stock selection within sectors

\[\text{Selection} = w_p \times (R_p^{sector} - R_b^{sector})\]

Interaction Effect: Combined allocation and selection

\[\text{Interaction} = (w_p - w_b) \times (R_p^{sector} - R_b^{sector})\]

docs.text:
Text Only

------------+-------------

Size        |    8.5%
Value       |   -2.1%
Momentum    |   12.3%
Alpha       |   28.3%
------------+-------------

Financials  |   10%  |  1.10  |    11.0%
...

docs.title: Define Custom Factors
docs.text:
Text Only
signal my_quality_factor:
  emit zscore(roe)

signal my_growth_factor:
  emit zscore(revenue_growth)

portfolio main:
  backtest factors=[MKT, my_quality_factor, my_growth_factor] from ...

docs.title: Industry Attribution
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  backtest sectors=sic_industry from ...

docs.title: Country Attribution
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  backtest sectors=country from ...

docs.title: What Good Attribution Looks Like
docs.text:
Text Only
Diversified factor contributions:
- Multiple factors contributing positively
- No single factor dominating (>80%)
- Positive alpha indicating skill

Good: Market 30%, Size 15%, Value 10%, Mom 20%, Alpha 25%
Bad:  Market 95%, Alpha 5% (just beta, no skill)

docs.title: Red Flags
docs.text:
1. Alpha > 50% of return: May be unexplained risk
  2. Single factor dominance: Strategy is just factor exposure
  3. Unstable exposures: Betas change wildly over time
  4. Negative alpha: Strategy destroys value vs factors

docs.title: 1. Use Appropriate Factor Model
docs.text:
Text Only
// For equity long-short
factors=[MKT, SMB, HML, MOM, QMJ]

// For momentum strategy
factors=[MKT, MOM, STR]  // Include short-term reversal

docs.title: 2. Check Factor Stability
docs.text: Factor exposures should be relatively stable unless designed otherwise.

docs.title: 3. Validate Alpha
docs.text: True alpha should: - Persist out-of-sample - Not be explained by known factors - Survive transaction costs

docs.title: 4. Report Multiple Views
docs.text:
Bash
# Factor view
sigc run strategy.sig --report factor-attribution

# Sector view
sigc run strategy.sig --report sector-attribution

# Position view
sigc run strategy.sig --report position-attribution

docs.title: 5. Attribution Should Sum
docs.text:
Text Only
Factor contributions + Alpha = Total Return

Always verify attribution sums correctly.

docs.text:

docs.title: Benchmark Analysis
docs.text: Compare strategy performance against market benchmarks.

docs.title: Specifying a Benchmark
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=21 benchmark=SPY from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Common Benchmarks
docs.text: Benchmark Description Use For SPY S&P 500 ETF US large cap QQQ Nasdaq 100 ETF US tech/growth IWM Russell 2000 ETF US small cap VTI Total US Market Broad US EFA EAFE ETF International developed EEM Emerging Markets ETF Emerging markets AGG US Aggregate Bond Bonds cash Risk-free rate Absolute return

docs.title: Alpha
docs.text:
Excess return after adjusting for market exposure:

\[\alpha = R_p - (R_f + \beta (R_m - R_f))\] Text Only
Alpha: 4.2% (annualized)

Interpretation: Strategy generated 4.2% above what beta exposure would predict.

docs.title: Beta
docs.text:
Sensitivity to benchmark:

\[\beta = \frac{\text{Cov}(R_p, R_m)}{\text{Var}(R_m)}\] Text Only
Beta: 0.72

Interpretation: When benchmark moves 1%, strategy moves ~0.72%.

docs.title: Tracking Error
docs.text:
Volatility of excess returns:

\[\text{TE} = \sigma(R_p - R_b)\] Text Only
Tracking Error: 9.3% (annualized)

docs.title: Information Ratio
docs.text:
Alpha per unit of tracking error:

\[\text{IR} = \frac{\alpha}{\text{TE}}\] Text Only
Information Ratio: 0.45
IR Quality < 0.25 Poor 0.25-0.5 Average 0.5-0.75 Good > 0.75 Excellent

docs.title: Active Share
docs.text:
How different is the portfolio from benchmark:

Text Only
Active Share: 85%
  • < 20%: Closet indexer
  • 20-60%: Moderate active

  • 60%: Truly active

docs.title: Up Capture Ratio
docs.text:
Performance when benchmark is up:

\[\text{Up Capture} = \frac{\text{Avg Strategy Return (up months)}}{\text{Avg Benchmark Return (up months)}}\] Text Only
Up Capture: 85%

docs.title: Down Capture Ratio
docs.text:
Performance when benchmark is down:

\[\text{Down Capture} = \frac{\text{Avg Strategy Return (down months)}}{\text{Avg Benchmark Return (down months)}}\] Text Only
Down Capture: 62%

docs.title: Capture Ratio
docs.text:
Text Only
Capture Ratio = Up Capture / Down Capture = 85% / 62% = 1.37

1.0 means strategy captures more upside than downside.

docs.title: Rolling Excess Return
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --report rolling-alpha

Shows 12-month rolling alpha over time.

docs.title: Single-Factor Model
docs.text:
Text Only
Strategy Return = Alpha + Beta × Benchmark Return + Residual

Example:
  Strategy: +12%
  Benchmark: +10%
  Beta: 0.8

  Expected: 0.8 × 10% = 8%
  Alpha: 12% - 8% = 4%

docs.text:

  backtest benchmark=SPY factors=[SMB, HML, MOM] from ...

SMB       |    0.35  |       2.1%    |      0.7%
HML       |   -0.15  |      -1.5%    |      0.2%
MOM       |    0.45  |       5.2%    |      2.3%
----------+----------+---------------+-------------
Factor Sum|          |               |     10.8%

Total     |          |               |     14.2%
