Create .vscode/settings.json:

JSON
{
  "sigc.binaryPath": "./target/release/sigc",
  "sigc.formatOnSave": true,
  "[sig]": {
    "editor.tabSize": 2,
    "editor.insertSpaces": true
  }
}

docs.title: Snippets
docs.text: Built-in snippets for common patterns:

docs.title: data - Data Section
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "$1"
  format = ${2|csv,parquet|}
  columns:
    date: Date
    ticker: Symbol
    $3: Numeric as $4

docs.title: signal - Signal Block
docs.text:
Text Only
signal ${1:name}:
  $2
  emit $3

docs.title: portfolio - Portfolio Block
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio ${1:name}:
  weights = rank(${2:signal}).long_short(top=${3:0.2}, bottom=${4:0.2})
  backtest from ${5:2020-01-01} to ${6:2024-12-31}

docs.title: momentum - Momentum Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  mom = zscore(ret(prices, ${1:60}))
  emit mom

docs.title: longshort - Long-Short Portfolio
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio long_short:
  weights = rank(${1:signal}).long_short(top=${2:0.2}, bottom=${3:0.2}, cap=${4:0.05})
  backtest rebal=${5:21} from ${6:2020-01-01} to ${7:2024-12-31}

Type the snippet prefix and press Tab to expand.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
Access via Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P):

Command Description sigc: Run Strategy Run current file sigc: Run with Parameters Run with param overrides sigc: Validate Check for errors sigc: Format Document Format current file sigc: Show Metrics Display performance metrics sigc: Export Results Export to CSV/JSON sigc: Open Documentation Open docs in browser

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Ctrl+Shift+R Run strategy Ctrl+Shift+V Validate Ctrl+Shift+D Open diagnostics F5 Run with debugger

docs.title: Customize Shortcuts
docs.text:
Add to keybindings.json:

JSON
[
  {
    "key": "ctrl+shift+r",
    "command": "sigc.run",
    "when": "editorLangId == sig"
  }
]

docs.title: Output Panel
docs.text:
Results appear in the Output panel:

  1. Open Output panel (Ctrl+Shift+U)
  2. Select "sigc" from dropdown

Shows:

  • Backtest results
  • Performance metrics
  • Errors and warnings
  • Debug output

docs.title: Problems Panel
docs.text:
Errors and warnings appear in Problems panel:

  • Click error to jump to line
  • Hover for details
  • Quick fix suggestions

docs.title: Basic Debugging
docs.text:
1. Set breakpoints (click line number)
  2. Press F5 to start debugging
  3. Step through execution

docs.title: Debug Configuration
docs.text:
Create .vscode/launch.json:

JSON
{
  "version": "0.2.0",
  "configurations": [
    {
      "type": "sigc",
      "request": "launch",
      "name": "Debug Strategy",
      "program": "${file}",
      "args": ["--verbose"]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Watch Variables
docs.text:
Add variables to watch panel:

  • Signal values
  • Weight calculations
  • Intermediate results

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
Define tasks in .vscode/tasks.json:

JSON
{
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "tasks": [
    {
      "label": "Run Backtest",
      "type": "shell",
      "command": "sigc run ${file}",
      "group": {
        "kind": "build",
        "isDefault": true
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "Validate All",
      "type": "shell",
      "command": "sigc validate strategies/*.sig"
    }
  ]
}

Run tasks: Ctrl+Shift+B

docs.title: Multi-Root Workspaces
docs.text:
Support for multiple strategy folders:

JSON
// workspace.code-workspace
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "production" },
    { "path": "research" },
    { "path": "backtest" }
  ],
  "settings": {
    "sigc.binaryPath": "/usr/local/bin/sigc"
  }
}

docs.title: File Associations
docs.text:
Ensure .sig files are recognized:

JSON
{
  "files.associations": {
    "*.sig": "sig"
  }
}

docs.title: Recommended Extensions
docs.text:
Enhance your sigc development:

Extension Purpose Error Lens Inline error display GitLens Git history Jupyter Notebook support

docs.title: Extension Not Working
docs.text:
1. Check sigc is installed: sigc --version
  2. Verify path in settings
  3. Restart VSCode

docs.title: No Syntax Highlighting
docs.text:
1. Check file extension is .sig
  2. Reload window: Ctrl+Shift+P → "Reload Window"

docs.title: LSP Errors
docs.text:
1. Check Output panel for errors
  2. Increase log level: JSON
    { "sigc.logLevel": "debug" }
  3. Restart LSP: Command Palette → "sigc: Restart Language Server"

docs.title: Slow Diagnostics
docs.text:
1. Increase delay: JSON
    { "sigc.diagnosticDelay": 1000 }
  2. Disable for large files: JSON
    { "sigc.diagnostics.maxFileSize": 100000 }

docs.title: Auto Update
docs.text: Extensions update automatically by default.

docs.title: Manual Update
docs.text:
1. Open Extensions view
  2. Click update button on sigc extension

docs.title: Check Version
docs.text:
Bash
code --list-extensions --show-versions | grep sigc

docs.text:
Report issues or contribute:

  • GitHub Issues

docs.title: Yahoo Finance Integration
docs.text: Free historical market data from Yahoo Finance.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Yahoo Finance provides:

  • Free historical prices (OHLCV)
  • Dividends and splits
  • Basic fundamentals
  • Wide symbol coverage

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL", "AMZN", "META"]
  start = "2020-01-01"
  end = "2024-12-31"

docs.title: Symbols List
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL"]
  start = "2020-01-01"

docs.title: From File
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols_file = "universe.txt"  # One symbol per line
  start = "2020-01-01"

docs.title: Index Components
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  index = "^GSPC"  # S&P 500 components
  start = "2020-01-01"

docs.title: Available Fields
docs.text: Field Description date Trading date open Opening price high Daily high low Daily low close Closing price adj_close Adjusted close volume Trading volume

docs.title: Default Mapping
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT"]
  start = "2020-01-01"
  columns:
    adj_close: Numeric as prices  # Use adjusted close
    volume: Numeric

docs.title: OHLCV Data
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL"]
  start = "2020-01-01"
  columns:
    date: Date
    symbol: Symbol as ticker
    open: Numeric
    high: Numeric
    low: Numeric
    adj_close: Numeric as close
    volume: Numeric

docs.title: Adjusted Prices
docs.text:
Yahoo provides split and dividend adjusted prices:

Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL"]
  columns:
    adj_close: Numeric as prices  # Adjusted for splits/dividends
    close: Numeric as raw_prices  # Unadjusted

Always use adj_close for backtesting.

docs.title: Dividends
docs.text:
Text Only
data dividends:
  source = "yahoo"
  type = "dividends"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT"]
  start = "2020-01-01"

docs.title: Splits
docs.text:
Text Only
data splits:
  source = "yahoo"
  type = "splits"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT"]
  start = "2020-01-01"

docs.title: Enable Caching
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  yahoo:
    cache:
      enabled: true
      directory: /var/cache/sigc/yahoo
      ttl_hours: 24

docs.title: Clear Cache
docs.text:
Bash
sigc cache clear --source yahoo

docs.title: Default Limits
docs.text:
Yahoo has rate limits:

  • ~2000 requests per hour
  • ~100 requests per minute

docs.title: Configure Rate Limiting
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  yahoo:
    rate_limit:
      requests_per_minute: 30
      retry_after_seconds: 60

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text: Error Cause Solution Invalid symbol Symbol not found Check ticker spelling No data Symbol delisted or no history Use alternative symbol Rate limit Too many requests Enable caching, reduce frequency

docs.title: Fallback Data
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT"]
  options:
    on_error = "skip"  # skip | fail
    fallback = "cache"

docs.title: Batch Downloading
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  yahoo:
    batch_size: 50       # Download in batches
    parallel: true        # Parallel requests
    max_workers: 4

docs.title: Progress Tracking
docs.text:
Bash
sigc data fetch --source yahoo --symbols sp500.txt --progress

docs.title: Basic Momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL", "AMZN", "META",
             "NVDA", "TSLA", "JPM", "JNJ", "V"]
  start = "2020-01-01"
  columns:
    adj_close: Numeric as prices
    volume: Numeric

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.3, bottom=0.3)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: With Volume Filter
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL"]
  start = "2020-01-01"
  columns:
    adj_close: Numeric as prices
    volume: Numeric

signal momentum:
  // Filter low volume stocks
  avg_vol = rolling_mean(volume, 20)
  liquid = avg_vol > 1000000

  mom = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  filtered = where(liquid, mom, 0)

  emit filtered

docs.title: Using Technical Indicators
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "yahoo"
  symbols = ["AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL"]
  start = "2020-01-01"
  columns:
    adj_close: Numeric as prices
    high: Numeric
    low: Numeric

signal mean_reversion:
  // RSI signal
  rsi_val = rsi(prices, 14)
  rsi_sig = -zscore((rsi_val - 50) / 25)

  // ATR for position sizing
  atr_val = atr(high, low, prices, 14)
  size_adj = 1 / atr_val

  emit rsi_sig * zscore(size_adj)

docs.title: US Stocks
docs.text:
• Use ticker symbol: AAPL, MSFT
  • Include exchange suffix for clarity: AAPL.US

docs.title: International
docs.text:
• UK: VOD.L (London)
  • Germany: BMW.DE (Frankfurt)
  • Japan: 7203.T (Tokyo)

docs.title: ETFs
docs.text:
• SPY, QQQ, IWM, VTI
  • International: EFA, EEM, VEA

docs.title: Indices
docs.text:
• S&P 500: ^GSPC
  • Dow Jones: ^DJI
  • Nasdaq: ^IXIC
  • VIX: ^VIX

docs.title: Data Quality
docs.text:
• May have occasional errors
  • Historical data can change
  • Corporate actions may be delayed

docs.title: Coverage
docs.text:
• US stocks: Excellent
  • International: Good
  • OTC/Pink sheets: Limited

docs.title: Real-time
docs.text:
• 15-20 minute delay for quotes
  • Not suitable for live trading data

docs.title: 1. Use Adjusted Close
docs.text:
Text Only
columns:
  adj_close: Numeric as prices  # Always

docs.title: 2. Enable Caching
docs.text:
YAML
cache:
  enabled: true
  ttl_hours: 24

docs.title: 3. Verify Data Quality
docs.text:
Text Only
signal quality_check:
  // Check for suspicious data
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  suspicious = abs(daily_ret) > 0.5

  emit where(not(suspicious), prices, 0)

docs.title: 4. Handle Missing Symbols
docs.text:
YAML
options:
  on_error = "skip"

docs.title: 5. Use for Research, Not Production
docs.text: For production, use a professional data provider.

docs.title: Python Integration
docs.text:
Python
import pysigc
import yfinance as yf

# Fetch data with yfinance
tickers = ["AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL"]
data = yf.download(tickers, start="2020-01-01")

# Convert to sigc format
prices = data["Adj Close"].reset_index().melt(
    id_vars=["Date"],
    var_name="ticker",
    value_name="prices"
)

# Run strategy with custom data
results = pysigc.run("strategy.sig", data={"prices": prices})

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• CSV Format - Using local CSV files
  • Parquet Format - Faster data loading
  • Streaming - Real-time data

docs.title: Language Reference
docs.text: The sigc DSL (Domain-Specific Language) is designed for expressing quantitative trading strategies concisely and safely.

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
The sigc language prioritizes:

  • Readability: Code should be self-documenting
  • Safety: Catch errors at compile time
  • Composability: Build complex strategies from simple parts
  • Performance: Compile to efficient execution plans

docs.title: Program Structure
docs.text:
A sigc program has up to six sections:

Text Only
// 1. Data declarations
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"

// 2. Parameter definitions
params:
  lookback = 20

// 3. Custom functions (optional)
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

// 4. Macro definitions (optional)
macro momentum(px: expr, lookback: number = 20):
  let r = ret(px, lookback)
  emit zscore(r)

// 5. Signal computations
signal my_signal:
  returns = ret(prices, lookback)
  emit zscore(returns)

// 6. Portfolio construction
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(my_signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Section Reference
docs.text: Section Required Purpose Link data: Yes Load data sources Data Section params: No Define parameters Params Section fn No Custom functions Functions macro No Reusable patterns Macros signal Yes Compute scores Signal Section portfolio Yes Build portfolios Portfolio Section

docs.title: Data Loading
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  name: load format from "path"

Formats: csv, parquet, arrow

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  name = default_value

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
Text Only
fn name(param1, param2=default):
  expression

docs.title: Macros
docs.text:
Text Only
macro name(param: type, ...):
  let var = expression
  emit result

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal name:
  var = expression
  emit output

docs.title: Portfolios
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio name:
  weights = construction_expression
  backtest from start to end

docs.title: Expressions
docs.text:
Expressions compute values from data and operators:

Text Only
// Arithmetic
x + y, x - y, x * y, x / y

// Function calls
zscore(x), ret(prices, 20)

// Chaining
rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

// Conditional
where(condition, true_value, false_value)

See Expressions for full reference.

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
Text Only
// Single-line comment
# Also single-line

signal example:
  x = ret(prices, 20)  // Inline comment
  emit x

See Comments for conventions.

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
Reserved words in sigc:

Keyword Purpose data Data section params Parameters section fn Function definition macro Macro definition signal Signal definition portfolio Portfolio definition emit Output statement let Variable in macro load Data loading from Source path backtest Run simulation rebal Rebalancing frequency benchmark Comparison benchmark

docs.title: Identifiers
docs.text:
Valid identifier names:

  • Start with letter or underscore
  • Contain letters, digits, underscores
  • Case-sensitive
Text Only
// Valid
my_signal
Signal2
_private

// Invalid
2signal   // starts with digit
my-signal // contains hyphen

docs.title: Numbers
docs.text:
Text Only
42        // Integer
3.14      // Float
-0.5      // Negative
1e-3      // Scientific

docs.title: Strings
docs.text:
Text Only
"path/to/file.csv"
"s3://bucket/data.parquet"

docs.title: Dates
docs.text:
Text Only
backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Format: YYYY-MM-DD

docs.title: Type System
docs.text:
sigc infers types and checks operations:

Text Only
signal typed:
  prices     // Panel<Float64>
  returns = ret(prices, 20)  // Panel<Float64>
  z = zscore(returns)        // Panel<Float64>
  emit z

See Type System for details.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The compiler provides helpful error messages:

Text Only
Error: Unknown identifier 'prces'
  --> strategy.sig:3:14
    |
  3 |   x = zscore(prces)
    |              ^^^^^
    |
help: Did you mean 'prices'?

See Error Messages for common errors.

docs.title: Section Index
docs.text: Page Description Syntax Complete syntax reference Data Section Loading data sources Params Section Defining parameters Signal Section Computing signals Portfolio Section Building portfolios Functions Custom functions Macros Reusable patterns Expressions Expression syntax Comments Documentation

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Start with Syntax Overview for a comprehensive reference, or jump to specific sections as needed.

docs.title: Comments
docs.text: Comments document your code and are ignored by the compiler.

docs.title: C-Style Comments
docs.text:
Text Only
// This is a single-line comment
signal example:
  x = ret(prices, 20)  // Inline comment
  emit x

docs.title: Shell-Style Comments
docs.text:
Text Only
# This is also a single-line comment
signal example:
  x = ret(prices, 20)  # Inline comment
  emit x

Both styles are equivalent. Choose one and be consistent.

docs.title: File Header
Block Comments
Inline Comments
docs.text:
Text Only
signal documented:
  returns = ret(prices, 60)           // 60-day returns (~3 months)
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 252)  // 252-day vol (~1 year)
  vol_adj = returns / vol             // Scale by volatility
  normalized = zscore(vol_adj)        // Cross-sectional normalization
  emit winsor(normalized, p=0.01)     // Remove outliers

docs.title: Signal Documentation
Parameter Documentation
Function/Macro Documentation
1. Explain "Why", Not "What"
docs.text:
Text Only
// BAD: Explains what (obvious from code)
returns = ret(prices, 20)  // Compute 20-day returns

// GOOD: Explains why
returns = ret(prices, 20)  // 20 days ~ 1 month, standard momentum horizon

docs.title: 2. Keep Comments Current
docs.text: Update comments when code changes. Stale comments are worse than no comments.

docs.title: 3. Use Consistent Style
docs.text:
Pick one comment style and use it throughout:

Text Only
// Preferred: C-style throughout
signal consistent:
  // Step 1
  x = ...
  // Step 2
  y = ...

// Avoid: Mixed styles
signal inconsistent:
  // Step 1
  x = ...
  # Step 2
  y = ...

docs.title: 4. Document Non-Obvious Choices
5. Group Related Code
docs.text:
Text Only
signal well_organized:
  // ----- Momentum Computation -----
  total_return = ret(prices, 252)
  recent_return = ret(prices, 21)
  raw_momentum = total_return - recent_return

  // ----- Volatility Adjustment -----
  daily_returns = ret(prices, 1)
  volatility = rolling_std(daily_returns, 60)
  vol_adj_momentum = raw_momentum / volatility

  // ----- Normalization & Cleaning -----
  normalized = zscore(vol_adj_momentum)
  cleaned = winsor(normalized, p=0.01)

  emit cleaned

docs.title: Example: Well-Documented Strategy
Next Steps
docs.text:
• Syntax Overview - Complete syntax reference
  • Signal Section - Writing signals
  • Best Practices - Strategy development

docs.title: Data Section
docs.text: The data: section declares data sources for your strategy.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  <name>: load <format> from <path> [options]

docs.title: CSV
docs.text:
Comma-separated values:

Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"

Expected format:

Text Only
date,AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL
2024-01-02,185.64,374.58,140.25
2024-01-03,184.25,373.31,139.12

docs.title: Parquet
docs.text:
Apache Parquet (recommended for large datasets):

Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "data/prices.parquet"

Benefits:

  • Columnar storage (efficient for analytics)
  • Compression (smaller file sizes)
  • Type preservation

docs.title: Arrow
docs.text:
Apache Arrow IPC format:

Text Only
data:
  prices: load arrow from "data/prices.arrow"

Best for:

  • Inter-process communication
  • Zero-copy reads

docs.title: Local Files
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"
  volume: load parquet from "/absolute/path/volume.parquet"
  factors: load csv from "../relative/path/factors.csv"

docs.title: S3
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "s3://my-bucket/data/prices.parquet"
  fundamentals: load parquet from "s3://my-bucket/fundamentals.parquet"

Set credentials via environment variables:

Bash
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your_key
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your_secret
export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1

docs.title: Azure Blob Storage
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "az://container/prices.parquet"

docs.title: Google Cloud Storage
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "gs://bucket/prices.parquet"

docs.title: Price Adjustment
docs.text:
Adjust for corporate actions:

Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv" adjust=split_div

Options:

  • adjust=split - Adjust for stock splits only
  • adjust=split_div - Adjust for splits and dividends
  • adjust=none - No adjustment (default)

docs.title: Data Type
docs.text:
Specify column data type:

Text Only
data:
  sectors: load csv from "data/sectors.csv" dtype=category

Types:

  • dtype=float64 - Double precision (default for numeric)
  • dtype=float32 - Single precision
  • dtype=int64 - 64-bit integer
  • dtype=category - Categorical data

docs.title: Date Column
docs.text:
Specify which column contains dates:

Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv" date_col="timestamp"

Default: First column is assumed to be the date.

docs.title: Multiple Data Sources
docs.text:
Load multiple datasets:

Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"
  volume: load csv from "data/volume.csv"
  market_cap: load parquet from "data/fundamentals.parquet"
  sectors: load csv from "data/sectors.csv" dtype=category

Use them in signals:

Text Only
signal composite:
  mom = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  vol_signal = zscore(volume)
  size = -zscore(log(market_cap))
  neutral = neutralize(mom, by=sectors)
  emit neutral

docs.title: Panel Structure
docs.text:
Data should be in panel format (dates as rows, assets as columns):

Text Only
date,AAPL,MSFT,GOOGL,AMZN
2024-01-02,185.64,374.58,140.25,151.94
2024-01-03,184.25,373.31,139.12,149.93
2024-01-04,181.91,367.94,137.98,147.44

docs.title: Date Formats
docs.text:
Supported date formats:

  • YYYY-MM-DD (preferred): 2024-01-15
  • YYYY/MM/DD: 2024/01/15
  • MM/DD/YYYY: 01/15/2024
  • ISO 8601: 2024-01-15T00:00:00

docs.title: Missing Values
docs.text:
Missing values can be:

  • Empty cells
  • NaN
  • NA
  • null

Handle in signals:

Text Only
signal cleaned:
  filled = fill_nan(prices, 0)
  emit zscore(filled)

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"

signal example:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 20))

docs.title: Multiple Sources
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"
  book_value: load csv from "data/book_value.csv"
  earnings: load parquet from "data/earnings.parquet"

signal value:
  pb = prices / book_value
  pe = prices / earnings
  emit -zscore(pb + pe)  // Lower is better for value

docs.title: S3 with Options
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "s3://quant-data/prices.parquet" adjust=split_div
  sectors: load parquet from "s3://quant-data/sectors.parquet" dtype=category

signal sector_neutral_mom:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  neutral = neutralize(raw, by=sectors)
  emit neutral

docs.title: Fundamental Data
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"
  roe: load csv from "data/roe.csv"
  debt_equity: load csv from "data/debt_equity.csv"
  revenue_growth: load csv from "data/revenue_growth.csv"

signal quality:
  roe_z = zscore(roe)
  leverage_z = -zscore(debt_equity)  // Lower debt is better
  growth_z = zscore(revenue_growth)
  emit (roe_z + leverage_z + growth_z) / 3

docs.title: File Not Found
docs.text:
Text Only
Error: Failed to load data from "data/prices.csv"
  File not found

Check:

  • File path is correct
  • Working directory is correct
  • File has read permissions

docs.title: Parse Error
docs.text:
Text Only
Error: Failed to parse CSV
  Invalid number at row 5, column 3

Check:

  • Data format is correct
  • No text in numeric columns
  • Consistent delimiters

docs.title: Date Parse Error
docs.text:
Text Only
Error: Failed to parse date "01-15-2024"

Use supported format: 2024-01-15

docs.title: S3 Access Denied
docs.text:
Text Only
Error: Access denied to s3://bucket/file.parquet

Check:

  • AWS credentials are set
  • IAM permissions allow read access
  • Bucket policy allows access

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Params Section - Define parameters
  • Data Loading Guide - Advanced data loading
  • Corporate Actions - Handling splits/dividends

docs.title: Expressions
docs.text: Expressions compute values from data, operators, and functions.

docs.title: Numbers
docs.text:
Text Only
42          // Integer
-5          // Negative integer
3.14        // Float
-0.5        // Negative float
1e-3        // Scientific: 0.001
2.5e10      // Scientific: 25000000000

docs.title: Strings
docs.text:
Text Only
"data/prices.csv"
"s3://bucket/file.parquet"

docs.title: Identifiers
docs.text:
Reference data, parameters, variables, and signals:

Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"

params:
  lookback = 20

signal example:
  returns = ret(prices, lookback)  // prices and lookback are identifiers
  emit returns                      // returns is an identifier

docs.title: Arithmetic Operators
docs.text: Operator Description Example + Addition x + y - Subtraction x - y * Multiplication x * y / Division x / y - (unary) Negation -x

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Text Only
signal arithmetic:
  a = prices + 1
  b = prices - lag(prices, 1)
  c = returns * 252
  d = returns / volatility
  e = -returns  // Negate
  emit a

docs.title: Mixed Operations
docs.text:
Text Only
signal mixed:
  // Parentheses control order
  a = (prices + 1) * 2
  b = prices + 1 * 2      // Same as: prices + 2
  c = (a - b) / (a + b)
  emit c

docs.title: Comparison Operators
docs.text: Operator Description Example > Greater than x > y < Less than x < y >= Greater or equal x >= y <= Less or equal x <= y == Equal x == y != Not equal x != y

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Text Only
signal comparisons:
  above_ma = prices > rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  positive_return = returns > 0
  not_excluded = status != excluded
  in_range = score >= -2 and score <= 2
  emit above_ma

docs.title: Logical Operators
docs.text: Operator Description Example and Logical AND a and b or Logical OR a or b not Logical NOT not(a)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Text Only
signal logical:
  condition1 = returns > 0
  condition2 = volatility < 0.3
  combined = condition1 and condition2
  either = condition1 or condition2
  inverted = not(condition1)
  emit combined

docs.title: Short-Circuit Evaluation
docs.text:
and and or use short-circuit evaluation:

Text Only
// If condition1 is false, condition2 is not evaluated
result = condition1 and condition2

// If condition1 is true, condition2 is not evaluated
result = condition1 or condition2

docs.title: Conditional Expression
docs.text:
The where function provides conditional logic:

Text Only
where(condition, true_value, false_value)

docs.title: Examples
Nested where
docs.text:
Text Only
signal nested:
  // Multiple conditions
  category = where(score > 1, 2,
             where(score > 0, 1,
             where(score > -1, 0, -1)))
  emit category

docs.title: Function Calls
docs.text:
Call built-in operators and custom functions:

Text Only
// Single argument
normalized = zscore(returns)
absolute = abs(returns)

// Multiple arguments
returns = ret(prices, 20)
mean = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
correlation = rolling_corr(x, y, 60)

// Named arguments (where supported)
cleaned = winsor(score, p=0.01)
weights = long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: Chaining
docs.text:
Some functions support method-style chaining:

Text Only
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: Operator Precedence
docs.text:
From highest to lowest precedence:

  1. Function calls: f(x), x.method()
  2. Unary: -x, not(x)
  3. Multiplicative: *, /
  4. Additive: +, -
  5. Comparison: >, <, >=, <=, ==, !=
  6. Logical AND: and
  7. Logical OR: or

docs.title: Examples
Expression Types
docs.text:
Expressions have inferred types:

Expression Type 42 Int64 3.14 Float64 "string" String prices Panel prices > 100 Panel zscore(x) Panel

docs.title: Type Coercion
docs.text:
Numeric types are automatically coerced:

Text Only
signal coercion:
  x = 5        // Int64
  y = 3.14     // Float64
  z = x + y    // Float64 (coerced)
  emit z

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
Text Only
// Simple return
ret(prices, 20)

// Log return
log(prices / lag(prices, 20))

// Skip recent
ret(prices, 60) - ret(prices, 5)

docs.title: Normalization
docs.text:
Text Only
// Z-score
zscore(x)

// Rank
rank(x)

// Scale to sum to 1
scale(abs(x))

docs.title: Volatility Adjustment
docs.text:
Text Only
// Divide by volatility
x / rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)

// Target volatility
x * (target_vol / current_vol)

docs.title: Cleaning
docs.text:
Text Only
// Winsorize
winsor(x, p=0.01)

// Fill missing
fill_nan(x, 0)

// Coalesce
coalesce(a, b)

docs.title: Technical
docs.text:
Text Only
// Moving average crossover
ema(prices, 10) - ema(prices, 50)

// RSI centered
rsi(prices, 14) - 50

// Bollinger position
(prices - rolling_mean(prices, 20)) / rolling_std(prices, 20)

docs.title: Multi-Step Computation
docs.text:
Text Only
signal complex:
  // Step by step for clarity
  raw_returns = ret(prices, 60)
  skip_returns = ret(prices, 5)
  momentum = raw_returns - skip_returns
  volatility = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 252)
  vol_adjusted = momentum / volatility
  normalized = zscore(vol_adjusted)
  cleaned = winsor(normalized, p=0.01)
  emit cleaned

docs.title: Inline (Compact)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal compact:
  emit winsor(zscore((ret(prices, 60) - ret(prices, 5)) / rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 252)), p=0.01)

docs.title: Recommended: Balance
docs.text:
Text Only
signal balanced:
  // Group related computations
  momentum = ret(prices, 60) - ret(prices, 5)
  vol_adj = momentum / rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 252)
  emit winsor(zscore(vol_adj), p=0.01)

docs.title: 1. Use Parentheses for Clarity
docs.text:
Text Only
// Clear
result = (a + b) * (c - d)

// Ambiguous
result = a + b * c - d

docs.title: 2. Break Down Complex Expressions
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good: Clear steps
signal clear:
  momentum = ret(prices, 60)
  volatility = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  vol_adj = momentum / volatility
  emit zscore(vol_adj)

// Avoid: One giant expression
signal unclear:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60) / rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60))

docs.title: 3. Name Meaningful Intermediates
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good: Descriptive names
risk_adjusted_return = returns / volatility
sector_neutral_score = neutralize(score, by=sector)

// Avoid: Generic names
x = returns / volatility
y = neutralize(score, by=sector)

docs.title: 4. Handle Edge Cases
Next Steps
docs.text:
• Operators - Available operators
  • Functions - Custom functions
  • Signal Section - Using expressions in signals

docs.title: Custom Functions
docs.text: Custom functions let you define reusable single-expression computations.

docs.title: Syntax
Basic Usage
docs.text:
Text Only
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

signal example:
  vol = volatility(prices)        // Uses default window=20
  vol60 = volatility(prices, 60)  // Override window
  emit vol

docs.title: Required Parameters
docs.text:
Text Only
fn returns(x, lookback):
  ret(x, lookback)

signal example:
  r = returns(prices, 20)  // Must provide both
  emit r

docs.title: Default Parameters
docs.text:
Text Only
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

signal example:
  vol1 = volatility(prices)       // window=20
  vol2 = volatility(prices, 60)   // window=60
  emit vol1

docs.title: Multiple Defaults
docs.text:
Text Only
fn momentum_score(x, lookback=20, skip=5):
  ret(x, lookback) - ret(x, skip)

signal example:
  m1 = momentum_score(prices)           // lookback=20, skip=5
  m2 = momentum_score(prices, 60)       // lookback=60, skip=5
  m3 = momentum_score(prices, 60, 10)   // lookback=60, skip=10
  emit m1

docs.title: Function Body
docs.text:
The body is a single expression (no variables):

Text Only
// CORRECT: Single expression
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

// ERROR: Multiple statements not allowed in fn
fn bad(x, window=20):
  r = ret(x, 1)           // Not allowed
  rolling_std(r, window)

For multiple statements, use macros instead.

docs.title: Positional Arguments
docs.text:
Text Only
fn example(a, b, c=10):
  a + b * c

signal test:
  x = example(1, 2)      // a=1, b=2, c=10
  y = example(1, 2, 3)   // a=1, b=2, c=3
  emit x

docs.title: Named Arguments
docs.text: Not yet supported. Use positional arguments.

docs.title: Volatility
docs.text:
Text Only
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

fn annualized_vol(x, window=252):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window) * sqrt(252)

docs.title: Sharpe Ratio
docs.text:
Text Only
fn sharpe(returns, window=252):
  rolling_mean(returns, window) / rolling_std(returns, window)

fn annualized_sharpe(returns, window=252):
  sharpe(returns, window) * sqrt(252)

docs.title: Momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
fn momentum(x, lookback=20):
  ret(x, lookback)

fn momentum_skip(x, lookback=252, skip=21):
  ret(x, lookback) - ret(x, skip)

docs.title: Moving Averages
docs.text:
Text Only
fn sma(x, window=20):
  rolling_mean(x, window)

fn ema_crossover(x, fast=10, slow=50):
  ema(x, fast) - ema(x, slow)

docs.title: Normalization
docs.text:
Text Only
fn normalize(x):
  zscore(x)

fn normalize_winsor(x, p=0.01):
  winsor(zscore(x), p)

docs.title: Technical
docs.text:
Text Only
fn rsi_signal(x, period=14):
  rsi(x, period) - 50

fn macd_signal(x, fast=12, slow=26, signal=9):
  macd(x, fast, slow, signal)

fn bollinger_position(x, window=20, num_std=2):
  ma = rolling_mean(x, window)
  std = rolling_std(x, window)
  (x - ma) / (num_std * std)

docs.title: Composing Functions
docs.text:
Functions can call other functions:

Text Only
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

fn vol_adjusted_returns(x, ret_window=20, vol_window=60):
  ret(x, ret_window) / volatility(x, vol_window)

fn normalized_vol_adj(x, ret_window=20, vol_window=60):
  zscore(vol_adjusted_returns(x, ret_window, vol_window))

signal example:
  emit normalized_vol_adj(prices)

docs.title: Using Functions in Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
fn vol(x, window=60):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

fn momentum(x, lookback=20):
  ret(x, lookback)

signal vol_adjusted_momentum:
  m = momentum(prices, 60)
  v = vol(prices, 252)
  emit zscore(m / v)

docs.title: Using Functions with Parameters
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback = 20
  vol_window = 60

fn momentum(x, lb):
  ret(x, lb)

fn vol(x, window):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

signal example:
  m = momentum(prices, lookback)    // Uses param
  v = vol(prices, vol_window)       // Uses param
  emit zscore(m / v)

docs.title: Functions vs Macros
docs.text:
Feature Functions (fn) Macros (macro) Body Single expression Multiple statements Variables Not allowed let statements Output Return value emit statement Type params No Yes (expr, number) Best for Simple computations Complex patterns

Use functions for:

  • Single-expression calculations
  • Commonly used operators
  • Simple transformations

Use macros for:

  • Multi-step computations
  • Reusable signal patterns
  • Complex logic with intermediates

docs.title: Example: Building a Signal Library
1. Use Descriptive Names
2. Provide Sensible Defaults
docs.text:
Text Only
// Common window sizes as defaults
fn volatility(x, window=20):   // 20-day is standard
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

fn momentum(x, lookback=60):   // ~3 months
  ret(x, lookback)

docs.title: 3. Keep Functions Focused
4. Document Complex Functions
docs.text:
Text Only
// Sharpe ratio: mean return / volatility, annualized
fn sharpe_annualized(returns, window=252):
  rolling_mean(returns, window) / rolling_std(returns, window) * sqrt(252)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Macros - Multi-statement reusable patterns
  • Operators - Available operators
  • Signal Section - Using functions in signals

docs.title: Macros
docs.text: Macros define reusable multi-statement patterns with typed parameters.

docs.title: Syntax
Basic Usage
docs.text:
Text Only
macro momentum_signal(px: expr, lookback: number = 20):
  let r = ret(px, lookback)
  let normalized = zscore(r)
  emit normalized

signal my_momentum:
  emit momentum_signal(prices, 60)

docs.title: Parameter Types
docs.text: Type Description Example expr Any expression px: expr number Numeric value lookback: number string String literal name: string ident Identifier col: ident

docs.title: expr Type
docs.text:
Accepts any expression:

Text Only
macro normalize(x: expr):
  emit zscore(x)

signal example:
  emit normalize(ret(prices, 20))  // Expression as argument

docs.title: number Type
docs.text:
Accepts numeric literals or parameters:

Text Only
macro rolling(x: expr, window: number):
  emit rolling_mean(x, window)

signal example:
  emit rolling(prices, 20)

docs.title: Default Values
docs.text:
Text Only
macro momentum(px: expr, lookback: number = 20, skip: number = 5):
  let total = ret(px, lookback)
  let recent = ret(px, skip)
  emit zscore(total - recent)

signal example:
  a = momentum(prices)            // lookback=20, skip=5
  b = momentum(prices, 60)        // lookback=60, skip=5
  c = momentum(prices, 60, 10)    // lookback=60, skip=10
  emit a

docs.title: let Statements
docs.text:
Define intermediate variables:

Text Only
macro vol_adjusted_momentum(px: expr, ret_window: number = 20, vol_window: number = 60):
  let returns = ret(px, ret_window)
  let vol = rolling_std(ret(px, 1), vol_window)
  let vol_adj = returns / vol
  let normalized = zscore(vol_adj)
  emit winsor(normalized, p=0.01)

docs.title: emit Statement
docs.text:
The final output (required):

Text Only
macro example(x: expr):
  let processed = zscore(x)
  emit processed  // This is the output

docs.title: Built-in Macros
docs.text: sigc includes 8 built-in macros:

docs.title: momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
// 12-1 month momentum
macro momentum(prices: expr, lookback: number = 20):
  let r = ret(prices, lookback)
  emit zscore(r)

docs.title: mean_reversion
docs.text:
Text Only
// Mean reversion from moving average
macro mean_reversion(prices: expr, window: number = 20):
  let ma = rolling_mean(prices, window)
  let std = rolling_std(prices, window)
  let z = (prices - ma) / std
  emit -zscore(z)

docs.title: vol_adj_momentum
trend
rsi_signal
docs.text:
Text Only
// RSI-based contrarian signal
macro rsi_signal(prices: expr, period: number = 14):
  let rsi_val = rsi(prices, period)
  let centered = rsi_val - 50
  emit -zscore(centered)

docs.title: breakout
docs.text:
Text Only
// Channel breakout
macro breakout(prices: expr, window: number = 20):
  let high = rolling_max(prices, window)
  let low = rolling_min(prices, window)
  let mid = (high + low) / 2
  emit zscore((prices - mid) / (high - low))

docs.title: cs_momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
// Cross-sectional momentum
macro cs_momentum(prices: expr, lookback: number = 252):
  let r = ret(prices, lookback)
  emit rank(r)

docs.title: quality
docs.text:
Text Only
// Quality/low-volatility factor
macro quality(prices: expr, window: number = 60):
  let vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), window)
  emit -zscore(vol)

docs.title: Invoking Macros
docs.text:
Text Only
signal using_builtin:
  emit momentum(prices, 60)

signal using_multiple:
  mom = momentum(prices, 60)
  rev = mean_reversion(prices, 20)
  emit 0.7 * mom + 0.3 * rev

docs.title: Simple Example
docs.text:
Text Only
macro normalize_and_clean(x: expr, winsor_pct: number = 0.01):
  let normalized = zscore(x)
  let cleaned = winsor(normalized, p=winsor_pct)
  emit cleaned

signal example:
  raw = ret(prices, 20)
  emit normalize_and_clean(raw)

docs.title: Complex Example
Macro Libraries
docs.text:
Build a library of reusable macros:

Text Only
// ============================================
// Macro Library: Factor Construction
// ============================================

// --- Momentum Variants ---
macro time_series_momentum(px: expr, lookback: number = 252, skip: number = 21):
  let total = ret(px, lookback)
  let recent = ret(px, skip)
  emit zscore(total - recent)

macro cross_sectional_momentum(px: expr, lookback: number = 60):
  let r = ret(px, lookback)
  emit rank(r)

macro vol_adjusted_momentum(px: expr, ret_window: number = 60, vol_window: number = 252):
  let r = ret(px, ret_window)
  let vol = rolling_std(ret(px, 1), vol_window)
  emit zscore(r / vol)

// --- Mean Reversion Variants ---
macro bollinger_reversion(px: expr, window: number = 20, num_std: number = 2):
  let ma = rolling_mean(px, window)
  let std = rolling_std(px, window)
  let z = (px - ma) / (num_std * std)
  emit -zscore(z)

macro short_term_reversal(px: expr, lookback: number = 5):
  let r = ret(px, lookback)
  emit -zscore(r)

// --- Technical Patterns ---
macro trend_following(px: expr, fast: number = 10, slow: number = 50):
  let fast_ma = ema(px, fast)
  let slow_ma = ema(px, slow)
  let trend = fast_ma - slow_ma
  emit zscore(trend)

macro rsi_contrarian(px: expr, period: number = 14):
  let rsi_val = rsi(px, period)
  let centered = rsi_val - 50
  emit -zscore(centered / 25)

// --- Utility Macros ---
macro clean_signal(x: expr, winsor_pct: number = 0.01):
  let normalized = zscore(x)
  emit winsor(normalized, p=winsor_pct)

macro vol_scale(x: expr, target_vol: number = 0.15, vol_window: number = 252):
  let current_vol = rolling_std(x, vol_window) * sqrt(252)
  emit x * (target_vol / current_vol)

docs.title: Macros vs Functions
docs.text: Feature Functions (fn) Macros (macro) Body Single expression Multiple let + emit Variables Not allowed let statements Parameters Untyped Typed (expr, number) Output Return value emit statement Best for Simple ops Complex patterns

docs.title: When to Use Functions
When to Use Macros
1. Use Descriptive Names
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good
macro sector_neutral_vol_adjusted_momentum(...)

// Avoid
macro snvam(...)

docs.title: 2. Provide Sensible Defaults
docs.text:
Text Only
macro momentum(px: expr, lookback: number = 60, skip: number = 5):
  // 60 days is ~3 months, 5 days is ~1 week
  ...

docs.title: 3. Document Complex Macros
4. Keep Macros Focused
5. Compose Smaller Macros
docs.text:
Text Only
macro vol_adjustment(x: expr, window: number = 60):
  let vol = rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)
  emit x / vol

macro clean_signal(x: expr):
  emit winsor(zscore(x), p=0.01)

// Compose in signal
signal composed:
  raw = ret(prices, 60)
  vol_adj = vol_adjustment(raw, 252)
  emit clean_signal(vol_adj)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Functions - Simple reusable expressions
  • Signal Section - Using macros in signals
  • Strategy Library - Complete strategy examples

docs.title: Params Section
docs.text: The params: section defines tunable parameters for your strategy.

docs.title: Syntax
Basic Usage
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback = 20
  threshold = 0.5
  top_pct = 0.2

signal example:
  returns = ret(prices, lookback)  // Uses lookback parameter
  score = zscore(returns)
  filtered = where(score > threshold, score, 0)
  emit filtered

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(example).long_short(top=top_pct, bottom=top_pct)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Parameter Types
docs.text:
Parameters can be integers or floats:

Text Only
params:
  // Integers
  lookback = 20
  skip_days = 5
  rebalance_freq = 21

  // Floats
  threshold = 0.5
  decay_rate = 0.94
  winsor_pct = 0.01
  commission_bps = 5.0

docs.title: In Expressions
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback = 20

signal example:
  returns = ret(prices, lookback)
  emit zscore(returns)

docs.title: In Function Calls
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  vol_window = 60
  winsor_pct = 0.01

signal example:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), vol_window)
  score = zscore(ret(prices, 20) / vol)
  emit winsor(score, p=winsor_pct)

docs.title: In Portfolio Construction
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  top_pct = 0.2
  bottom_pct = 0.2
  max_weight = 0.05

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(
    top=top_pct,
    bottom=bottom_pct,
    cap=max_weight
  )
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: In Functions and Macros
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  default_vol_window = 60

fn volatility(x, window=default_vol_window):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

Note

Parameters used as function defaults must be defined before the function.

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text:
Use descriptive, snake_case names:

Text Only
// Good
params:
  momentum_lookback = 60
  vol_estimation_window = 252
  winsorization_percentile = 0.01
  position_cap = 0.05

// Avoid
params:
  n = 60      // Too short
  window1 = 252  // Not descriptive
  p = 0.01    // Unclear

docs.title: Grouped Parameters
docs.text:
Organize related parameters together:

Text Only
params:
  // Momentum parameters
  mom_lookback = 60
  mom_skip = 5

  // Volatility parameters
  vol_window = 252
  vol_target = 0.15

  // Portfolio parameters
  top_pct = 0.2
  bottom_pct = 0.2
  max_weight = 0.05

  // Cost parameters
  commission_bps = 5.0
  impact_coef = 0.1

docs.title: Parameter Optimization
docs.text:
Parameters can be optimized using grid search in Python:

Python
import pysigc

template = """
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"

params:
  lookback = {lookback}

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, lookback))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31
"""

# Grid search
results = []
for lookback in [10, 20, 30, 40, 60]:
    source = template.format(lookback=lookback)
    result = pysigc.backtest(source)
    results.append({
        'lookback': lookback,
        'sharpe': result.sharpe_ratio,
        'return': result.total_return
    })

# Find best
best = max(results, key=lambda x: x['sharpe'])
print(f"Best lookback: {best['lookback']}, Sharpe: {best['sharpe']:.2f}")

docs.title: Walk-Forward Optimization
docs.text:
For robust parameter selection:

Rust
use sig_runtime::{WalkForward, WalkForwardConfig};

let config = WalkForwardConfig::new(252, 126, 21);
let mut wf = WalkForward::new(config);

// Define parameter ranges
wf.add_range("lookback", 10.0, 60.0, 10.0);
wf.add_range("vol_window", 20.0, 120.0, 20.0);

let result = wf.run(&ir, &mut runtime)?;
println!("Best params: {:?}", result.best_params);
println!("Efficiency: {:.1}%", result.efficiency_ratio * 100.0);

docs.title: 1. Use Meaningful Defaults
docs.text:
Choose defaults that work across market conditions:

Text Only
params:
  lookback = 20       // Reasonable for many strategies
  vol_window = 60     // Standard volatility estimation
  winsor_pct = 0.01   // Common outlier threshold

docs.title: 2. Document Parameter Purpose
docs.text:
Add comments explaining each parameter:

Text Only
params:
  lookback = 20       // Days to compute returns
  skip_days = 5       // Days to skip for reversal
  vol_window = 60     // Window for volatility estimation
  winsor_pct = 0.01   // Percentile for winsorization

docs.title: 3. Avoid Over-Parameterization
docs.text:
Too many parameters leads to overfitting:

Text Only
// Too many parameters - suspicious
params:
  lookback = 17
  skip = 3
  vol_window = 47
  winsor_pct = 0.023
  decay = 0.937
  threshold = 0.178

docs.title: 4. Use Round Numbers
docs.text:
Prefer simple, interpretable values:

Text Only
// Good
params:
  lookback = 20
  vol_window = 60

// Suspicious (likely overfit)
params:
  lookback = 17
  vol_window = 53

docs.title: 5. Test Sensitivity
docs.text:
Parameters should be robust to small changes:

Bash
# Test multiple lookback values
sigc run strategy_10d.sig
sigc run strategy_20d.sig
sigc run strategy_30d.sig

# Results should be similar

docs.title: Momentum Strategy
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  // Return calculation
  lookback = 252        // 1-year lookback
  skip_days = 21        // Skip recent month

  // Volatility
  vol_window = 60       // Vol estimation window

  // Cleaning
  winsor_pct = 0.01     // Clip at 1st/99th percentile

  // Portfolio
  top_pct = 0.2         // Long top 20%
  bottom_pct = 0.2      // Short bottom 20%
  max_weight = 0.05     // Max 5% per position

signal momentum:
  // 12-1 month momentum, skip recent
  total = ret(prices, lookback)
  recent = ret(prices, skip_days)
  mom = total - recent

  // Volatility adjustment
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), vol_window)
  vol_adj = mom / vol

  // Clean and normalize
  emit winsor(zscore(vol_adj), winsor_pct)

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(
    top=top_pct,
    bottom=bottom_pct,
    cap=max_weight
  )
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Multi-Factor Strategy
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  // Factor lookbacks
  mom_lookback = 60
  rev_lookback = 5
  vol_lookback = 20

  // Factor weights
  mom_weight = 0.5
  rev_weight = 0.3
  vol_weight = 0.2

  // Portfolio
  top_pct = 0.2
  bottom_pct = 0.2

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, mom_lookback))

signal reversal:
  emit -zscore(ret(prices, rev_lookback))

signal low_vol:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), vol_lookback)
  emit -zscore(vol)

signal combined:
  emit mom_weight * momentum + rev_weight * reversal + vol_weight * low_vol

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(combined).long_short(top=top_pct, bottom=bottom_pct)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Signal Section - Computing signals
  • Walk-Forward - Parameter optimization
  • Tutorials - Complete examples

docs.title: Portfolio Section
docs.text: The portfolio section constructs portfolios from signals and runs backtests.

docs.title: Syntax
Basic Usage
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 20))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Long-Short Portfolio
docs.text:
The most common construction: long winners, short losers.

Text Only
portfolio long_short:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Parameters:

  • top: Fraction of assets to long (default: 0.2)
  • bottom: Fraction of assets to short (default: 0.2)
  • cap: Maximum position size (optional)

docs.title: With Position Cap
docs.text:
Limit individual position sizes:

Text Only
portfolio capped:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.05)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

With cap=0.05, no position exceeds 5% of portfolio.

docs.title: Asymmetric Long-Short
docs.text:
Different top/bottom percentages:

Text Only
portfolio asymmetric:
  // More longs than shorts
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.3, bottom=0.1)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Date Range
docs.text:
Required: specify start and end dates.

Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Rebalancing Frequency
docs.text:
Default is daily. Specify different frequencies:

Text Only
// Weekly (every 5 trading days)
portfolio weekly:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=5 from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

// Monthly (every 21 trading days)
portfolio monthly:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=21 from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

// Quarterly (every 63 trading days)
portfolio quarterly:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=63 from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Benchmark
docs.text:
Specify a benchmark for relative metrics:

Text Only
portfolio hedged:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest benchmark=SPY from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Enables calculation of:

  • Alpha
  • Beta
  • Information Ratio
  • Tracking Error

docs.title: Basic Commission
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio with_commission:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  costs = tc.bps(5)  // 5 basis points per trade
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Commission + Slippage
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio institutional:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  costs = tc.bps(5) + slippage.model("linear", coef=0.5)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Advanced Cost Model
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio realistic:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  costs = tc.bps(3) + slippage.model("square-root", coef=0.1)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Slippage models:

  • "linear": Cost proportional to trade size
  • "square-root": Cost proportional to sqrt(size)
  • "almgren-chriss": Full market impact model

See Transaction Costs for details.

docs.title: Multiple Portfolios
docs.text:
Define multiple portfolios for comparison:

Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

// Different top/bottom percentages
portfolio conservative:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.1, bottom=0.1)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

portfolio aggressive:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.3, bottom=0.3)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

// Different rebalancing
portfolio daily:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

portfolio weekly:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=5 from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Compare with:

Bash
sigc diff conservative.sig aggressive.sig

docs.title: Using Rank
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio ranked:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

rank() converts scores to uniform ranks (0 to 1), making portfolios robust to outliers.

docs.title: Using Signal Directly
Combined Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal reversal:
  emit -zscore(ret(prices, 5))

portfolio combined:
  combined_score = 0.7 * momentum + 0.3 * reversal
  weights = rank(combined_score).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Backtest Results
docs.text:
Running a backtest produces:

Text Only
=== Backtest Results ===
Total Return:         45.23%
Annualized Return:    10.12%
Sharpe Ratio:          1.23
Max Drawdown:         12.45%
Turnover:            285.00%

docs.title: With Benchmark
docs.text:
Text Only
=== Backtest Results ===
Total Return:         45.23%
Annualized Return:    10.12%
Sharpe Ratio:          1.23
Max Drawdown:         12.45%
Turnover:            285.00%

--- Benchmark-Relative ---
Alpha:                 5.67%
Beta:                  0.35
Information Ratio:     1.45
Tracking Error:        8.20%

docs.title: Export Results
docs.text:
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --output results.json

docs.title: Basic Momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"

params:
  lookback = 60
  top_pct = 0.2

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, lookback))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=top_pct, bottom=top_pct)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: With All Options
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "s3://bucket/prices.parquet"
  sectors: load csv from "data/sectors.csv" dtype=category

params:
  lookback = 60
  vol_window = 252
  top_pct = 0.2
  max_weight = 0.05
  commission_bps = 5

signal sector_neutral_momentum:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, lookback))
  neutral = neutralize(raw, by=sectors)
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), vol_window)
  vol_adj = neutral / vol
  emit winsor(zscore(vol_adj), 0.01)

portfolio institutional:
  weights = rank(sector_neutral_momentum).long_short(
    top=top_pct,
    bottom=top_pct,
    cap=max_weight
  )
  costs = tc.bps(commission_bps) + slippage.model("square-root", coef=0.1)
  backtest rebal=21 benchmark=SPY from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Multi-Strategy Portfolio
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal value:
  emit zscore(book_to_market)

signal quality:
  emit zscore(roe)

// Strategy 1: Momentum only
portfolio momentum_only:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

// Strategy 2: Value only
portfolio value_only:
  weights = rank(value).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

// Strategy 3: Combined
portfolio multi_factor:
  combined = 0.4 * momentum + 0.4 * value + 0.2 * quality
  weights = rank(combined).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 1. Use Ranking
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good: Robust to outliers
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

// Risky: Signal outliers affect weights
weights = signal.long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: 2. Set Position Caps
docs.text:
Text Only
// Prevent concentration
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.05)

docs.title: 3. Include Transaction Costs
docs.text:
Text Only
// Realistic performance estimate
costs = tc.bps(5) + slippage.model("square-root", coef=0.1)

docs.title: 4. Match Rebalancing to Signal
docs.text:
• Fast signals → frequent rebalancing
  • Slow signals → infrequent rebalancing
Text Only
// Fast momentum: rebalance more frequently
portfolio fast:
  weights = rank(fast_signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=5 from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

// Slow value: rebalance less frequently
portfolio slow:
  weights = rank(slow_signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=21 from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 5. Use Benchmark for Context
docs.text:
Text Only
backtest benchmark=SPY from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Backtesting - Detailed backtest guide
  • Transaction Costs - Cost modeling
  • Constraints - Position limits

docs.title: Signal Section
docs.text: The signal section defines computed signals that score assets.

docs.title: Syntax
Basic Structure
docs.text:
Every signal block has:

  1. A name
  2. Zero or more variable assignments
  3. One emit statement (required)
Text Only
signal momentum:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  normalized = zscore(returns)
  emit normalized

docs.title: Variable Assignment
docs.text:
Create intermediate variables to break down complex computations:

Text Only
signal step_by_step:
  // Step 1: Compute raw returns
  returns = ret(prices, 20)

  // Step 2: Adjust for volatility
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  vol_adj = returns / vol

  // Step 3: Normalize cross-sectionally
  normalized = zscore(vol_adj)

  // Step 4: Clean outliers
  cleaned = winsor(normalized, p=0.01)

  emit cleaned

docs.title: Variable Scope
docs.text:
Variables are scoped to their signal block:

Text Only
signal signal_a:
  x = ret(prices, 20)  // x only exists in signal_a
  emit x

signal signal_b:
  x = ret(prices, 60)  // Different x, independent
  emit x

docs.title: Referencing Previous Variables
docs.text:
Later variables can reference earlier ones:

Text Only
signal chained:
  step1 = ret(prices, 20)
  step2 = zscore(step1)        // Uses step1
  step3 = winsor(step2, 0.01)  // Uses step2
  emit step3

docs.title: The emit Statement
docs.text:
The emit statement specifies the signal's output:

Text Only
signal example:
  computation = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  emit computation  // This is the output

docs.title: Emit Must Be Last
docs.text:
The emit statement must be the last statement:

Text Only
// CORRECT
signal good:
  x = ret(prices, 20)
  emit zscore(x)

// ERROR: emit not last
signal bad:
  emit zscore(x)
  x = ret(prices, 20)

docs.title: Inline Emit
docs.text:
For simple signals, emit directly:

Text Only
signal concise:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 20))

docs.title: Multiple Signals
docs.text:
Define multiple signals in one file:

Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal reversal:
  emit -zscore(ret(prices, 5))

signal volatility:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 20)
  emit -zscore(vol)  // Low vol is good

docs.title: Referencing Other Signals
docs.text:
Signals can reference other signals:

Text Only
signal base_momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal adjusted_momentum:
  // Uses base_momentum signal
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  emit base_momentum / vol

docs.title: Combining Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal reversal:
  emit -zscore(ret(prices, 5))

signal combined:
  // Reference other signals
  mom = momentum
  rev = reversal
  emit 0.7 * mom + 0.3 * rev

docs.title: Using Functions
docs.text:
Call custom functions in signals:

Text Only
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

fn sharpe_ratio(returns, window=252):
  rolling_mean(returns, window) / rolling_std(returns, window)

signal vol_adjusted:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  vol = volatility(prices, 60)
  emit zscore(returns / vol)

docs.title: Using Macros
docs.text:
Invoke macros for reusable patterns:

Text Only
macro momentum_signal(px: expr, lookback: number = 20):
  let r = ret(px, lookback)
  let vol = rolling_std(ret(px, 1), 60)
  emit zscore(r / vol)

signal my_momentum:
  emit momentum_signal(prices, 30)

docs.title: Momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  // 12-1 month momentum (skip recent month)
  total_return = ret(prices, 252)
  recent_return = ret(prices, 21)
  raw_mom = total_return - recent_return
  emit zscore(raw_mom)

docs.title: Mean Reversion
docs.text:
Text Only
signal mean_reversion:
  ma = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  std = rolling_std(prices, 20)
  z_score = (prices - ma) / std
  emit -zscore(z_score)  // Negative: fade extremes

docs.title: Value
docs.text:
Text Only
signal value:
  // Book-to-market ratio
  bm = book_value / prices
  emit zscore(bm)

docs.title: Quality
docs.text:
Text Only
signal quality:
  roe_z = zscore(roe)
  leverage_z = -zscore(debt_to_equity)
  emit (roe_z + leverage_z) / 2

docs.title: Volatility (Low Vol)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal low_volatility:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  emit -zscore(vol)  // Lower vol is better

docs.title: Technical (RSI)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal rsi_signal:
  rsi_value = rsi(prices, 14)
  centered = rsi_value - 50
  emit -zscore(centered)  // Contrarian: buy oversold

docs.title: Normalization
docs.text:
Always normalize for cross-sectional comparability:

Text Only
signal normalized:
  raw = ret(prices, 20)
  emit zscore(raw)  // Mean=0, Std=1

docs.title: Outlier Handling
docs.text:
Clip extreme values:

Text Only
signal cleaned:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  emit winsor(raw, p=0.01)  // Clip at 1st/99th percentile

docs.title: Volatility Adjustment
docs.text:
Prevent high-vol stocks from dominating:

Text Only
signal vol_adjusted:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  emit zscore(returns / vol)

docs.title: Sector Neutralization
docs.text:
Remove sector bias:

Text Only
signal sector_neutral:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  emit neutralize(raw, by=sectors)

docs.title: Missing Data Handling
docs.text:
Text Only
signal with_fillna:
  raw = ret(prices, 20)
  filled = fill_nan(raw, 0)
  emit zscore(filled)

docs.title: 1. Use Descriptive Names
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good
signal vol_adjusted_momentum:
  ...

// Avoid
signal s1:
  ...

docs.title: 2. Break Down Complex Logic
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good: Clear steps
signal clear:
  step1_returns = ret(prices, 20)
  step2_vol_adj = step1_returns / vol
  step3_normalized = zscore(step2_vol_adj)
  emit winsor(step3_normalized, 0.01)

// Avoid: One giant expression
signal unclear:
  emit winsor(zscore(ret(prices, 20) / rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)), 0.01)

docs.title: 3. Add Comments
4. Always Normalize Output
docs.text:
Text Only
signal always_normalize:
  raw = some_computation(data)
  emit zscore(raw)  // Don't forget!

docs.title: 5. Handle Edge Cases
docs.text:
Text Only
signal robust:
  raw = ret(prices, 20)

  // Handle missing data
  filled = fill_nan(raw, 0)

  // Handle outliers
  normalized = zscore(filled)
  cleaned = winsor(normalized, 0.01)

  emit cleaned

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Portfolio Section - Convert signals to portfolios
  • Operators - Available operators
  • Macros - Reusable signal patterns

docs.title: Syntax Overview
docs.text: This page provides a complete reference for sigc syntax.

docs.title: Program Structure
Whitespace
docs.text:
Whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines) separates tokens but is otherwise insignificant:

Text Only
// These are equivalent
x = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
x=zscore(ret(prices,20))

Indentation is conventional but not required:

Text Only
signal example:
  x = ret(prices, 20)
  emit x

// Also valid (but less readable)
signal example:
x = ret(prices, 20)
emit x

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
Text Only
// C-style single-line
# Shell-style single-line

x = ret(prices, 20)  // Inline comment

Multi-line comments are not supported.

docs.title: Identifiers
docs.text:
Text Only
identifier ::= (letter | '_') (letter | digit | '_')*
letter     ::= 'a'..'z' | 'A'..'Z'
digit      ::= '0'..'9'

Examples: prices, my_signal, lookback_20, _internal

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
Reserved words cannot be used as identifiers:

Text Only
data, params, fn, macro, signal, portfolio,
emit, let, load, from, backtest, rebal, benchmark,
and, or, not, where, by, top, bottom, cap

docs.title: Numbers
docs.text:
Text Only
integer ::= '-'? digit+
float   ::= '-'? digit+ '.' digit+ ('e' '-'? digit+)?

Examples: 42, -5, 3.14, -0.5, 1e-3, 2.5e10

docs.title: Strings
docs.text:
Text Only
string ::= '"' <characters> '"'

Examples: "data/prices.csv", "s3://bucket/file.parquet"

docs.title: Dates
docs.text:
Text Only
date ::= digit{4} '-' digit{2} '-' digit{2}

Examples: 2024-01-01, 2020-12-31

docs.title: Data Section
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  <name>: load <format> from <path> [options]

docs.title: Formats
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv"
  volume: load parquet from "data/volume.parquet"
  factors: load arrow from "data/factors.arrow"

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load csv from "data/prices.csv" adjust=split_div
  sectors: load csv from "data/sectors.csv" dtype=category

docs.title: S3 Sources
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices: load parquet from "s3://bucket/prices.parquet"

docs.title: Params Section
Examples
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback = 20
  threshold = 0.5
  top_pct = 0.2
  skip_days = 5

docs.title: Function Definition
Examples
docs.text:
Text Only
fn volatility(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

fn sharpe(returns, window=252):
  rolling_mean(returns, window) / rolling_std(returns, window)

fn momentum_score(prices, fast=10, slow=60):
  zscore(ret(prices, fast) - ret(prices, slow))

docs.title: Calling Functions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal example:
  vol = volatility(prices)          // Uses default window=20
  vol60 = volatility(prices, 60)    // Overrides window
  emit vol

docs.title: Macro Definition
Parameter Types
docs.text: Type Description Example expr Any expression px: expr number Numeric value lookback: number string String literal path: string ident Identifier name: ident

docs.title: Examples
Invoking Macros
docs.text:
Text Only
signal example:
  emit momentum_signal(prices, 30, 90)

signal another:
  emit mean_reversion(prices)  // Uses defaults

docs.title: Signal Section
Variable Assignment
docs.text:
Text Only
signal example:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  normalized = zscore(returns)
  cleaned = winsor(normalized, p=0.01)
  emit cleaned

docs.title: Multiple Signals

Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal reversal:
  emit -zscore(ret(prices, 5))

signal combined:
  mom = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  rev = -zscore(ret(prices, 5))
  emit 0.7 * mom + 0.3 * rev

docs.title: Using Other Signals

signal base:

signal derived:
  emit winsor(base, p=0.01)

docs.title: Arithmetic
docs.text:
Text Only
+  addition
-  subtraction (binary) or negation (unary)
*  multiplication
/  division
Text Only
x + y
x - y
-x
x * y
x / y
x * 2 + y / 3

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text:
Text Only
>   greater than
<   less than
>=  greater or equal
<=  less or equal
==  equal
!=  not equal
Text Only
prices > threshold
score >= 0
status != excluded

docs.title: Logical
docs.text:
Text Only
and  logical AND
or   logical OR
not  logical NOT
Text Only
condition1 and condition2
a or b
not(excluded)

docs.title: Conditional
docs.text:
Text Only
where(condition, true_value, false_value)
Text Only
adjusted = where(is_nan(prices), 0, prices)
signed = where(score > 0, 1, -1)

docs.title: Function Calls
docs.text:
Text Only
function(arg1, arg2, ...)
function(arg, named=value)
Text Only
ret(prices, 20)
zscore(returns)
winsor(scores, p=0.01)
rolling_mean(data, 20)

docs.title: Method Chaining
docs.text:
Text Only
expression.method(args)
Text Only
rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: Operator Precedence
docs.text:
From highest to lowest:

  1. Function calls, method calls
  2. Unary -, not
  3. *, /
  4. +, -
  5. >, <, >=, <=, ==, !=
  6. and
  7. or

Use parentheses to override:

Text Only
(a + b) * c
not(x and y)

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Text Only
// Volatility-Adjusted Momentum Strategy
// Implements 12-1 month momentum with vol adjustment

data:
  prices: load parquet from "s3://bucket/prices.parquet" adjust=split_div
  sectors: load csv from "data/sectors.csv" dtype=category

params:
  lookback = 252
  skip = 21
  vol_window = 60
  winsor_pct = 0.01
  top_pct = 0.2
  bottom_pct = 0.2
  max_weight = 0.05

// Custom function for volatility
fn vol(x, window=20):
  rolling_std(ret(x, 1), window)

// Macro for momentum calculation
macro mom_signal(px: expr, lb: number = 252, sk: number = 21):
  let total = ret(px, lb)
  let recent = ret(px, sk)
  let mom = total - recent
  emit zscore(mom)

signal momentum:
  // Use macro
  raw = mom_signal(prices, lookback, skip)

  // Volatility adjustment
  volatility = vol(prices, vol_window)
  vol_adj = raw / volatility

  // Sector neutralization
  neutral = neutralize(vol_adj, by=sectors)

  // Clean outliers
  cleaned = winsor(zscore(neutral), winsor_pct)

  emit cleaned

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(
    top=top_pct,
    bottom=bottom_pct,
    cap=max_weight
  )
  costs = tc.bps(5) + slippage.model("square-root", coef=0.1)
  backtest rebal=21 benchmark=SPY from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Most Common Operators
docs.text:
Text Only
// Returns
ret(prices, 20)           // 20-day return

// Time-series

rolling_std(x, 20)        // 20-day rolling std

// Cross-sectional

rank(x)                   // Rank 0-1

// Portfolio

Example:

zscore(x: Panel) -> Panel
rolling_mean(x: Panel, window: Scalar) -> Panel

docs.text:

signal chained:
  step1 = ret(prices, 20)
  step2 = zscore(step1)
  step3 = winsor(step2, p=0.01)
  emit step3

signal composed:
