docs.title: Arithmetic Operators
docs.text: Mathematical operations for signal computation.

docs.title: Addition (+)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a + b

Element-wise addition.

Text Only
signal example:
  adjusted = prices + 1
  combined = signal_a + signal_b
  emit combined

docs.title: Subtraction (-)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a - b

Element-wise subtraction.

Text Only
signal example:
  change = prices - lag(prices, 1)
  spread = signal_a - signal_b
  emit spread

docs.title: Multiplication (*)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a * b

Element-wise multiplication.

Text Only
signal example:
  scaled = returns * 252        // Annualize
  weighted = signal * weight
  emit scaled

docs.title: Division (/)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a / b

Element-wise division.

Text Only
signal example:
  ratio = prices / lag(prices, 20)
  vol_adj = returns / volatility
  emit vol_adj

docs.title: Negation (-)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = -x

Negate values.

Text Only
signal example:
  inverted = -returns
  contrarian = -zscore(returns)  // Buy losers, sell winners
  emit contrarian

docs.title: abs(x)
docs.text:
Absolute value.

Text Only
abs(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  absolute_return = abs(returns)
  distance = abs(prices - moving_avg)
  emit distance

docs.title: sign(x)
docs.text:
Sign of value (-1, 0, or 1).

Text Only
sign(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  direction = sign(returns)
  // direction is -1 for negative, 0 for zero, 1 for positive
  emit direction

docs.title: log(x)
docs.text:
Natural logarithm.

Text Only
log(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  log_prices = log(prices)
  log_return = log(prices / lag(prices, 1))
  log_market_cap = log(market_cap)
  emit log_return

Warning

log(x) is undefined for x <= 0. Ensure positive values.

docs.title: exp(x)
docs.text:
Exponential function (e^x).

Text Only
exp(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  growth = exp(log_return)
  cumulative = exp(cumsum(log_returns))
  emit growth

docs.title: pow(x, n)
docs.text:
Raise to power.

Text Only
pow(x: Numeric, n: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  squared = pow(returns, 2)
  variance = rolling_mean(squared, 20)
  cubed = pow(x, 3)
  emit variance

docs.title: sqrt(x)
docs.text:
Square root.

Text Only
sqrt(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  std_dev = sqrt(variance)
  annualized_vol = sqrt(252) * daily_vol
  emit std_dev

Warning

sqrt(x) is undefined for x < 0. Ensure non-negative values.

docs.title: floor(x)
docs.text:
Round down to nearest integer.

Text Only
floor(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  buckets = floor(score * 10)  // 0-9 buckets
  emit buckets

docs.title: ceil(x)
docs.text:
Round up to nearest integer.

Text Only
ceil(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  min_position = ceil(score)
  emit min_position

docs.title: round(x)
docs.text:
Round to nearest integer.

Text Only
round(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  discrete = round(score)
  emit discrete

docs.title: min(a, b)
docs.text:
Element-wise minimum.

Text Only
min(a: Numeric, b: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  capped = min(score, 2)        // Cap at 2
  lower = min(signal_a, signal_b)
  emit capped

docs.title: max(a, b)
docs.text:
Element-wise maximum.

Text Only
max(a: Numeric, b: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  floored = max(score, -2)      // Floor at -2
  higher = max(signal_a, signal_b)
  emit floored

docs.title: clip(x, lo, hi)
docs.text:
Clamp values to range [lo, hi].

Text Only
clip(x: Numeric, lo: Numeric, hi: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  bounded = clip(score, -3, 3)  // Clip to [-3, 3]
  weights = clip(raw_weights, -0.1, 0.1)  // Max 10% position
  emit bounded

Equivalent to:

Text Only
clip(x, lo, hi) = max(min(x, hi), lo)

docs.title: Log Returns
docs.text:
Text Only
signal log_returns:
  log_ret = log(prices / lag(prices, 1))
  emit log_ret

docs.title: Volatility Scaling
docs.text:
Text Only
signal vol_scaled:
  target_vol = 0.15  // 15% annualized
  current_vol = sqrt(252) * rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 20)
  scale_factor = target_vol / current_vol
  emit returns * scale_factor

docs.title: Distance from Mean
docs.text:
Text Only
signal distance:
  ma = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  dist = abs(prices - ma)
  normalized_dist = dist / ma
  emit normalized_dist

docs.title: Bounded Scores
docs.text:
Text Only
signal bounded:
  raw = zscore(returns)
  bounded = clip(raw, -3, 3)  // Clip extremes
  emit bounded

docs.title: Annualization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal annualized:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  annual_ret = daily_ret * 252
  daily_vol = rolling_std(daily_ret, 20)
  annual_vol = daily_vol * sqrt(252)
  sharpe = annual_ret / annual_vol
  emit sharpe

docs.title: Scalar Operations
docs.text:
Text Only
x + 1        // Adds 1 to every element
x * 2        // Multiplies every element by 2
x / 100      // Divides every element by 100

docs.title: Element-wise Operations
docs.text:
Text Only
a + b        // Adds corresponding elements
a * b        // Multiplies corresponding elements

docs.title: Broadcasting
docs.text:
Scalars broadcast to match array dimensions:

Text Only
prices + 1           // 1 broadcasts to all elements
returns * 252        // 252 broadcasts

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
From highest to lowest:

  1. Function calls: abs(x), sqrt(x)
  2. Unary negation: -x
  3. Multiplication/Division: *, /
  4. Addition/Subtraction: +, -

Use parentheses to clarify:

Text Only
a + b * c      // = a + (b * c)
(a + b) * c    // = (a + b) * c

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Comparison Operators
  • Time-Series Operators
  • Cross-Sectional Operators

docs.title: Comparison Operators
docs.text: Relational operators that produce boolean results.

docs.title: Greater Than (>)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a > b

True where a is greater than b.

Text Only
signal example:
  above_ma = prices > rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  positive = returns > 0
  emit above_ma

docs.title: Less Than (<)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a < b

True where a is less than b.

Text Only
signal example:
  below_ma = prices < rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  negative = returns < 0
  oversold = rsi < 30
  emit below_ma

docs.title: Greater or Equal (>=)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a >= b

True where a is greater than or equal to b.

Text Only
signal example:
  at_or_above = prices >= threshold
  included = score >= cutoff
  emit at_or_above

docs.title: Less or Equal (<=)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a <= b

True where a is less than or equal to b.

Text Only
signal example:
  at_or_below = prices <= threshold
  low_vol = volatility <= 0.2
  emit at_or_below

docs.title: Equal (==)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a == b

True where a equals b.

Text Only
signal example:
  is_tech = sector == "Technology"
  at_target = price == target_price
  emit is_tech

docs.title: Not Equal (!=)
docs.text:
Text Only
result = a != b

True where a does not equal b.

Text Only
signal example:
  not_excluded = status != "Excluded"
  active = volume != 0
  emit not_excluded

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
Text Only
signal filtered:
  raw_score = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  // Only keep positive scores
  filtered = where(raw_score > 0, raw_score, 0)
  emit filtered

docs.title: Conditional Logic
docs.text:
Text Only
signal conditional:
  ma_20 = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  ma_50 = rolling_mean(prices, 50)

  // Trend direction
  uptrend = ma_20 > ma_50
  downtrend = ma_20 < ma_50

  // Signal based on trend
  signal = where(uptrend, 1, where(downtrend, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: Multiple Conditions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal multi_condition:
  positive_momentum = returns > 0
  low_volatility = volatility < 0.3
  not_excluded = status != "Excluded"

  // Combine conditions
  valid = positive_momentum and low_volatility and not_excluded
  score = where(valid, raw_signal, 0)
  emit score

docs.title: Range Checks
docs.text:
Text Only
signal in_range:
  // Check if score is in [-2, 2]
  in_range = score >= -2 and score <= 2
  bounded = where(in_range, score, 0)
  emit bounded

docs.title: Trend Detection
docs.text:
Text Only
signal trend:
  fast_ma = ema(prices, 10)
  slow_ma = ema(prices, 50)

  uptrend = fast_ma > slow_ma
  downtrend = fast_ma < slow_ma

  // +1 for uptrend, -1 for downtrend
  trend_signal = where(uptrend, 1, where(downtrend, -1, 0))
  emit trend_signal

docs.title: Breakout Detection
docs.text:
Text Only
signal breakout:
  high_20 = rolling_max(prices, 20)
  low_20 = rolling_min(prices, 20)

  breakout_up = prices > high_20
  breakout_down = prices < low_20

  signal = where(breakout_up, 1, where(breakout_down, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: RSI Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal rsi_signal:
  rsi_val = rsi(prices, 14)

  oversold = rsi_val < 30
  overbought = rsi_val > 70

  // Contrarian: buy oversold, sell overbought
  signal = where(oversold, 1, where(overbought, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: Volume Filter
docs.text:
Text Only
signal volume_filtered:
  avg_volume = rolling_mean(volume, 20)
  sufficient_volume = volume > avg_volume * 0.5

  raw_signal = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  filtered = where(sufficient_volume, raw_signal, 0)
  emit filtered

docs.title: Quality Filter
docs.text:
Text Only
signal quality_filter:
  // Only include stocks with positive ROE
  quality_filter = roe > 0

  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  filtered = where(quality_filter, momentum, 0)
  emit filtered

docs.title: Comparison with Scalar
docs.text:
Text Only
signal scalar_comparison:
  // Compare with constant
  above_zero = returns > 0
  below_threshold = volatility < 0.3
  equals_one = bucket == 1
  emit above_zero

docs.title: Comparison with Series
docs.text:
Text Only
signal series_comparison:
  // Compare two series element-wise
  outperforms = stock_return > market_return
  above_ma = prices > rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  emit outperforms

docs.title: Chaining Comparisons
docs.text:
Use and/or to chain comparisons:

Text Only
signal chained:
  // NOT: a < x < b (doesn't work)
  // USE: x > a and x < b
  in_range = score > -2 and score < 2
  emit where(in_range, score, 0)

docs.title: NaN Handling
docs.text:
Comparisons with NaN return false:

Text Only
signal nan_handling:
  // NaN > 0 is false
  // NaN == NaN is false

  // Check for NaN explicitly
  is_valid = not(is_nan(x))
  valid_positive = is_valid and x > 0
  emit valid_positive

docs.title: Type Coercion
docs.text:
Comparison results are boolean (true/false):

Text Only
signal bool_result:
  condition = prices > 100  // Boolean
  // Use in where() or logical operations
  result = where(condition, 1, 0)
  emit result

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
Comparison operators have lower precedence than arithmetic:

Text Only
a + b > c    // = (a + b) > c
a > b + c    // = a > (b + c)

But higher than logical operators:

Text Only
a > b and c < d    // = (a > b) and (c < d)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Logical Operators - Combine conditions
  • Data Handling - Handle missing data
  • Time-Series - Compute values to compare

docs.title: Cross-Sectional Operators
docs.text: Operators that compute values across assets at each point in time.

docs.title: zscore(x)
docs.text:
Cross-sectional z-score standardization.

Text Only
zscore(x: Panel) -> Panel

For each date: subtract mean, divide by standard deviation.

Text Only
signal example:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  normalized = zscore(returns)
  // Result: mean=0, std=1 at each date
  emit normalized

docs.title: rank(x)
docs.text:
Cross-sectional rank (0 to 1).

Text Only
rank(x: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  ranked = rank(returns)
  // Lowest = 0, Highest = 1
  emit ranked

docs.title: rank_pct(x)
docs.text:
Cross-sectional percentile rank (0 to 100).

Text Only
rank_pct(x: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  percentile = rank_pct(returns)
  // 50th percentile = median
  emit percentile

docs.title: scale(x)
docs.text:
Scale to sum to 1.

Text Only
scale(x: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Convert to weights
  abs_scores = abs(signal)
  weights = scale(abs_scores)
  // Sum of weights = 1
  emit weights

docs.title: demean(x)
docs.text:
Remove cross-sectional mean.

Text Only
demean(x: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Center around zero
  centered = demean(returns)
  // mean = 0 at each date
  emit centered

docs.title: winsor(x, p)
docs.text:
Winsorize at percentile p (clip at p-th and (1-p)-th percentile).

Text Only
winsor(x: Panel, p: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  normalized = zscore(returns)
  // Clip at 1st and 99th percentile
  cleaned = winsor(normalized, p=0.01)
  emit cleaned

docs.title: clip(x, lo, hi)
docs.text:
Clip values to range [lo, hi].

Text Only
clip(x: Panel, lo: Scalar, hi: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Clip scores to [-3, 3]
  bounded = clip(signal, -3, 3)
  emit bounded

docs.title: neutralize(x, by)
docs.text:
Group neutralization (demean within groups).

Text Only
neutralize(x: Panel, by: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Remove sector bias
  sector_neutral = neutralize(returns, by=sectors)
  emit sector_neutral

Each sector will have mean=0.

docs.title: quantile(x, q)
docs.text:
Cross-sectional q-th quantile value.

Text Only
quantile(x: Panel, q: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  median = quantile(returns, 0.5)
  q75 = quantile(returns, 0.75)
  emit median

docs.title: bucket(x, n)
docs.text:
Assign to n buckets (1 to n).

Text Only
bucket(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Quintiles
  quintile = bucket(returns, 5)
  // Returns 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
  emit quintile

docs.title: median(x)
docs.text:
Cross-sectional median.

Text Only
median(x: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  med = median(returns)
  // Compare to median
  above_median = where(returns > med, 1, 0)
  emit above_median

docs.title: mad(x)
docs.text:
Median Absolute Deviation.

Text Only
mad(x: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Robust measure of dispersion
  deviation = mad(returns)
  // Robust z-score
  robust_z = (returns - median(returns)) / deviation
  emit robust_z

docs.title: Standard Normalization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal normalized:
  raw = ret(prices, 20)
  z = zscore(raw)
  emit z

docs.title: Robust Normalization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal robust:
  raw = ret(prices, 20)
  z = zscore(raw)
  cleaned = winsor(z, p=0.01)
  emit cleaned

docs.title: Sector Neutralization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal sector_neutral:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  neutral = neutralize(raw, by=sectors)
  emit neutral

docs.title: Rank-Based Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
signal ranked:
  // Use ranks instead of raw values
  returns = ret(prices, 60)
  ranked = rank(returns)
  // Rank is robust to outliers
  emit ranked

docs.title: Quintile Spread
docs.text:
Text Only
signal quintile_spread:
  returns = ret(prices, 60)
  q = bucket(returns, 5)
  // Long Q5 (top), short Q1 (bottom)
  signal = where(q == 5, 1, where(q == 1, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: Multi-Step Normalization
Comparison to Median
docs.text:
Text Only
signal above_median:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  med = median(returns)
  above = where(returns > med, 1, -1)
  emit above

docs.title: Robust Z-Score
docs.text:
Text Only
signal robust_zscore:
  // Use MAD instead of std for robustness
  x = ret(prices, 20)
  med = median(x)
  deviation = mad(x)
  robust_z = (x - med) / deviation
  emit robust_z

docs.title: Market Neutralization
docs.text:
Text Only
signal market_neutral:
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  // Simple market neutralization
  market_neutral = demean(returns)
  emit zscore(market_neutral)

docs.title: Industry-Relative
docs.text:
Text Only
signal industry_relative:
  returns = ret(prices, 60)
  // Relative to industry peers
  industry_neutral = neutralize(returns, by=industry)
  emit zscore(industry_neutral)

docs.title: Cross-Sectional vs Time-Series
docs.text: Aspect Cross-Sectional Time-Series Direction Across assets Over time Example zscore(x) rolling_mean(x, 20) At date t Uses all assets Uses one asset Output Ranks among peers Historical pattern

docs.title: Type Behavior
docs.text:
All cross-sectional operators:

  • Input: Panel (dates × assets)
  • Output: Panel (same shape)
  • Operate on each date independently

docs.title: 1. Always Normalize
docs.text:
Text Only
// Raw returns vary in scale
raw = ret(prices, 20)
// Normalized for comparability
z = zscore(raw)

docs.title: 2. Handle Outliers
docs.text:
Text Only
z = zscore(raw)
cleaned = winsor(z, p=0.01)  // Clip extremes

docs.title: 3. Consider Sector Effects
docs.text:
Text Only
// If signal correlates with sectors
neutral = neutralize(signal, by=sectors)

docs.title: 4. Use Ranks for Robustness
docs.text:
Text Only
// Ranks are robust to outliers
ranked = rank(raw_signal)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Time-Series - Per-asset operators
  • Technical - Technical indicators
  • Portfolio - Weight construction

docs.title: Data Handling Operators
docs.text: Operators for missing data and cumulative computations.

docs.title: is_nan(x)
docs.text:
Check for NaN (Not a Number) values.

Text Only
is_nan(x: Numeric) -> Boolean
Text Only
signal example:
  missing = is_nan(prices)
  valid = not(is_nan(prices))
  emit where(valid, prices, 0)

docs.title: fill_nan(x, value)
docs.text:
Replace NaN values with a constant.

Text Only
fill_nan(x: Numeric, value: Scalar) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  // Fill missing prices with 0
  filled = fill_nan(prices, 0)

  // Fill missing returns with 0
  returns = ret(prices, 20)
  safe_returns = fill_nan(returns, 0)

  emit safe_returns

docs.title: coalesce(a, b)
docs.text:
Return first non-NaN value.

Text Only
coalesce(a: Numeric, b: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  // Use primary source, fallback to secondary
  price = coalesce(primary_price, secondary_price)

  // Chain multiple fallbacks
  value = coalesce(estimate_a, coalesce(estimate_b, default_value))

  emit price

docs.title: cumsum(x)
docs.text:
Cumulative sum.

Text Only
cumsum(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  // Cumulative returns
  cum_returns = cumsum(daily_returns)

  // Running total
  total_volume = cumsum(volume)

  emit cum_returns

docs.title: cumprod(x)
docs.text:
Cumulative product.

Text Only
cumprod(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  // Cumulative wealth (starting at 1)
  growth_factors = 1 + daily_returns
  wealth = cumprod(growth_factors)

  emit wealth

docs.title: cummax(x)
docs.text:
Cumulative maximum (running max).

Text Only
cummax(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  // Track all-time high
  all_time_high = cummax(prices)

  // Drawdown from peak
  drawdown = (prices - all_time_high) / all_time_high

  emit drawdown

docs.title: cummin(x)
docs.text:
Cumulative minimum (running min).

Text Only
cummin(x: Numeric) -> Numeric
Text Only
signal example:
  // Track all-time low
  all_time_low = cummin(prices)

  // Distance from trough
  recovery = (prices - all_time_low) / all_time_low

  emit recovery

docs.title: Handle Missing Returns
docs.text:
Text Only
signal safe_returns:
  raw_returns = ret(prices, 20)
  // Fill missing returns with 0 (no change)
  safe = fill_nan(raw_returns, 0)
  emit zscore(safe)

docs.title: Forward Fill (with Lag)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal forward_fill:
  // Use previous value if current is missing
  filled = where(is_nan(prices), lag(prices, 1), prices)
  emit filled

docs.title: Multiple Data Sources
docs.text:
Text Only
signal multi_source:
  // Primary source preferred, fallback to secondary
  combined = coalesce(primary_data, secondary_data)

  // Triple fallback
  best_estimate = coalesce(estimate_a, coalesce(estimate_b, estimate_c))

  emit combined

docs.title: Drawdown Calculation
Wealth Index
High Water Mark
Data Quality Score
docs.text:
Text Only
signal quality:
  // Count non-missing values
  has_price = where(is_nan(prices), 0, 1)
  has_volume = where(is_nan(volume), 0, 1)

  // Quality score
  quality = (has_price + has_volume) / 2

  emit quality

docs.title: Safe Division
docs.text:
Text Only
signal safe_div:
  // Avoid division by zero
  ratio = where(denominator != 0, numerator / denominator, 0)

  // Or use coalesce
  safe_ratio = coalesce(numerator / denominator, 0)

  emit ratio

docs.title: Cumulative Indicator
docs.text:
Text Only
signal trend_days:
  // Count consecutive up days
  up = where(ret(prices, 1) > 0, 1, 0)
  cum_up = cumsum(up)

  emit cum_up

docs.title: NaN Propagation
docs.text:
Most operators propagate NaN:

Text Only
// If any input is NaN, output is NaN
NaN + 1 = NaN
zscore([1, NaN, 3]) = [z1, NaN, z3]
rolling_mean([1, NaN, 3], 2) = [NaN, NaN, NaN]  // Window contains NaN

Handle NaN explicitly:

Text Only
signal robust:
  // Remove NaN before computation
  clean = fill_nan(raw_data, 0)
  result = zscore(clean)
  emit result

docs.title: Type Behavior
docs.text: Operator Input Output is_nan Numeric Boolean fill_nan Numeric, Scalar Numeric coalesce Numeric, Numeric Numeric cumsum Numeric Numeric cumprod Numeric Numeric cummax Numeric Numeric cummin Numeric Numeric

docs.title: 1. Handle Missing Data Early
docs.text:
Text Only
signal clean_first:
  // Clean data at the start
  clean_prices = fill_nan(prices, 0)

  // Then compute
  returns = ret(clean_prices, 20)
  emit zscore(returns)

docs.title: 2. Use Coalesce for Fallbacks
docs.text:
Text Only
signal with_fallback:
  // Prefer primary, use secondary if missing
  price = coalesce(bloomberg_price, yahoo_price)
  emit zscore(ret(price, 20))

docs.title: 3. Check for NaN in Conditions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal safe_condition:
  has_data = not(is_nan(x))
  valid_and_positive = has_data and x > 0
  emit where(valid_and_positive, x, 0)

docs.title: 4. Document Missing Data Handling
Next Steps
docs.text:
• Time-Series - Rolling computations
  • Cross-Sectional - Normalization
  • Logical - Conditional logic

docs.title: Logical Operators
docs.text: Boolean logic operators for combining conditions.

docs.title: and
docs.text:
Logical AND - true when both operands are true.

Text Only
result = a and b
Text Only
signal example:
  positive_momentum = returns > 0
  low_volatility = volatility < 0.3
  both = positive_momentum and low_volatility
  emit where(both, score, 0)

docs.title: or
docs.text:
Logical OR - true when either operand is true.

Text Only
result = a or b
Text Only
signal example:
  oversold = rsi < 30
  strong_momentum = momentum > 2
  either = oversold or strong_momentum
  emit where(either, 1, 0)

docs.title: not(x)
docs.text:
Logical NOT - inverts boolean value.

Text Only
result = not(x)
Text Only
signal example:
  excluded = status == "Excluded"
  included = not(excluded)
  emit where(included, score, 0)

docs.title: where(condition, true_value, false_value)
docs.text:
Conditional selection - returns true_value where condition is true, false_value otherwise.

Text Only
result = where(condition, true_value, false_value)
Text Only
signal example:
  // Replace NaN with 0
  cleaned = where(is_nan(x), 0, x)

  // Conditional scoring
  score = where(trend > 0, momentum, reversal)

  // Sign function
  sign = where(x > 0, 1, where(x < 0, -1, 0))

  emit cleaned

docs.title: Multiple Conditions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal multi_condition:
  cond1 = returns > 0
  cond2 = volatility < 0.3
  cond3 = volume > avg_volume

  // All conditions must be true
  all_true = cond1 and cond2 and cond3

  // At least one condition must be true
  any_true = cond1 or cond2 or cond3

  emit where(all_true, score, 0)

docs.title: Nested Conditionals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal nested:
  // Multiple categories
  category = where(score > 1, 3,
             where(score > 0, 2,
             where(score > -1, 1, 0)))
  emit category

docs.title: Conditional Scoring
docs.text:
Text Only
signal conditional_score:
  uptrend = ma_fast > ma_slow
  downtrend = ma_fast < ma_slow

  // Different signal based on trend
  trend_signal = where(uptrend, momentum, where(downtrend, reversal, 0))
  emit trend_signal

docs.title: Filter and Score
Regime Detection
docs.text:
Text Only
signal regime_aware:
  // Detect volatility regime
  current_vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 20)
  avg_vol = rolling_mean(current_vol, 252)
  high_vol = current_vol > avg_vol * 1.5

  // Different strategies for different regimes
  high_vol_signal = -zscore(ret(prices, 5))   // Mean reversion
  low_vol_signal = zscore(ret(prices, 60))    // Momentum

  signal = where(high_vol, high_vol_signal, low_vol_signal)
  emit signal

docs.title: Binary Indicator
docs.text:
Text Only
signal indicator:
  // Golden cross indicator
  golden_cross = ema(prices, 50) > ema(prices, 200)

  // Convert to +1/-1
  indicator = where(golden_cross, 1, -1)
  emit indicator

docs.title: Missing Data Handling
Exclusion List
docs.text:
Text Only
signal exclude:
  // Exclude based on multiple criteria
  too_small = market_cap < 1e9
  too_illiquid = avg_volume < 100000
  excluded_sector = sector == "Utilities"

  excluded = too_small or too_illiquid or excluded_sector
  included = not(excluded)

  filtered_score = where(included, raw_score, 0)
  emit filtered_score

docs.title: and
docs.text: a b a and b T T T T F F F T F F F F

docs.title: or
docs.text: a b a or b T T T T F T F T T F F F

docs.title: not
docs.text: a not(a) T F F T

docs.title: Short-Circuit Evaluation
docs.text:
and and or use short-circuit evaluation:

Text Only
// If a is false, b is not evaluated
result = a and b

// If a is true, b is not evaluated
result = a or b

This is useful for avoiding errors:

Text Only
// Safe: denominator checked before division
safe = denominator != 0 and (numerator / denominator > threshold)

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
From highest to lowest:

  1. not
  2. Comparison operators (>, <, ==, etc.)
  3. and
  4. or
Text Only
// These are equivalent:
a > b and c < d or e > f
((a > b) and (c < d)) or (e > f)

// Use parentheses for clarity
(a > b and c < d) or (e > f)

docs.title: Type Behavior
docs.text:
Logical operators work on boolean values:

Text Only
// Comparison produces boolean
condition = prices > 100  // Boolean

// Logical operators combine booleans
combined = condition and another_condition  // Boolean

// where() uses boolean to select values
result = where(combined, value_a, value_b)  // Same type as value_a/b

docs.title: NaN Handling
docs.text:
• NaN and x → false
  • NaN or x → depends on x
  • not(NaN) → true (because NaN is falsy)

For explicit NaN handling:

Text Only
is_valid = not(is_nan(x))

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Data Handling - Missing data operators
  • Comparison - Create conditions
  • Time-Series - Compute values

docs.title: Portfolio Operators
docs.text: Operators for constructing portfolio weights.

docs.title: long_short(top, bottom, cap)
docs.text:
Create dollar-neutral long-short weights.

Text Only
rank(signal).long_short(top: Scalar, bottom: Scalar, cap: Scalar = None) -> Panel

Parameters:

  • top: Fraction of assets to long (e.g., 0.2 = top 20%)
  • bottom: Fraction of assets to short (e.g., 0.2 = bottom 20%)
  • cap: Maximum position size (optional)
Text Only
portfolio main:
  // Long top 20%, short bottom 20%
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Weight Distribution
docs.text:
With long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2) and 100 assets:

Position Count Individual Weight Total Long 20 +5% +100% Neutral 60 0% 0% Short 20 -5% -100% Net 0% Gross 200%

docs.title: With Position Cap
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio capped:
  // No position > 5%
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.05)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

The cap limits individual position sizes while maintaining the long-short structure.

docs.title: Examples
neutralize(x, by)
docs.text:
Remove group exposure by demeaning within groups.

Text Only
neutralize(x: Panel, by: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal sector_neutral:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  // Demean within each sector
  neutral = neutralize(raw, by=sectors)
  emit neutral

After neutralization, each group (sector) has mean = 0.

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
Sector Neutralization:

Text Only
signal sector_neutral:
  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  neutral = neutralize(momentum, by=sectors)
  emit neutral

Industry Neutralization:

Text Only
signal industry_neutral:
  value = zscore(book_to_market)
  neutral = neutralize(value, by=industries)
  emit neutral

Country Neutralization:

Text Only
signal country_neutral:
  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  neutral = neutralize(momentum, by=countries)
  emit neutral

docs.title: clip(x, lo, hi)
docs.text:
Bound values to a range.

Text Only
clip(x: Panel, lo: Scalar, hi: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal bounded:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 20))
  // Clip to [-3, 3]
  bounded = clip(raw, -3, 3)
  emit bounded

docs.title: For Weights
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio constrained:
  raw_weights = some_weight_calculation
  // Limit position sizes
  bounded_weights = clip(raw_weights, -0.05, 0.05)

docs.title: Basic Long-Short
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio basic:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Sector-Neutral Long-Short
docs.text:
Text Only
signal sector_neutral_mom:
  raw = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  neutral = neutralize(raw, by=sectors)
  emit neutral

portfolio sector_neutral:
  weights = rank(sector_neutral_mom).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Position-Capped
docs.text:
Text Only
signal signal:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio capped:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Combined Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal value:
  emit zscore(book_to_market)

signal combined:
  emit 0.5 * momentum + 0.5 * value

portfolio multi_factor:
  weights = rank(combined).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Long-Only (Conceptual)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal signal:
  scores = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  // Only keep positive scores
  long_only = where(scores > 0, scores, 0)
  emit long_only

portfolio long:
  // Use rank for top selection
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0)
  backtest from 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Why Use Rank?
docs.text:
rank() before long_short() provides:

  1. Robustness: Not sensitive to outliers
  2. Uniform Distribution: Equal weight to each position
  3. Consistency: Same number of positions regardless of signal scale

docs.title: Without Rank (Signal-Weighted)
With Rank (Equal-Weighted)
docs.text:
Text Only
// Equal weight to each position
// Robust to outliers
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: Dollar Neutral
docs.text:
With equal top and bottom:

Text Only
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
// Sum of long weights = Sum of short weights (absolute)
// Net exposure = 0

docs.title: Gross Exposure
Adjusting Exposure
docs.text:
To achieve specific gross exposure:

Text Only
// For 150% gross (75% long, 75% short)
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
scaled_weights = weights * 0.75

docs.title: Integration with Backtest
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio full_example:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.05)
  costs = tc.bps(5) + slippage.model("square-root", coef=0.1)
  backtest rebal=21 benchmark=SPY from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 1. Always Use Rank
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good: Robust to outliers
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

// Risky: Sensitive to signal scale
weights = signal.long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: 2. Apply Position Caps
docs.text:
Text Only
// Prevent concentration
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.05)

docs.title: 3. Consider Sector Neutrality
docs.text:
Text Only
// If sector exposure is undesirable
neutral_signal = neutralize(raw_signal, by=sectors)
weights = rank(neutral_signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: 4. Match Rebalancing to Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
// Fast signal: frequent rebalancing
backtest rebal=5 from ...

// Slow signal: infrequent rebalancing
backtest rebal=21 from ...

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Portfolio Section - Full portfolio syntax
  • Backtesting - Running backtests
  • Constraints - Advanced constraints

docs.title: Technical Indicators
docs.text: Classic technical analysis indicators.

docs.title: rsi(x, n)
docs.text:
Relative Strength Index.

Text Only
rsi(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel

Returns value between 0 and 100.

  • RSI > 70: Overbought
  • RSI < 30: Oversold
Text Only
signal example:
  rsi_14 = rsi(prices, 14)
  emit rsi_14

docs.title: RSI Signal Example
docs.text:
Text Only
signal rsi_signal:
  rsi_val = rsi(prices, 14)

  // Contrarian: buy oversold, sell overbought
  oversold = rsi_val < 30
  overbought = rsi_val > 70

  signal = where(oversold, 1, where(overbought, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: RSI as Continuous Signal
macd(x, fast, slow, signal)
docs.text:
Moving Average Convergence/Divergence.

Text Only
macd(x: Panel, fast: Scalar, slow: Scalar, signal: Scalar) -> Panel

Returns MACD histogram (MACD line - signal line).

  • Standard parameters: fast=12, slow=26, signal=9
Text Only
signal example:
  macd_hist = macd(prices, 12, 26, 9)
  emit macd_hist

docs.title: MACD Signal Example
docs.text:
Text Only
signal macd_signal:
  hist = macd(prices, 12, 26, 9)

  // Positive histogram = bullish
  bullish = hist > 0

  signal = where(bullish, 1, -1)
  emit signal

docs.title: MACD Crossover
docs.text:
Text Only
signal macd_crossover:
  hist_today = macd(prices, 12, 26, 9)
  hist_yesterday = lag(hist_today, 1)

  // Crossover signals
  bullish_cross = hist_today > 0 and hist_yesterday <= 0
  bearish_cross = hist_today < 0 and hist_yesterday >= 0

  signal = where(bullish_cross, 1, where(bearish_cross, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: atr(high, low, close, n)
docs.text:
Average True Range.

Text Only
atr(high: Panel, low: Panel, close: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel

Measures volatility based on high, low, close prices.

Text Only
signal example:
  atr_14 = atr(high, low, close, 14)
  emit atr_14

docs.title: ATR-Based Position Sizing
docs.text:
Text Only
signal atr_signal:
  atr_val = atr(high, low, close, 14)

  // Inverse ATR for position sizing (less position for volatile stocks)
  inv_atr = 1 / atr_val

  // Normalize
  emit zscore(inv_atr)

docs.title: ATR Channel
docs.text:
Text Only
signal atr_channel:
  atr_val = atr(high, low, close, 20)
  ma = rolling_mean(close, 20)

  // Position in ATR channel
  distance = (close - ma) / atr_val
  emit distance

docs.title: vwap(price, volume)
docs.text:
Volume-Weighted Average Price.

Text Only
vwap(price: Panel, volume: Panel) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  vwap_price = vwap(prices, volume)
  emit vwap_price

docs.title: VWAP Deviation Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
signal vwap_signal:
  vwap_price = vwap(prices, volume)

  // Distance from VWAP
  deviation = (prices - vwap_price) / vwap_price

  // Mean reversion: fade moves away from VWAP
  emit -zscore(deviation)

docs.title: Trend Following
docs.text:
Text Only
signal trend:
  // EMA crossover
  fast = ema(prices, 10)
  slow = ema(prices, 50)
  trend = (fast - slow) / slow
  emit zscore(trend)

docs.title: Mean Reversion
docs.text:
Text Only
signal reversion:
  // Bollinger Band position
  ma = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  std = rolling_std(prices, 20)
  z = (prices - ma) / std
  emit -zscore(z)

docs.title: Momentum + RSI
docs.text:
Text Only
signal mom_rsi:
  // Combine momentum with RSI
  mom = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  rsi_val = rsi(prices, 14)
  rsi_sig = -zscore((rsi_val - 50) / 25)

  // Blend
  combined = 0.7 * mom + 0.3 * rsi_sig
  emit combined

docs.title: Volatility Breakout
docs.text:
Text Only
signal vol_breakout:
  atr_val = atr(high, low, close, 14)
  prev_close = lag(close, 1)

  // Breakout if moved > 2 ATR
  up_break = close > prev_close + 2 * atr_val
  down_break = close < prev_close - 2 * atr_val

  signal = where(up_break, 1, where(down_break, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: MACD + Trend Filter
docs.text:
Text Only
signal filtered_macd:
  // MACD signal
  macd_hist = macd(prices, 12, 26, 9)

  // Trend filter: only take MACD signals in trend direction
  ma_50 = rolling_mean(prices, 50)
  ma_200 = rolling_mean(prices, 200)
  uptrend = ma_50 > ma_200

  // Only long signals in uptrend
  signal = where(uptrend, where(macd_hist > 0, 1, 0),
                          where(macd_hist < 0, -1, 0))
  emit signal

docs.title: Williams %R
docs.text:
Text Only
signal williams_r:
  high_n = rolling_max(high, 14)
  low_n = rolling_min(low, 14)
  wr = (high_n - close) / (high_n - low_n) * -100
  // -100 to 0, oversold < -80, overbought > -20
  emit zscore(-wr)

docs.title: Stochastic Oscillator
docs.text:
Text Only
signal stochastic:
  low_14 = rolling_min(low, 14)
  high_14 = rolling_max(high, 14)
  k = (close - low_14) / (high_14 - low_14) * 100
  d = rolling_mean(k, 3)
  emit zscore(k - 50)

docs.title: On-Balance Volume (OBV)
docs.text:
Text Only
signal obv:
  price_change = delta(close, 1)
  signed_vol = where(price_change > 0, volume, where(price_change < 0, -volume, 0))
  obv = cumsum(signed_vol)
  // OBV trend
  obv_trend = obv - rolling_mean(obv, 20)
  emit zscore(obv_trend)

docs.title: Money Flow Index
docs.text:
Text Only
signal mfi:
  typical_price = (high + low + close) / 3
  money_flow = typical_price * volume

  pos_flow = where(delta(typical_price, 1) > 0, money_flow, 0)
  neg_flow = where(delta(typical_price, 1) < 0, money_flow, 0)

  pos_sum = rolling_sum(pos_flow, 14)
  neg_sum = rolling_sum(neg_flow, 14)

  mfi = 100 - (100 / (1 + pos_sum / neg_sum))
  emit zscore(mfi - 50)

docs.title: 1. Normalize Indicators
docs.text:
Text Only
// Raw RSI: 0-100
raw_rsi = rsi(prices, 14)

// Normalized: approximately [-2, 2]
normalized = (raw_rsi - 50) / 25
emit zscore(normalized)

docs.title: 2. Combine with Fundamentals
3. Use Confirmation
docs.text:
Text Only
signal confirmed:
  macd_sig = macd(prices, 12, 26, 9) > 0
  rsi_sig = rsi(prices, 14) < 70

  // Only long when both agree
  signal = where(macd_sig and rsi_sig, 1, 0)
  emit signal

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Portfolio - Weight construction
  • Tutorials - Complete strategies
  • Time-Series - Building blocks

docs.title: Time-Series Operators
docs.text: Operators that compute values over time for each asset independently.

docs.title: lag(x, n)
docs.text:
Shift values back by n periods.

Text Only
lag(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  previous_price = lag(prices, 1)     // Yesterday's price
  week_ago_price = lag(prices, 5)     // 5 days ago
  month_ago_price = lag(prices, 21)   // ~1 month ago
  emit previous_price

docs.title: ret(x, n)
docs.text:
Compute n-period return: (x - lag(x, n)) / lag(x, n)

Text Only
ret(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  daily_return = ret(prices, 1)       // 1-day return
  weekly_return = ret(prices, 5)      // 5-day return
  monthly_return = ret(prices, 21)    // ~1-month return
  annual_return = ret(prices, 252)    // ~1-year return
  emit monthly_return

docs.title: delta(x, n)
docs.text:
Compute n-period difference: x - lag(x, n)

Text Only
delta(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  price_change = delta(prices, 1)     // $ change
  volume_change = delta(volume, 5)    // Volume change
  emit price_change

docs.title: rolling_mean(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling mean (moving average).

Text Only
rolling_mean(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  sma_20 = rolling_mean(prices, 20)   // 20-day SMA
  sma_50 = rolling_mean(prices, 50)   // 50-day SMA
  avg_volume = rolling_mean(volume, 20)
  emit sma_20

docs.title: rolling_std(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling standard deviation.

Text Only
rolling_std(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  volatility = rolling_std(daily_ret, 20)  // 20-day vol
  annualized_vol = volatility * sqrt(252)
  emit volatility

docs.title: rolling_sum(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling sum.

Text Only
rolling_sum(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  volume_20d = rolling_sum(volume, 20)
  cum_return_5d = rolling_sum(ret(prices, 1), 5)
  emit volume_20d

docs.title: rolling_min(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling minimum.

Text Only
rolling_min(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  low_20d = rolling_min(prices, 20)
  support = rolling_min(prices, 52 * 5)  // 52-week low
  emit low_20d

docs.title: rolling_max(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling maximum.

Text Only
rolling_max(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  high_20d = rolling_max(prices, 20)
  resistance = rolling_max(prices, 52 * 5)  // 52-week high
  emit high_20d

docs.title: rolling_corr(x, y, n)
docs.text:
Rolling correlation between two series.

Text Only
rolling_corr(x: Panel, y: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Correlation with market
  market_corr = rolling_corr(stock_returns, market_returns, 60)

  // Pairs correlation
  pair_corr = rolling_corr(ret(stock_a, 1), ret(stock_b, 1), 20)

  emit market_corr

docs.title: ema(x, span)
docs.text:
Exponential moving average.

Text Only
ema(x: Panel, span: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  ema_10 = ema(prices, 10)
  ema_20 = ema(prices, 20)
  ema_50 = ema(prices, 50)

  // EMA crossover signal
  crossover = ema_10 - ema_50

  emit crossover

EMA formula: \(\alpha = 2 / (span + 1)\)

docs.title: decay_linear(x, n)
docs.text:
Linear decay weighted average.

Text Only
decay_linear(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel

Recent values have higher weights:

Text Only
signal example:
  // Recent days weighted more heavily
  weighted_return = decay_linear(ret(prices, 1), 20)
  emit weighted_return

docs.title: ts_argmax(x, n)
docs.text:
Index of maximum value in window (days since max).

Text Only
ts_argmax(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Days since 20-day high
  days_since_high = ts_argmax(prices, 20)
  emit days_since_high

docs.title: ts_argmin(x, n)
docs.text:
Index of minimum value in window (days since min).

Text Only
ts_argmin(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Days since 20-day low
  days_since_low = ts_argmin(prices, 20)
  emit days_since_low

docs.title: ts_rank(x, n)
docs.text:
Time-series rank (rank within asset's own history).

Text Only
ts_rank(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // How current price ranks vs last 20 days
  ts_rank_20 = ts_rank(prices, 20)
  emit ts_rank_20

docs.title: ts_skew(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling skewness.

Text Only
ts_skew(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  return_skew = ts_skew(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  emit return_skew

docs.title: ts_kurt(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling kurtosis.

Text Only
ts_kurt(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  return_kurt = ts_kurt(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  // High kurtosis = fat tails
  emit return_kurt

docs.title: ts_product(x, n)
docs.text:
Rolling product.

Text Only
ts_product(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Cumulative return over window
  growth_factors = 1 + ret(prices, 1)
  cum_return = ts_product(growth_factors, 20) - 1
  emit cum_return

docs.title: ts_zscore(x, n)
docs.text:
Time-series z-score (normalize within asset's history).

Text Only
ts_zscore(x: Panel, n: Scalar) -> Panel
Text Only
signal example:
  // Current price relative to own history
  price_zscore = ts_zscore(prices, 252)
  // Positive = above historical mean
  emit price_zscore

docs.title: Momentum
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  // 12-month return minus 1-month return
  r12 = ret(prices, 252)
  r1 = ret(prices, 21)
  mom = r12 - r1
  emit zscore(mom)

docs.title: Moving Average Crossover
docs.text:
Text Only
signal ma_crossover:
  fast = ema(prices, 10)
  slow = ema(prices, 50)
  crossover = (fast - slow) / slow
  emit zscore(crossover)

docs.title: Volatility
docs.text:
Text Only
signal volatility:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  vol_20 = rolling_std(daily_ret, 20)
  vol_60 = rolling_std(daily_ret, 60)
  // Ratio of short to long vol
  vol_ratio = vol_20 / vol_60
  emit zscore(vol_ratio)

docs.title: Bollinger Bands
docs.text:
Text Only
signal bollinger:
  ma = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  std = rolling_std(prices, 20)
  z = (prices - ma) / std
  emit z  // Position within bands

docs.title: Relative Strength
docs.text:
Text Only
signal relative_strength:
  // Price relative to 52-week range
  high_52 = rolling_max(prices, 252)
  low_52 = rolling_min(prices, 252)
  rs = (prices - low_52) / (high_52 - low_52)
  emit rs

docs.title: Channel Breakout
docs.text:
Text Only
signal breakout:
  high_20 = rolling_max(prices, 20)
  low_20 = rolling_min(prices, 20)
  mid = (high_20 + low_20) / 2

  // Position in channel (0 = bottom, 1 = top)
  position = (prices - low_20) / (high_20 - low_20)
  emit position

docs.title: Warm-up Period
docs.text:
Rolling operators require a warm-up period:

Text Only
// rolling_mean(x, 20) produces NaN for first 19 rows
// Because there aren't enough observations yet

Handle with fill_nan if needed:

Text Only
signal handle_warmup:
  raw = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  safe = fill_nan(raw, 0)
  emit safe

docs.title: Type Behavior
docs.text:
All time-series operators:

  • Input: Panel (dates × assets)
  • Output: Panel (same shape)
  • Operate on each asset independently

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Cross-Sectional - Across-asset operators
  • Technical - Technical indicators
  • Signal Section - Using in signals

docs.title: Production Deployment
docs.text: Deploy sigc strategies for live trading.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
sigc provides production-grade features:

  • Daemon Mode: Long-running signal computation
  • Safety Systems: Pre-trade checks and circuit breakers
  • Alerting: Notifications for anomalies and errors
  • Monitoring: Prometheus metrics and health checks
  • Scheduling: Cron-based signal generation
  • Audit Logging: Complete audit trail

docs.title: 1. Create Production Config
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.prod.yaml
mode: production

data:
  source: s3://bucket/prices/
  cache_ttl: 3600

signals:
  - momentum_strategy.sig

schedule:
  cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"  # 9 AM weekdays

output:
  type: alpaca
  paper: false

safety:
  max_position: 0.05
  max_daily_loss: 0.02
  max_drawdown: 0.10

alerting:
  slack:
    webhook: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
    channel: "#trading"

docs.title: 2. Start Daemon
docs.text:
Bash
sigc daemon --config sigc.prod.yaml

docs.title: 3. Monitor
docs.text:
Bash
# Check status
sigc status

# View logs
sigc logs --follow

# Check health
curl http://localhost:9090/health

docs.title: Before Going Live
docs.text:
• Backtest with realistic costs
  • Walk-forward validation complete
  • Paper trading for 30+ days
  • Safety limits configured
  • Alerting tested
  • Monitoring dashboards ready
  • Runbook documented
  • Rollback plan ready

docs.title: Data
docs.text:
• Data source reliable (SLA)
  • Backup data source configured
  • Corporate actions handled
  • Missing data alerts

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Server provisioned
  • Network connectivity tested
  • Secrets managed securely
  • Backups configured
  • Disaster recovery tested

docs.title: Production vs Development
docs.text: Aspect Development Production Data Local files S3/Database Caching Optional Required Safety Minimal Strict Logging Console Structured JSON Alerts None Slack/PagerDuty Monitoring Optional Required Error handling Fail fast Graceful degradation

docs.title: Idempotency
docs.text:
Same input produces same output:

Bash
# Running twice produces identical results
sigc run strategy.sig --date 2024-01-15
sigc run strategy.sig --date 2024-01-15  # Same output

docs.title: Determinism
docs.text:
Results are reproducible:

Bash
# Same version + same data = same results
sigc run strategy.sig --seed 42

docs.title: Graceful Degradation
docs.text:
Handle failures gracefully:

YAML
safety:
  on_data_error: use_cache      # Use cached data
  on_compute_error: hold        # Keep current positions
  on_execution_error: cancel    # Cancel pending orders

docs.title: 1. Paper Trade First
docs.text:
YAML
output:
  type: alpaca
  paper: true  # Paper trading mode

Run paper trading for at least 30 days.

docs.title: 2. Start Small
docs.text:
YAML
portfolio:
  initial_capital: 10000  # Start small
  max_position_pct: 0.02  # Very conservative

docs.title: 3. Set Conservative Limits
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  max_daily_loss: 0.01      # 1% daily loss limit
  max_position: 0.03        # 3% max per position
  max_drawdown: 0.05        # 5% drawdown stops trading

docs.title: 4. Monitor Everything
docs.text:
YAML
monitoring:
  prometheus:
    enabled: true
    port: 9090
  health_check:
    enabled: true
    port: 8080

docs.title: 5. Test Alerts
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alert test --channel slack

docs.title: Alerting
docs.text: Configure notifications for trading events, errors, and anomalies.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
sigc supports multiple alerting channels:

  • Slack: Team notifications
  • PagerDuty: On-call escalation
  • Email: Backup notifications
  • Webhooks: Custom integrations

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Create Slack App at https://api.slack.com/apps
  2. Add Incoming Webhook
  3. Configure in sigc:
YAML
alerting:
  slack:
    enabled: true
    webhook: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
    channel: "#trading-alerts"

docs.title: Alert Levels
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  slack:
    channels:
      info: "#trading-info"
      warning: "#trading-alerts"
      critical: "#trading-critical"
    mention:
      warning: "@here"
      critical: "@channel"

docs.title: Example Alert
docs.text:
Text Only
🚨 sigc Alert: Daily Loss Limit

Severity: HIGH
Strategy: momentum_strategy
Time: 2024-01-15 14:32:00 EST

Daily P&L: -2.1%
Limit: 2.0%

Action: Trading halted
Status: Circuit breaker triggered

@channel Please investigate immediately.

docs.title: Setup
Escalation Policies
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  pagerduty:
    escalation:
      - level: 1
        delay_minutes: 0
        target: on_call_engineer

      - level: 2
        delay_minutes: 15
        target: trading_lead

      - level: 3
        delay_minutes: 30
        target: engineering_manager

docs.title: Severity Mapping
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  pagerduty:
    severity_map:
      info: info
      warning: warning
      high: error
      critical: critical

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  email:
    enabled: true
    smtp:
      host: smtp.gmail.com
      port: 587
      username: ${SMTP_USERNAME}
      password: ${SMTP_PASSWORD}
      tls: true
    from: alerts@example.com
    to:
      - trading-team@example.com
      - oncall@example.com

docs.title: Email Template
Custom Webhook
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  webhooks:
    - name: custom_endpoint
      url: https://api.example.com/alerts
      method: POST
      headers:
        Authorization: "Bearer ${WEBHOOK_TOKEN}"
        Content-Type: application/json
      body_template: |
        {
          "alert": "${alert_name}",
          "severity": "${severity}",
          "message": "${message}",
          "timestamp": "${timestamp}"
        }

docs.title: Microsoft Teams
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  teams:
    enabled: true
    webhook: ${TEAMS_WEBHOOK}

docs.title: Define Alert Conditions
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  rules:
    # Performance alerts
    - name: daily_loss
      condition: "daily_pnl_pct < -0.02"
      severity: critical
      channels: [slack, pagerduty]
      message: "Daily loss limit exceeded: ${daily_pnl_pct}%"

    - name: large_drawdown
      condition: "drawdown > 0.10"
      severity: high
      channels: [slack, pagerduty]
      message: "Drawdown at ${drawdown}%"

    # Data alerts
    - name: data_stale
      condition: "data_age_hours > 2"
      severity: high
      channels: [slack, email]
      message: "Data is ${data_age_hours} hours old"

    - name: data_missing
      condition: "missing_symbols > 0"
      severity: warning
      channels: [slack]
      message: "Missing ${missing_symbols} symbols"

    # System alerts
    - name: high_memory
      condition: "memory_pct > 90"
      severity: warning
      channels: [slack]
      message: "Memory usage at ${memory_pct}%"

    - name: connection_lost
      condition: "broker_connected == false"
      severity: critical
      channels: [slack, pagerduty]
      message: "Lost connection to broker"

    # Trading alerts
    - name: large_position
      condition: "max_position_pct > 0.04"
      severity: warning
      channels: [slack]
      message: "Position in ${max_position_ticker} at ${max_position_pct}%"

    - name: high_turnover
      condition: "daily_turnover > 0.50"
      severity: warning
      channels: [slack]
      message: "Daily turnover at ${daily_turnover}%"

docs.title: Complex Conditions
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  rules:
    - name: sustained_loss
      condition: |
        daily_pnl_pct < -0.01 AND
        weekly_pnl_pct < -0.03
      severity: high
      channels: [slack, pagerduty]

    - name: correlation_breakdown
      condition: |
        abs(rolling_corr_benchmark - historical_corr) > 0.3
      severity: warning
      channels: [slack]

docs.title: Alert Throttling
docs.text:
Prevent alert fatigue:

YAML
alerting:
  throttling:
    # Don't repeat same alert within window
    dedupe_window_minutes: 30

    # Max alerts per hour
    max_alerts_per_hour: 10

    # Aggregate similar alerts
    aggregate:
      enabled: true
      window_minutes: 5

docs.title: Check Alert Status
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alerts status
Text Only
Active Alerts:
  [HIGH] data_stale - Data is 3 hours old (triggered 2h ago)

Recent Alerts (24h):
  [RESOLVED] daily_loss - Resolved after 45 minutes
  [WARNING] high_turnover - Turnover at 52% (auto-resolved)

Alert Statistics:
  Total (24h): 5
  Critical: 0
  High: 1
  Warning: 4

docs.title: Acknowledge Alert
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alerts ack --id alert_123

docs.title: Resolve Alert
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alerts resolve --id alert_123 --message "Investigated, false positive"

docs.title: Test All Channels
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alerts test

docs.title: Test Specific Channel
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alerts test --channel slack
sigc alerts test --channel pagerduty
sigc alerts test --channel email

docs.title: Simulate Alert
docs.text:
Bash
sigc alerts simulate --rule daily_loss --value -0.025

docs.title: Enable Daily Summary
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  reports:
    daily_summary:
      enabled: true
      time: "17:00"
      timezone: America/New_York
      channels: [email, slack]

docs.title: Daily Summary Content
docs.text:
Text Only
📊 sigc Daily Summary - 2024-01-15

Performance:
  Daily P&L: +1.2%
  MTD: +3.5%
  YTD: +8.2%

Positions:
  Long: 42 positions
  Short: 38 positions
  Gross: 185%
  Net: +5%

docs.title: 1. Start with Fewer Alerts
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  rules:
    # Start with critical alerts only
    - name: daily_loss_critical
      condition: "daily_pnl_pct < -0.03"
      severity: critical

Add more alerts as you understand baseline.

docs.title: 2. Use Severity Appropriately
docs.text: Severity When to Use info FYI, no action needed warning Worth watching, may need action high Needs attention soon critical Immediate action required

docs.title: 3. Include Context
docs.text:
YAML
message: |
  Daily loss exceeded: ${daily_pnl_pct}%
  Largest loss: ${worst_position_ticker} (${worst_position_pnl}%)
  Time in drawdown: ${drawdown_duration}
  Recommended: Review position sizing

docs.title: 4. Test Regularly
docs.text:
Bash
# Weekly alert testing
0 9 * * 1 sigc alerts test

docs.title: 5. Review and Tune
docs.text:
Track alert frequency and adjust thresholds:

Bash
sigc alerts stats --period 30d

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Monitoring - Prometheus metrics
  • Safety Systems - Circuit breakers
  • Configuration - Full config reference

docs.title: Audit Logging
docs.text: Comprehensive audit trail for compliance and debugging.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Audit logs capture:

  • All trading decisions
  • Order submissions and fills
  • Position changes
  • Configuration changes
  • User actions

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
audit:
  enabled: true
  file: /var/log/sigc/audit.log
  format: json

  # What to log
  events:
    - signals
    - orders
    - fills
    - positions
    - config_changes
    - user_actions

  # Retention
  retention:
    days: 365
    compress: true
    archive_to: s3://bucket/audit/

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Complete configuration reference for sigc.

docs.title: Location Priority
docs.text:
1. Command line: --config path/to/config.yaml
  2. Environment: $SIGC_CONFIG
  3. Current directory: ./sigc.yaml
  4. Home directory: ~/.sigc/config.yaml
  5. System: /etc/sigc/config.yaml

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
YAML format with environment variable substitution:

YAML
# Variables from environment
api_key: ${ALPACA_API_KEY}

# With default value
region: ${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}

docs.title: Full Configuration Reference
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.yaml - Complete Configuration

#=============================================================================
# Mode: development | staging | production
#=============================================================================
mode: production

#=============================================================================
# Data Sources
#=============================================================================
data:
  # Primary data source
  source: s3://my-bucket/prices/
  format: parquet

  # Backup data source (used if primary fails)
  backup:
    source: postgresql://readonly@db.example.com/marketdb
    query: "SELECT date, ticker, close, volume FROM prices"

  # Caching
  cache:
    enabled: true
    directory: /var/cache/sigc
    ttl_hours: 24
    max_size_gb: 10

  # Data validation
  validation:
    max_missing_pct: 0.05
    max_stale_hours: 24
    require_recent: true

#=============================================================================
# Strategies
#=============================================================================
strategies:
  - name: momentum
    file: strategies/momentum.sig
    enabled: true
    schedule: "0 9 * * 1-5"  # 9 AM weekdays

  - name: value
    file: strategies/value.sig
    enabled: true
    schedule: "0 9 * * 1-5"

#=============================================================================
# Output / Execution
#=============================================================================
output:
  type: alpaca

  alpaca:
    api_key: ${ALPACA_API_KEY}
    api_secret: ${ALPACA_API_SECRET}
    base_url: https://api.alpaca.markets
    paper: false

  # Order settings
  orders:
    type: limit            # market | limit | adaptive
    limit_offset_pct: 0.1  # 0.1% better than market
    time_in_force: day
    timeout_seconds: 60

  # Execution algorithm
  execution:
    algorithm: twap        # twap | vwap | pov | arrival
    duration_minutes: 30
    max_participation: 0.05

#=============================================================================
# Safety Systems
#=============================================================================
safety:
  # Position limits
  max_position: 0.05          # 5% max per position
  max_sector: 0.25            # 25% max per sector
  max_gross_exposure: 2.0     # 200% max gross
  max_net_exposure: 0.1       # 10% max net

  # Loss limits
  max_daily_loss: 0.02        # 2% daily loss limit
  max_weekly_loss: 0.05       # 5% weekly loss limit
  max_drawdown: 0.10          # 10% max drawdown

  # Trading limits
  max_order_size: 0.03        # 3% max single order
  max_daily_orders: 100       # Max orders per day
  max_daily_turnover: 0.50    # 50% max daily turnover

  # Circuit breakers
  circuit_breakers:
    enabled: true
    triggers:
      - type: daily_loss
        threshold: 0.02
        action: halt_trading

      - type: drawdown
        threshold: 0.10
        action: halt_trading

      - type: consecutive_losses
        count: 5
        action: alert

  # Error handling
  on_data_error: use_cache    # use_cache | hold | halt
  on_compute_error: hold      # hold | halt | alert
  on_execution_error: cancel  # cancel | retry | halt

#=============================================================================
# Alerting
#=============================================================================
alerting:
  # Slack integration
  slack:
    enabled: true
    webhook: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
    channel: "#trading-alerts"
    mention: "@oncall"

  # PagerDuty integration
  pagerduty:
    enabled: false
    api_key: ${PAGERDUTY_API_KEY}
    service_id: ${PAGERDUTY_SERVICE_ID}

  # Email
  email:
    enabled: true
    smtp_host: smtp.gmail.com
    smtp_port: 587
    username: ${SMTP_USERNAME}
    password: ${SMTP_PASSWORD}
    from: alerts@example.com
    to:
      - team@example.com
      - oncall@example.com

  # Alert rules
  rules:
    - name: large_loss
      condition: "daily_pnl < -0.01"
      severity: high
      channels: [slack, pagerduty]

    - name: position_limit
      condition: "max_position > 0.04"
      severity: medium
      channels: [slack]

    - name: data_stale
      condition: "data_age > 2h"
      severity: high
      channels: [slack, email]

#=============================================================================
# Monitoring
#=============================================================================
monitoring:
  # Prometheus metrics
  prometheus:
    enabled: true
    port: 9090
    path: /metrics

  # Health checks
  health:
    enabled: true
    port: 8080
    checks:
      - data_freshness
      - broker_connection
      - memory_usage
      - disk_space

  # Logging
  logging:
    level: info                # debug | info | warn | error
    format: json               # json | text
    file: /var/log/sigc/sigc.log
    max_size_mb: 100
    max_files: 10
    compress: true

#=============================================================================
# Daemon Settings
#=============================================================================
daemon:
  # RPC interface
  rpc:
    address: "127.0.0.1:5555"
    auth: token
    token: ${SIGC_RPC_TOKEN}

  # Process settings
  pid_file: /var/run/sigc.pid
  work_dir: /var/lib/sigc

  # Resource limits
  limits:
    max_memory_mb: 4096
    max_cpu_pct: 80
    worker_threads: 4

  # Recovery
  recovery:
    enabled: true
    max_restarts: 3
    restart_delay_seconds: 10

  # Shutdown
  shutdown:
    timeout_seconds: 60
    save_state: true

#=============================================================================
# Scheduling
#=============================================================================
schedule:
  timezone: America/New_York

  # Trading calendar
  calendar:
    type: nyse                # nyse | nasdaq | custom
    holidays: auto            # auto | custom file

  # Schedule rules
  rules:
    - name: morning_rebalance
      cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
      strategy: momentum
      if: market_open

    - name: eod_check
      cron: "0 15 * * 1-5"
      action: check_positions

#=============================================================================
# Audit Logging
#=============================================================================
audit:
  enabled: true
  file: /var/log/sigc/audit.log
  format: json

  # What to log
  log:
    - signals
    - orders
    - fills
    - positions
    - config_changes

  # Retention
  retention_days: 365

  # External sink
  sink:
    type: s3
    bucket: my-bucket
    prefix: audit/

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Variable Description SIGC_RPC_TOKEN RPC authentication token ALPACA_API_KEY Alpaca API key ALPACA_API_SECRET Alpaca API secret

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: Variable Description Default SIGC_CONFIG Config file path ./sigc.yaml SIGC_LOG_LEVEL Log level info SIGC_DATA_DIR Data directory /var/lib/sigc SIGC_CACHE_DIR Cache directory /var/cache/sigc AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS access key - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY AWS secret - AWS_REGION AWS region us-east-1

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.dev.yaml
mode: development

data:
  source: ./data/prices.csv
  format: csv

output:
  type: mock

safety:
  enabled: false

monitoring:
  logging:
    level: debug
    format: text

docs.title: Staging
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.staging.yaml
mode: staging

output:
  type: alpaca
  alpaca:
    paper: true  # Paper trading

safety:
  enabled: true
  # Same limits as production

docs.title: Production
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.prod.yaml
mode: production

output:
  type: alpaca
  alpaca:
    paper: false  # Live trading

safety:
  enabled: true
  max_daily_loss: 0.02

alerting:
  slack:
    enabled: true

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Validate configuration:

Bash
sigc config validate --config sigc.yaml

Output:

Text Only
Configuration validation: PASSED

Warnings:
  - alerting.pagerduty.enabled is false (no escalation for critical alerts)
  - safety.max_position (5%) may be too high for concentrated strategies

Recommendations:
  - Consider enabling audit logging for compliance
  - Set up backup data source for resilience

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Bash
export ALPACA_API_KEY=your_key
sigc daemon --config sigc.yaml

docs.title: AWS Secrets Manager
docs.text:
YAML
secrets:
  provider: aws-secrets-manager
  secret_name: sigc/production

docs.title: HashiCorp Vault
docs.text:
YAML
secrets:
  provider: vault
  address: https://vault.example.com
  path: secret/sigc

docs.title: 1. Use Separate Configs per Environment
docs.text:
Text Only
config/
├── sigc.dev.yaml
├── sigc.staging.yaml
└── sigc.prod.yaml

docs.title: 2. Never Commit Secrets
docs.text:
YAML
# Good
api_key: ${ALPACA_API_KEY}

# Bad
api_key: pk_live_abc123  # Never do this!

docs.title: 3. Validate Before Deploy
docs.text:
Bash
sigc config validate --config sigc.prod.yaml --strict

docs.title: 4. Version Control Configs
docs.text: Track configuration changes in git.

docs.title: 5. Document Changes
docs.text: Add comments explaining non-obvious settings.

docs.text:
• Safety Systems - Pre-trade checks
  • Alerting - Notification setup
  • Daemon Mode - Running as service

docs.title: Daemon Mode
docs.text: Run sigc as a long-running service for production trading.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Daemon mode provides:

  • Persistent process for continuous operation
  • RPC interface for external commands
  • Automatic scheduling of signal computation
  • Health monitoring and self-healing
  • Graceful shutdown handling

docs.title: Basic Start
docs.text:
Bash
sigc daemon

docs.title: With Configuration
docs.text:
Bash
sigc daemon --config sigc.yaml

docs.title: Background Process
docs.text:
Bash
sigc daemon --daemonize

docs.title: With PID File
docs.text:
Bash
sigc daemon --pid-file /var/run/sigc.pid

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.yaml
daemon:
  # RPC settings
  rpc:
    address: "127.0.0.1:5555"
    auth: token
    token: ${SIGC_RPC_TOKEN}

  # Process settings
  pid_file: /var/run/sigc.pid
  work_dir: /var/lib/sigc

  # Resource limits
  max_memory_mb: 4096
  max_cpu_pct: 80

  # Logging
  log_file: /var/log/sigc/daemon.log
  log_level: info

docs.title: RPC Commands
docs.text: The daemon exposes an RPC interface for control:

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
Bash
sigc status

Output:

Text Only
sigc daemon status
==================
Status: running
PID: 12345
Uptime: 3d 14h 22m
Memory: 1.2 GB / 4 GB
CPU: 15%

Strategies:
  momentum_strategy: active
    Last run: 2024-01-15 09:00:00
    Next run: 2024-01-16 09:00:00
    Status: success

  value_strategy: active
    Last run: 2024-01-15 09:00:00
    Next run: 2024-01-16 09:00:00
    Status: success

docs.title: Run Strategy
docs.text:
Bash
# Run immediately
sigc run momentum_strategy.sig

# Dry run (no execution)
sigc run momentum_strategy.sig --dry-run

# Override date
sigc run momentum_strategy.sig --date 2024-01-15

docs.title: Pause/Resume
docs.text:
Bash
# Pause all strategies
sigc pause

# Pause specific strategy
sigc pause momentum_strategy

# Resume
sigc resume
sigc resume momentum_strategy

docs.title: Reload Configuration
docs.text:
Bash
sigc reload

Reloads configuration without restarting daemon.

docs.title: Stop
docs.text:
Bash
# Graceful stop
sigc stop

# Force stop
sigc stop --force

# Stop with timeout
sigc stop --timeout 30

docs.title: Data Caching
docs.text:
The daemon caches data for efficiency:

YAML
daemon:
  cache:
    enabled: true
    directory: /var/cache/sigc
    max_size_gb: 10
    ttl_hours: 24

docs.title: Cache Management
docs.text:
Bash
# View cache status
sigc cache status

# Clear cache
sigc cache clear

# Clear specific data
sigc cache clear --pattern "prices_*"

docs.title: Health Endpoint
Liveness Probe
docs.text:
Bash
curl http://localhost:8080/live

docs.title: Readiness Probe
docs.text:
Bash
curl http://localhost:8080/ready

docs.title: Graceful Shutdown
docs.text:
On SIGTERM or sigc stop:

  1. Stop accepting new computations
  2. Complete in-progress computations
  3. Flush pending writes
  4. Close connections
  5. Exit cleanly
YAML
daemon:
  shutdown:
    timeout_seconds: 60
    save_state: true

docs.title: Crash Recovery
docs.text:
Daemon automatically recovers from crashes:

YAML
daemon:
  recovery:
    enabled: true
    max_restarts: 3
    restart_delay_seconds: 10

docs.title: State Persistence
docs.text:
Save state for recovery:

YAML
daemon:
  state:
    persist: true
    file: /var/lib/sigc/state.json

docs.title: Service File
docs.text:
INI
# /etc/systemd/system/sigc.service
[Unit]
Description=sigc Trading Daemon
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=sigc
Group=sigc
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/sigc daemon --config /etc/sigc/sigc.yaml
ExecReload=/usr/local/bin/sigc reload
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/sigc stop
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal

# Resource limits
MemoryLimit=4G
CPUQuota=80%

# Security
NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/sigc /var/log/sigc /var/cache/sigc

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

docs.title: Management
docs.text:
Bash
# Start
sudo systemctl start sigc

# Stop
sudo systemctl stop sigc

# Status
sudo systemctl status sigc

# Logs
journalctl -u sigc -f

# Enable on boot
sudo systemctl enable sigc

docs.title: Multiple Daemons
docs.text:
Run separate daemons for different purposes:

Bash
# Production daemon
sigc daemon --config sigc.prod.yaml --port 5555

# Paper trading daemon
sigc daemon --config sigc.paper.yaml --port 5556

docs.title: Memory Limits
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  limits:
    max_memory_mb: 4096
    gc_threshold_mb: 3072  # Trigger GC at 3GB

docs.title: CPU Limits
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  limits:
    max_cpu_pct: 80
    worker_threads: 4

docs.title: Connection Pools
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  connections:
    data_pool_size: 10
    broker_pool_size: 5
    timeout_seconds: 30

docs.title: Prometheus Metrics
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  metrics:
    enabled: true
    port: 9090
    path: /metrics

Exposed metrics:

Text Only
sigc_daemon_uptime_seconds
sigc_daemon_memory_bytes
sigc_computation_duration_seconds
sigc_computation_total
sigc_computation_errors_total
sigc_positions_count
sigc_trades_total

docs.title: Daemon Won't Start
docs.text:
Bash
# Check port availability
lsof -i :5555

# Check permissions
ls -la /var/run/sigc.pid

# Run in foreground for debugging
sigc daemon --foreground --log-level debug

docs.title: Connection Refused
docs.text:
Bash
# Check if daemon is running
sigc status

# Check RPC address
sigc status --address 127.0.0.1:5555

docs.title: High Memory Usage
docs.text:
Bash
# Check memory
sigc status --detailed

# Clear cache
sigc cache clear

# Restart daemon
sigc restart

docs.title: 1. Use Systemd in Production
docs.text: Provides automatic restart, logging, and resource management.

docs.title: 2. Set Resource Limits
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  limits:
    max_memory_mb: 4096
    max_cpu_pct: 80

docs.title: 3. Enable Health Checks
docs.text:
YAML
daemon:
  health:
    enabled: true
    interval_seconds: 30

docs.title: 4. Monitor Metrics
docs.text: Set up Prometheus/Grafana for visibility.

docs.title: 5. Test Shutdown/Recovery
docs.text:
Bash
# Test graceful shutdown
sigc stop
sigc daemon

# Test crash recovery
kill -9 $(cat /var/run/sigc.pid)
# Daemon should restart via systemd

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configuration - Full configuration reference
  • Scheduling - Automated scheduling
  • Monitoring - Prometheus metrics

docs.title: Docker Deployment
docs.text: Run sigc in containers for reproducible, scalable deployments.

docs.title: Pull Image
docs.text:
Bash
docker pull ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest

docs.title: Run Backtest
docs.text:
Bash
docker run -v $(pwd):/data ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest \
  run /data/strategy.sig

docs.title: Run Daemon
docs.text:
Bash
docker run -d \
  --name sigc-daemon \
  -v $(pwd)/config:/etc/sigc \
  -v $(pwd)/data:/data \
  -p 5555:5555 \
  -p 9090:9090 \
  ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest \
  daemon --config /etc/sigc/sigc.yaml

docs.title: Production Dockerfile
docs.text:
Docker
# Use official sigc image as base
FROM ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy strategy files
COPY strategies/ /app/strategies/

# Copy configuration
COPY config/sigc.yaml /etc/sigc/sigc.yaml

# Expose ports
EXPOSE 5555 9090 8080

# Run daemon
CMD ["daemon", "--config", "/etc/sigc/sigc.yaml"]

docs.title: Build Custom Image
docs.text:
Bash
docker build -t my-sigc:latest .

docs.title: Basic Setup
docs.text:
YAML
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  sigc:
    image: ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest
    container_name: sigc-daemon
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5555:5555"   # RPC
      - "9090:9090"   # Prometheus
      - "8080:8080"   # Health
    volumes:
      - ./config:/etc/sigc:ro
      - ./strategies:/app/strategies:ro
      - ./data:/data
      - sigc-cache:/var/cache/sigc
      - sigc-logs:/var/log/sigc
    environment:
      - ALPACA_API_KEY=${ALPACA_API_KEY}
      - ALPACA_API_SECRET=${ALPACA_API_SECRET}
      - SIGC_RPC_TOKEN=${SIGC_RPC_TOKEN}
    command: daemon --config /etc/sigc/sigc.yaml

volumes:
  sigc-cache:
  sigc-logs:

docs.title: With Monitoring Stack
docs.text:
YAML
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  sigc:
    image: ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest
    container_name: sigc-daemon
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5555:5555"
      - "9090:9090"
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./config:/etc/sigc:ro
      - ./strategies:/app/strategies:ro
      - ./data:/data
      - sigc-cache:/var/cache/sigc
      - sigc-logs:/var/log/sigc
    environment:
      - ALPACA_API_KEY=${ALPACA_API_KEY}
      - ALPACA_API_SECRET=${ALPACA_API_SECRET}
    command: daemon --config /etc/sigc/sigc.yaml
    networks:
      - sigc-network

  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    container_name: prometheus
    ports:
      - "9091:9090"
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml:ro
      - prometheus-data:/prometheus
    command:
      - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
      - '--storage.tsdb.retention.time=90d'
    networks:
      - sigc-network

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    container_name: grafana
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - grafana-data:/var/lib/grafana
      - ./grafana/dashboards:/etc/grafana/provisioning/dashboards:ro
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=${GRAFANA_PASSWORD}
    networks:
      - sigc-network

  alertmanager:
    image: prom/alertmanager:latest
    container_name: alertmanager
    ports:
      - "9093:9093"
    volumes:
      - ./alertmanager.yml:/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml:ro
    networks:
      - sigc-network

volumes:
  sigc-cache:
  sigc-logs:
  prometheus-data:
  grafana-data:

networks:
  sigc-network:

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: sigc
  labels:
    app: sigc
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sigc
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: sigc
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: sigc
          image: ghcr.io/skelf-research/sigc:latest
          args: ["daemon", "--config", "/etc/sigc/sigc.yaml"]
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5555
              name: rpc
            - containerPort: 9090
              name: metrics
            - containerPort: 8080
              name: health
          env:
            - name: ALPACA_API_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: sigc-secrets
                  key: alpaca-api-key
            - name: ALPACA_API_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: sigc-secrets
                  key: alpaca-api-secret
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/sigc
              readOnly: true
            - name: strategies
              mountPath: /app/strategies
              readOnly: true
            - name: cache
              mountPath: /var/cache/sigc
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "4Gi"
              cpu: "2000m"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /live
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 5
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: sigc-config
        - name: strategies
          configMap:
            name: sigc-strategies
        - name: cache
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: sigc-cache-pvc

docs.title: ConfigMap
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc-config.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: sigc-config
data:
  sigc.yaml: |
    mode: production
    data:
      source: s3://bucket/prices/
    schedule:
      jobs:
        - name: rebalance
          cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
          strategy: momentum

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text: Prometheus metrics and observability for sigc.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
sigc exposes comprehensive metrics for:

  • Strategy performance
  • System health
  • Trading activity
  • Data pipeline status

docs.title: Enable Metrics
docs.text:
YAML
monitoring:
  prometheus:
    enabled: true
    port: 9090
    path: /metrics

docs.title: Scrape Configuration
docs.text:
Add to your Prometheus config:

YAML
# prometheus.yml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'sigc'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:9090']
    scrape_interval: 15s
