docs.title: Dashboard Panels
docs.text:
Overview Panel: - Portfolio value over time - Daily P&L - Current drawdown - Key metrics

Performance Panel: - Cumulative returns - Rolling Sharpe - Drawdown chart - Monthly returns heatmap

Positions Panel: - Long/short counts - Gross/net exposure - Sector breakdown - Top positions

Trading Panel: - Orders by status - Turnover trend - Transaction costs - Fill rates

System Panel: - Memory/CPU usage - Computation times - Data freshness - Error rates

docs.title: Configure Alertmanager
docs.text:
YAML
# alertmanager.yml
route:
  group_by: ['alertname', 'strategy']
  group_wait: 30s
  group_interval: 5m
  repeat_interval: 4h
  receiver: 'default'

  routes:
    - match:
        severity: critical
      receiver: 'pagerduty'

receivers:
  - name: 'default'
    slack_configs:
      - channel: '#trading-alerts'

  - name: 'pagerduty'
    pagerduty_configs:
      - service_key: ${PAGERDUTY_KEY}

docs.title: Alert Rules
docs.text:
YAML
# sigc.rules.yml
groups:
  - name: sigc
    rules:
      - alert: SigcHighDrawdown
        expr: sigc_drawdown_pct > 0.10
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: high
        annotations:
          summary: "High drawdown: {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}"

      - alert: SigcDaemonDown
        expr: up{job="sigc"} == 0
        for: 1m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "sigc daemon is down"

      - alert: SigcDataStale
        expr: sigc_data_age_seconds > 7200
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: high
        annotations:
          summary: "Data is {{ $value | humanizeDuration }} old"

docs.title: 2. Alert on Symptoms, Not Causes
docs.text:
YAML
# Good: Alert on outcome
- alert: SigcHighDrawdown
  expr: sigc_drawdown_pct > 0.10

# Less useful: Alert on potential cause
- alert: SigcHighMemory
  expr: sigc_memory_used_pct > 80

docs.title: 3. Use Recording Rules
docs.text:
Pre-compute expensive queries:

YAML
groups:
  - name: sigc_recording
    rules:
      - record: sigc:sharpe_ratio_30d
        expr: |
          (avg_over_time(sigc_return_daily[30d]) * 252) /
          (stddev_over_time(sigc_return_daily[30d]) * sqrt(252))

    retention.time: 90d  # Keep 90 days

sigc monitoring test

Verify all metrics are being collected.

docs.title: Safety Systems
docs.text: Pre-trade checks and circuit breakers to protect your portfolio.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Safety systems prevent:

  • Excessive losses
  • Position concentration
  • Runaway algorithms
  • Data errors causing bad trades

docs.title: Pre-Trade Checks
docs.text:
Every order is validated before submission:

YAML
safety:
  pre_trade_checks:
    - position_limit
    - sector_limit
    - loss_limit
    - liquidity_check
    - price_sanity

docs.title: Position Limits
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  max_position: 0.05        # No position > 5%
  min_position: 0.001       # No position < 0.1%
  max_sector: 0.25          # No sector > 25%

If an order would violate limits, it's rejected:

Text Only
[REJECTED] Order would exceed position limit
  Ticker: AAPL
  Current Position: 4.2%
  Order Would Create: 6.1%
  Limit: 5.0%

docs.title: Loss Limits
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  max_daily_loss: 0.02      # 2% daily loss limit
  max_weekly_loss: 0.05     # 5% weekly loss limit
  max_monthly_loss: 0.10    # 10% monthly loss limit
  max_drawdown: 0.15        # 15% max drawdown

docs.title: Liquidity Checks
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  liquidity:
    min_adv: 1000000        # Min $1M average daily volume
    max_participation: 0.10  # Max 10% of daily volume
    max_order_pct_volume: 0.01  # Max 1% of daily volume per order

docs.title: Price Sanity
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  price_checks:
    max_spread_bps: 50      # Reject if spread > 50 bps
    max_price_move: 0.10    # Reject if price moved > 10% from reference
    stale_quote_seconds: 60 # Reject if quote older than 60s

docs.title: Circuit Breakers
docs.text: Automatic trading halts when thresholds are breached.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  circuit_breakers:
    enabled: true

    triggers:
      - name: daily_loss
        type: loss
        threshold: 0.02     # 2% daily loss
        window: 1d
        action: halt_trading

      - name: rapid_loss
        type: loss
        threshold: 0.01     # 1% loss
        window: 1h          # In 1 hour
        action: halt_trading

      - name: drawdown
        type: drawdown
        threshold: 0.10     # 10% drawdown
        action: halt_trading

      - name: consecutive_losses
        type: streak
        losses: 5           # 5 consecutive losing trades
        action: pause_30min

      - name: volatility_spike
        type: volatility
        threshold: 3.0      # 3x normal volatility
        action: reduce_exposure

    # Recovery
    recovery:
      auto_resume: false    # Require manual intervention
      cooldown_minutes: 30

docs.title: Circuit Breaker Actions
docs.text: Action Description halt_trading Stop all trading immediately pause_30min Pause for 30 minutes reduce_exposure Reduce positions by 50% flatten Close all positions alert_only Send alert, continue trading

docs.title: Manual Override
docs.text:
Bash
# Check circuit breaker status
sigc safety status

# Reset circuit breaker (after investigation)
sigc safety reset --breaker daily_loss

# Force halt
sigc safety halt

# Resume trading
sigc safety resume

docs.title: Size Validation
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  orders:
    max_order_value: 100000     # Max $100K per order
    max_order_pct: 0.03         # Max 3% of portfolio
    min_order_value: 100        # Min $100 per order

docs.title: Price Validation
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  orders:
    max_slippage_bps: 50        # Reject if execution > 50 bps from limit
    require_limit_price: true    # No market orders
    limit_price_offset_max: 0.01 # Limit within 1% of market

docs.title: Duplicate Detection
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  orders:
    duplicate_window_seconds: 5  # Block duplicate orders within 5s
    max_orders_per_minute: 20    # Rate limit

docs.title: Freshness Checks
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  data:
    max_stale_hours: 2          # Data must be < 2 hours old
    require_complete: true       # All expected symbols present
    min_data_points: 252         # At least 1 year of history

docs.title: Anomaly Detection
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  data:
    anomaly_detection:
      enabled: true
      max_return_1d: 0.50       # Flag returns > 50%
      max_volume_ratio: 10      # Flag volume > 10x average
      check_gaps: true          # Flag missing data

docs.title: Action on Data Issues
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  on_data_error: use_cache      # use_cache | hold | halt
  on_stale_data: alert          # alert | use_cache | halt
  on_missing_symbol: exclude    # exclude | halt

docs.title: Sanity Checks
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  signals:
    max_turnover: 1.0           # Reject if turnover > 100%
    max_position_change: 0.10   # No single position change > 10%
    require_neutral: true       # Must be dollar neutral

docs.title: Anomaly Detection
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  signals:
    anomaly_detection:
      enabled: true
      historical_comparison: true
      max_deviation_sigma: 3    # Flag if signal > 3σ from history

docs.title: Kill Switch
docs.text: Emergency stop for all trading:

docs.title: Manual Kill Switch
docs.text:
Bash
sigc kill

This immediately:

  1. Cancels all open orders
  2. Halts new order submission
  3. Sends alert to all channels
  4. Requires manual restart

docs.title: Automatic Kill Switch
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  kill_switch:
    # External kill file
    file: /var/run/sigc/kill

    # Network-based kill
    endpoint: https://kill-switch.example.com/sigc
    check_interval_seconds: 10

Create kill file to stop:

Bash
touch /var/run/sigc/kill

Remove to resume:

Bash
rm /var/run/sigc/kill
sigc safety resume

docs.title: Automatic Reconciliation
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  reconciliation:
    enabled: true
    frequency: "*/5 * * * *"    # Every 5 minutes
    tolerance_pct: 0.01         # 1% tolerance
    on_mismatch: alert          # alert | halt | sync

sigc reconcile

docs.title: Real-time Risk
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  risk:
    monitor_interval_seconds: 60
    metrics:
      - daily_var_95
      - portfolio_beta
      - sector_exposure
      - concentration

docs.title: Risk Alerts

YAML
safety:

      - metric: portfolio_beta

docs.title: 1. Start Conservative

safety:

sigc safety test --breaker daily_loss

docs.title: Scheduling
docs.text: Automate signal generation and trading with cron-based scheduling.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
sigc scheduling:

  • Cron expressions for flexibility
  • Trading calendar awareness
  • Market hours handling
  • Timezone support

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  timezone: America/New_York

  jobs:
    - name: morning_rebalance
      cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"          # 9 AM weekdays
      strategy: momentum_strategy

docs.title: Common Schedules
docs.text: Schedule Cron Description Daily 9 AM 0 9 * * * Every day at 9 AM Weekdays 9 AM 0 9 * * 1-5 Mon-Fri at 9 AM Hourly 0 * * * * Every hour Every 15 min */15 * * * * Every 15 minutes Monday 9 AM 0 9 * * 1 Weekly on Monday First of month 0 9 1 * * Monthly on 1st

docs.title: NYSE Calendar
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  calendar: nyse
  skip_holidays: true
  skip_early_close: false

  jobs:
    - name: rebalance
      cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
      strategy: momentum
      # Automatically skips NYSE holidays

docs.title: Custom Calendar
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  calendar: custom
  holidays_file: /etc/sigc/holidays.yaml

  # Or inline
  holidays:
    - "2024-01-01"  # New Year's Day
    - "2024-01-15"  # MLK Day
    - "2024-02-19"  # Presidents' Day
    # ...

docs.title: Market Hours
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  market_hours:
    open: "09:30"
    close: "16:00"
    timezone: America/New_York

  jobs:
    - name: intraday_signal
      cron: "*/30 9-15 * * 1-5"  # Every 30 min during market
      condition: market_open

docs.title: Signal Computation
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: compute_signals
    type: compute
    cron: "0 8 * * 1-5"
    strategy: momentum_strategy

docs.title: Execute Trades
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: execute_trades
    type: execute
    cron: "30 9 * * 1-5"
    strategy: momentum_strategy

docs.title: Compute + Execute
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: full_rebalance
    type: rebalance
    cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
    strategy: momentum_strategy
    # Computes signals, then executes trades

docs.title: Data Refresh
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: refresh_data
    type: data_refresh
    cron: "0 6 * * 1-5"
    source: prices

docs.title: Reconciliation
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: position_check
    type: reconcile
    cron: "0 16 * * 1-5"

docs.title: Custom Script
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: custom_job
    type: script
    cron: "0 17 * * 1-5"
    script: /opt/sigc/scripts/eod_report.sh

docs.title: Market Open
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: intraday_signal
    cron: "*/30 * * * 1-5"
    condition: market_open

docs.title: Data Fresh
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: compute
    cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
    condition: data_fresh
    data_max_age_minutes: 30

docs.title: Previous Job Success
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: compute
    cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"

  - name: execute
    cron: "30 9 * * 1-5"
    depends_on: compute

docs.title: Custom Condition
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: low_vol_trade
    cron: "0 10 * * 1-5"
    condition:
      type: metric
      expr: "vix < 20"

docs.title: Time Window
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: rebalance
    cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
    execution:
      window_minutes: 30
      algorithm: twap

docs.title: Retry Policy
docs.text:
YAML
jobs:
  - name: rebalance
    cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
    retry:
      max_attempts: 3
      delay_minutes: 5
      backoff: exponential

docs.title: View Schedule
docs.text:
Bash
sigc schedule list
Text Only
Scheduled Jobs:
NAME               | CRON           | NEXT RUN            | STATUS
-------------------+----------------+---------------------+--------
morning_rebalance  | 0 9 * * 1-5   | 2024-01-16 09:00:00| enabled
eod_reconcile      | 0 16 * * 1-5  | 2024-01-15 16:00:00| enabled
data_refresh       | 0 6 * * 1-5   | 2024-01-16 06:00:00| enabled

docs.title: Enable/Disable Jobs
docs.text:
Bash
# Disable a job
sigc schedule disable morning_rebalance

# Enable a job
sigc schedule enable morning_rebalance

# Disable all
sigc schedule pause

# Enable all
sigc schedule resume

docs.title: Run Immediately
docs.text:
Bash
# Run job now (outside schedule)
sigc schedule run morning_rebalance

# Dry run
sigc schedule run morning_rebalance --dry-run

docs.title: View History
docs.text:
Bash
sigc schedule history morning_rebalance
Text Only
Job History: morning_rebalance

TIMESTAMP            | STATUS  | DURATION | DETAILS
---------------------+---------+----------+---------
2024-01-15 09:00:00 | success | 2.34s    | 80 positions
2024-01-12 09:00:00 | success | 2.51s    | 82 positions
2024-01-11 09:00:00 | success | 2.28s    | 79 positions
2024-01-10 09:00:00 | failed  | 0.85s    | Data error
2024-01-09 09:00:00 | success | 2.45s    | 81 positions

docs.title: Separate Schedules
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  jobs:
    - name: momentum_rebal
      cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
      strategy: momentum

    - name: value_rebal
      cron: "0 9 1 * *"        # Monthly
      strategy: value

    - name: intraday_signal
      cron: "*/30 9-15 * * 1-5"
      strategy: mean_reversion

docs.title: Coordinated Execution
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  jobs:
    # Compute all signals first
    - name: compute_all
      cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
      type: compute
      strategies: [momentum, value, mean_reversion]

    # Then execute in order
    - name: execute_momentum
      cron: "15 9 * * 1-5"
      type: execute
      strategy: momentum
      depends_on: compute_all

    - name: execute_value
      cron: "20 9 * * 1-5"
      type: execute
      strategy: value
      depends_on: compute_all

docs.title: Job Notifications
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  notifications:
    on_start: false
    on_success: true
    on_failure: true
    channels: [slack]

  jobs:
    - name: rebalance
      cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
      notifications:
        on_success: slack
        on_failure: [slack, pagerduty]

docs.title: Schedule Metrics
docs.text:
Text Only
sigc_schedule_runs_total{job="morning_rebalance",status="success"} 152
sigc_schedule_runs_total{job="morning_rebalance",status="failed"} 3
sigc_schedule_last_run_timestamp{job="morning_rebalance"} 1705320000
sigc_schedule_next_run_timestamp{job="morning_rebalance"} 1705406400
sigc_schedule_duration_seconds{job="morning_rebalance"} 2.34

docs.title: 1. Schedule Before Market Open
docs.text:
YAML
# Good: Compute before market opens
- name: compute
  cron: "0 8 * * 1-5"     # 8 AM

# Execute at open
- name: execute
  cron: "30 9 * * 1-5"    # 9:30 AM
  depends_on: compute

docs.title: 2. Use Trading Calendar
docs.text:
YAML
schedule:
  calendar: nyse
  skip_holidays: true     # Don't run on holidays

docs.title: 3. Add Retry Logic
docs.text:
YAML
retry:
  max_attempts: 3
  delay_minutes: 5

docs.title: 4. Monitor Job Health
docs.text:
Set up alerts for failed jobs:

YAML
alerting:
  rules:
    - name: schedule_failure
      condition: "job_status == 'failed'"
      severity: high

docs.title: 5. Test Schedule Changes
docs.text:
Bash
# Preview next N runs
sigc schedule preview --runs 10

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:

  • Monitoring - Track schedule execution
  • Alerting - Notifications on failure

docs.title: What is Quantitative Trading?
docs.text:
Using mathematical models to identify trading opportunities:

Text Only
Data → Analysis → Signal → Portfolio → Execution

docs.title: The sigc Approach
docs.text:
Text Only
// 1. Load data
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

// 2. Compute signal
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

// 3. Construct portfolio
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Why Systematic Trading?
docs.text: Aspect Discretionary Systematic Emotions Affected Removed Consistency Variable Consistent Scalability Limited High Backtesting Difficult Easy Speed Slow Fast

docs.text: Tool Purpose sigc Strategy development Text editor/VSCode Code editing Terminal Running sigc Sample data Testing strategies

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Bash
# Install sigc
cargo install sigc

# Create first strategy
cat > momentum.sig << 'EOF'
data:
  source = "prices.csv"
  format = csv
  columns:
    date: Date
    ticker: Symbol
    close: Numeric as prices

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31
EOF

# Run backtest
sigc run momentum.sig

docs.title: What You'll Build
docs.text:
By the end of this guide, you'll be able to:

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Start with Chapter 1: Introduction to begin your quant trading journey.

docs.title: Chapter 1: Introduction to Quantitative Trading
docs.text: Welcome to the world of systematic trading.

docs.title: What is Quantitative Trading?
docs.text:
Quantitative trading uses mathematical models and computer programs to identify and execute trading opportunities:

Text Only
Data → Analysis → Signal → Decision → Execution

Instead of relying on intuition or gut feelings, quant traders:

  • Analyze historical data
  • Build mathematical models
  • Test strategies rigorously
  • Execute systematically

docs.title: Systematic vs. Discretionary
docs.text: Aspect Discretionary Systematic Decision Making Human judgment Rules-based Emotions Can interfere Removed Consistency Variable Consistent Scalability Limited High Speed Slow Fast Backtesting Difficult Easy

docs.title: 1. Statistical Arbitrage
docs.text:
Exploiting price relationships between related securities:

Text Only
signal pairs_trade:
  // Trade the spread between related stocks
  spread = prices[GOOGL] - beta * prices[META]
  zscore = (spread - mean(spread)) / std(spread)
  emit -zscore

docs.title: 2. Factor Investing
docs.text:
Systematic exposure to return drivers:

Text Only
signal multi_factor:
  momentum = zscore(ret(prices, 60))
  value = zscore(book_to_market)
  quality = zscore(roe)
  emit 0.4 * momentum + 0.3 * value + 0.3 * quality

docs.title: 3. Trend Following
docs.text:
Riding market trends:

Text Only
signal trend:
  ma_fast = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  ma_slow = rolling_mean(prices, 60)
  emit zscore(ma_fast - ma_slow)

docs.title: 4. Mean Reversion
docs.text:
Betting on prices returning to normal:

Text Only
signal mean_reversion:
  z = (prices - rolling_mean(prices, 20)) / rolling_std(prices, 20)
  emit -z  // Buy oversold, short overbought

docs.title: 5. Market Making
docs.text:
Providing liquidity for a profit:

Text Only
Not covered in this guide (requires low-latency infrastructure)

docs.title: 1. Research
docs.text:
• Generate ideas from academic papers, market observations
  • Formalize hypotheses mathematically
  • Initial data exploration

docs.title: 2. Signal Development
docs.text:
Text Only
// Transform ideas into computable signals
signal my_idea:
  // Mathematical implementation
  emit zscore(my_calculation)

docs.title: 3. Backtesting
docs.text:
Text Only
// Test on historical data
portfolio test:
  weights = rank(my_idea).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 4. Validation
docs.text:
• Out-of-sample testing
  • Walk-forward analysis
  • Parameter stability

docs.title: 5. Production
docs.text:
• Deploy to live trading
  • Monitor performance
  • Manage risk

docs.title: Why Use sigc?
docs.text: sigc provides a complete platform for quantitative trading:

docs.title: Domain-Specific Language
docs.text:
Write strategies naturally:

Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

docs.title: Rigorous Backtesting
docs.text:
Test with realistic assumptions:

Text Only

  weights = ...
  costs = tc.bps(10)  // Transaction costs
  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Deploy the same code to live trading:

Bash
sigc daemon start --strategy momentum.sig --live

Numerical scores indicating expected returns:

Text Only

  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

docs.title: Portfolios

Converting signals to positions:

  weights = rank(alpha).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

docs.title: Install sigc

Bash
cargo install sigc

docs.title: Your First Strategy
docs.text:
Text Only
// Save as first_strategy.sig
data:
  source = "prices.csv"
  format = csv
  columns:
    date: Date
    ticker: Symbol
    close: Numeric as prices

signal momentum:

Bash

docs.title: Next Chapter
docs.text: Continue to Chapter 2: Fundamentals to learn about returns, volatility, and other core concepts.

docs.title: Chapter 2: Financial Fundamentals
docs.text: Core concepts every quant trader must understand.

docs.title: Simple Returns
docs.text:
The percentage change in price:

\[R_t = \frac{P_t - P_{t-1}}{P_{t-1}} = \frac{P_t}{P_{t-1}} - 1\] Text Only
signal simple_return:
  ret_1d = (prices - lag(prices, 1)) / lag(prices, 1)
  emit ret_1d

docs.title: Log Returns
docs.text:
Natural logarithm of price ratio:

\[r_t = \ln\left(\frac{P_t}{P_{t-1}}\right)\] Text Only
signal log_return:
  ret_1d = ln(prices / lag(prices, 1))
  emit ret_1d

docs.title: Why Log Returns?
docs.text:
Property Simple Log Additive over time No Yes Symmetric No Yes Normal distribution Less More Multi-period Multiply Add

sigc default: Text Only

// ret() uses simple returns by default
ret_60 = ret(prices, 60)  // 60-day simple return

docs.title: Annualization
docs.text:
Convert daily to annual:

Text Only
// Volatility: multiply by sqrt(252)
annual_vol = daily_vol * sqrt(252)

// Returns: multiply by 252
annual_ret = daily_ret * 252

docs.title: Standard Deviation
docs.text:
Most common volatility measure:

\[\sigma = \sqrt{\frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{i=1}^n(r_i - \bar{r})^2}\] Text Only
signal volatility:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  vol = rolling_std(daily_ret, 60) * sqrt(252)  // Annualized
  emit vol

docs.title: Realized Volatility
docs.text:
Historical volatility from actual returns:

Text Only
signal realized_vol:
  // Sum of squared returns
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  squared_ret = daily_ret * daily_ret
  rv = sqrt(rolling_sum(squared_ret, 21) * 252 / 21)
  emit rv

docs.title: Implied Volatility
docs.text: Forward-looking volatility from options prices. Often proxied by VIX.

docs.title: Sharpe Ratio
docs.text:
Return per unit of risk:

\[\text{Sharpe} = \frac{R_p - R_f}{\sigma_p}\] Text Only
// In backtest results
sharpe = annual_return / annual_volatility

docs.title: Information Ratio
docs.text:
Active return per unit of active risk:

\[\text{IR} = \frac{R_p - R_b}{\sigma_{p-b}}\] Text Only
// Return vs benchmark, divided by tracking error
information_ratio = active_return / tracking_error

docs.title: Sortino Ratio
docs.text:
Uses downside deviation instead of total volatility:

\[\text{Sortino} = \frac{R_p - R_f}{\sigma_d}\]

More appropriate when return distribution is asymmetric.

docs.title: Pearson Correlation
docs.text:
Linear relationship between two variables:

\[\rho_{xy} = \frac{\text{Cov}(x,y)}{\sigma_x \sigma_y}\] Text Only
signal correlation:
  ret_aapl = ret(prices[AAPL], 1)
  ret_msft = ret(prices[MSFT], 1)
  corr = rolling_corr(ret_aapl, ret_msft, 60)
  emit corr

docs.title: Why Correlation Matters
docs.text:
• Diversification: Low correlation = better diversification
  • Risk: High correlation in crisis = increased risk
  • Factor exposure: Correlation reveals common factors

docs.title: Maximum Drawdown
docs.text:
Largest peak-to-trough decline:

\[\text{MaxDD} = \max_t\left(\frac{\text{Peak}_t - P_t}{\text{Peak}_t}\right)\] Text Only
signal drawdown:
  peak = rolling_max(prices, 252)
  dd = (peak - prices) / peak
  emit dd

docs.title: Drawdown Duration
docs.text: Time underwater: - Drawdown start: When price falls from peak - Drawdown end: When new peak is reached - Recovery time: Duration of drawdown

docs.title: Beta
docs.text:
Sensitivity to market movements:

\[\beta = \frac{\text{Cov}(R_i, R_m)}{\text{Var}(R_m)}\] Text Only
signal beta:
  stock_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  market_ret = ret(market_index, 1)
  beta = rolling_cov(stock_ret, market_ret, 60) / rolling_var(market_ret, 60)
  emit beta

docs.title: Alpha
docs.text:
Return unexplained by market:

\[\alpha = R_i - \beta \cdot R_m\]

docs.title: CAPM
docs.text:
\[E[R_i] = R_f + \beta_i(E[R_m] - R_f)\]

Expected return = Risk-free rate + Beta × Market risk premium

docs.title: Z-Score
docs.text:
Standardized value (number of standard deviations from mean):

\[z = \frac{x - \mu}{\sigma}\] Text Only
signal momentum_zscore:
  raw_momentum = ret(prices, 60)
  // zscore() cross-sectionally normalizes
  emit zscore(raw_momentum)

docs.title: Percentile Rank
docs.text:
Position in distribution:

Text Only
signal percentile:
  // Rank relative to history
  percentile = ts_rank(prices, 252) / 252
  emit percentile

docs.title: Cross-Sectional vs Time-Series
docs.text:
Operation Cross-Sectional Time-Series zscore Across assets today Single asset over time rank Across assets today Single asset over time mean Average of all assets Average over time Text Only
// Cross-sectional (default)
cs_zscore = zscore(momentum)

// Time-series
ts_zscore = (momentum - rolling_mean(momentum, 252)) / rolling_std(momentum, 252)

docs.title: Computing Key Metrics
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

signal returns:
  emit ret(prices, 1)

signal volatility:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  emit rolling_std(daily_ret, 60) * sqrt(252)

signal sharpe_estimate:
  daily_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  annual_ret = rolling_mean(daily_ret, 252) * 252
  annual_vol = rolling_std(daily_ret, 252) * sqrt(252)
  emit annual_ret / annual_vol

signal drawdown:
  peak = rolling_max(prices, 252)
  emit (peak - prices) / peak

signal beta:
  stock_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  market_ret = ret(market, 1)
  emit rolling_cov(stock_ret, market_ret, 60) / rolling_var(market_ret, 60)

docs.title: Key Takeaways
docs.text:
1. Returns: Use simple returns for most cases, log returns for compounding
  2. Volatility: Annualize daily vol by multiplying by √252
  3. Sharpe Ratio: Primary measure of risk-adjusted performance
  4. Correlation: Critical for portfolio construction
  5. Drawdowns: Maximum drawdown shows worst-case scenario
  6. Z-scores: Standardize signals for comparison

docs.title: Exercises
docs.text:
1. Calculate the 60-day rolling Sharpe ratio for a stock
  2. Compute the correlation between two stocks
  3. Find the maximum drawdown in a price series
  4. Calculate beta relative to SPY

docs.title: Next Chapter
docs.text: Continue to Chapter 3: Building Signals to learn how to construct trading signals.

docs.title: Chapter 3: Building Trading Signals
docs.text: Transform data into actionable trading signals.

docs.title: What is a Signal?
docs.text:
A signal is a numerical score that predicts expected returns:

  • Higher score → expect higher returns → buy more
  • Lower score → expect lower returns → sell/short
Text Only
signal momentum:
  // Stocks with higher past returns get higher scores
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

docs.title: Good Signals Have:
docs.text: Property Description Example Predictive Forecasts future returns High momentum → high future returns Stable Doesn't change too rapidly Monthly, not minute-by-minute Diversified Works across many assets Not just one stock Economically sensible Has logical explanation "Winners keep winning"

docs.title: Signal Quality Metrics
docs.text:
Information Coefficient (IC): Correlation between signal and forward returns.

Text Only
IC = 0.05 → Good signal
IC = 0.10 → Excellent signal
IC > 0.15 → Suspicious (check for errors)

docs.title: Step 1: Start with a Hypothesis
docs.text: "Stocks with strong recent performance continue to outperform."

docs.title: Step 2: Implement Mathematically
Step 3: Test and Validate
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio test_momentum:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Momentum Signals
docs.text:
Past returns predict future returns:

Text Only
// Simple momentum
signal momentum_simple:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

// 12-1 momentum (skip last month)
signal momentum_12_1:
  ret_12m = ret(prices, 252)
  ret_1m = ret(prices, 21)
  emit zscore(ret_12m - ret_1m)

// Relative strength
signal relative_strength:
  stock_ret = ret(prices, 60)
  market_ret = ret(market, 60)
  emit zscore(stock_ret - market_ret)

docs.title: Value Signals
docs.text:
Cheap stocks outperform:

Text Only
// Book-to-market
signal value_btm:
  emit zscore(book_value / market_cap)

// Earnings yield
signal value_ey:
  emit zscore(earnings / prices)

// Combined value
signal value_composite:
  btm = zscore(book_value / market_cap)
  ey = zscore(earnings / prices)
  emit 0.5 * btm + 0.5 * ey

docs.title: Quality Signals
docs.text:
High-quality companies outperform:

Text Only
// Profitability
signal quality_roe:
  emit zscore(net_income / equity)

// Earnings stability
signal quality_stability:
  emit -zscore(rolling_std(earnings, 8))

// Combined quality
signal quality_composite:
  roe = zscore(net_income / equity)
  stability = -zscore(rolling_std(earnings, 8))
  emit 0.6 * roe + 0.4 * stability

docs.title: Low Volatility Signals
docs.text:
Low-risk stocks outperform (risk-adjusted):

Text Only
// Inverse volatility
signal low_vol:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60) * sqrt(252)
  emit -zscore(vol)

// Beta
signal low_beta:
  stock_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  market_ret = ret(market, 1)
  beta = rolling_cov(stock_ret, market_ret, 60) / rolling_var(market_ret, 60)
  emit -zscore(beta)

docs.title: Mean Reversion Signals
docs.text:
Extreme moves revert:

Text Only
// Price vs moving average
signal mean_reversion:
  ma = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  deviation = (prices - ma) / ma
  emit -zscore(deviation)

// RSI-based
signal rsi_reversion:
  change = diff(prices, 1)
  gains = where(change > 0, change, 0)
  losses = where(change < 0, -change, 0)
  rs = ema(gains, 14) / (ema(losses, 14) + 0.0001)
  rsi = 100 - 100 / (1 + rs)
  emit zscore(50 - rsi)

docs.title: Standardization
docs.text:
Make signals comparable:

Text Only
// Z-score (mean=0, std=1)
signal standardized:
  emit zscore(raw_signal)

// Rank (uniform distribution)
signal ranked:
  emit rank(raw_signal) / count(raw_signal)

docs.title: Winsorization
docs.text:
Handle outliers:

Text Only
signal winsorized:
  lower = quantile(raw_signal, 0.01)
  upper = quantile(raw_signal, 0.99)
  emit clip(raw_signal, lower, upper)

docs.title: Sector Neutralization
docs.text:
Remove sector bias:

Text Only
signal sector_neutral:
  // Demean within each sector
  emit neutralize(raw_signal, by=sectors)

docs.title: Smoothing
docs.text:
Reduce noise:

Text Only
signal smoothed:
  // Exponential moving average of signal
  emit ema(raw_signal, 5)

docs.title: Simple Average
docs.text:
Text Only
signal combined_avg:
  emit (momentum + value + quality) / 3

docs.title: Weighted Average
docs.text:
Text Only
signal combined_weighted:
  emit 0.4 * momentum + 0.4 * value + 0.2 * quality

docs.title: IC-Weighted
docs.text:
Weight by predictive power:

Text Only
signal combined_ic:
  // Weights based on historical IC
  ic_mom = 0.04
  ic_val = 0.03
  ic_qual = 0.02
  total = ic_mom + ic_val + ic_qual

  emit (ic_mom * momentum + ic_val * value + ic_qual * quality) / total

docs.title: Rank Then Average
docs.text:
Text Only
signal combined_rank:
  r_mom = rank(momentum)
  r_val = rank(value)
  r_qual = rank(quality)
  emit (r_mom + r_val + r_qual) / 3

docs.title: Signal Decay
docs.text:
Signals lose predictive power over time:

Text Only
Day 1: IC = 0.05
Day 5: IC = 0.04
Day 21: IC = 0.03
Day 63: IC = 0.01

docs.title: Managing Decay
docs.text:
Text Only
// More frequent rebalancing
portfolio fast_decay:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=5 from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31  // Every 5 days

// Less frequent for slow decay
portfolio slow_decay:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=21 from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31  // Monthly

docs.title: Multi-Factor Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Individual factors
signal momentum:
  ret_12m = ret(prices, 252)
  ret_1m = ret(prices, 21)
  emit neutralize(zscore(ret_12m - ret_1m), by=sectors)

signal value:
  btm = zscore(book_value / market_cap)
  ey = zscore(earnings / prices)
  raw = 0.5 * btm + 0.5 * ey
  emit neutralize(raw, by=sectors)

signal quality:
  prof = zscore(net_income / equity)
  stab = -zscore(rolling_std(earnings, 8))
  raw = 0.6 * prof + 0.4 * stab
  emit neutralize(raw, by=sectors)

signal low_vol:
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60) * sqrt(252)
  emit neutralize(-zscore(vol), by=sectors)

// Combined signal
signal multi_factor:
  emit 0.30 * momentum + 0.30 * value + 0.25 * quality + 0.15 * low_vol

// Portfolio
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(multi_factor).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)

  constraints:
    max_sector = 0.20

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 1. Start Simple
docs.text:
Text Only
// Start here
signal simple:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

// Not here
signal complex:
  emit complicated_formula(many, parameters, here)

docs.title: 2. Use Economic Intuition
docs.text: Ask: "Why should this work?"

docs.title: 3. Normalize Everything
docs.text:
Text Only
// Always standardize
emit zscore(raw_signal)

// Or rank
emit rank(raw_signal)

docs.title: 4. Sector Neutralize
docs.text:
Text Only
// Avoid unintended sector bets
emit neutralize(signal, by=sectors)

docs.title: 5. Test Out-of-Sample
docs.text: Use walk-forward validation to avoid overfitting.

docs.title: Exercises
docs.text:
1. Build a momentum signal using 12-1 month returns
  2. Create a value signal combining book-to-market and earnings yield
  3. Combine momentum and value with equal weights
  4. Sector-neutralize your combined signal

docs.title: Chapter 4: The sigc Language
docs.text: Master the sigc Domain-Specific Language (DSL).

docs.title: Language Overview
docs.text:
sigc uses a purpose-built DSL for trading strategies:

Text Only
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Program Structure
docs.text:
Every sigc program has three main sections:

Text Only
// 1. DATA: Where to get data
data:
  ...

// 2. SIGNALS: How to compute scores
signal name:
  ...

// 3. PORTFOLIO: How to construct positions
portfolio name:
  ...

docs.title: Basic Data Loading
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices.csv"
  format = csv
  columns:
    date: Date
    ticker: Symbol
    close: Numeric as prices
    volume: Numeric as volume

docs.title: Parquet (Recommended)
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

docs.title: Multiple Data Sources
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  prices = "prices.parquet"
  fundamentals = "fundamentals.parquet"
  factors = "factors.parquet"

docs.title: S3 Data
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "s3://mybucket/data/prices.parquet"
  format = parquet

docs.title: Basic Signal
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  ret_60 = ret(prices, 60)
  z = zscore(ret_60)
  emit z

docs.title: Signal with Intermediate Calculations
Using Other Signals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

signal value:
  emit zscore(book_to_market)

signal combined:
  // Reference other signals
  emit 0.5 * momentum + 0.5 * value

docs.title: Arithmetic
docs.text:
Text Only
signal math_examples:
  // Basic arithmetic
  sum = a + b
  diff = a - b
  prod = a * b
  quot = a / b

  // With constants
  scaled = prices * 100
  shifted = prices + 10

  // Compound expressions
  result = (a + b) / (c - d)

  emit result

docs.title: Comparisons
docs.text:
Text Only
signal compare_examples:
  // Comparisons (return boolean)
  above_100 = prices > 100
  below_ma = prices < rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  equals = a == b
  not_equals = a != b

  emit above_100

docs.title: Conditionals
docs.text:
Text Only
signal conditional_examples:
  // where(condition, true_value, false_value)
  signal = where(prices > ma, 1, -1)

  // Nested
  signal2 = where(a > 0, 1,
            where(a < 0, -1, 0))

  emit signal

docs.title: Logical Operations
docs.text:
Text Only
signal logical_examples:
  // and, or, not
  both = condition1 and condition2
  either = condition1 or condition2
  neither = not(condition1)

  // Combine
  signal = where(bull and low_vol, momentum, defensive)

  emit signal

docs.title: Time-Series Functions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal ts_examples:
  // Returns
  ret_1d = ret(prices, 1)
  ret_60d = ret(prices, 60)

  // Lags
  prev_price = lag(prices, 1)
  prev_week = lag(prices, 5)

  // Differences
  change = diff(prices, 1)

  // Rolling statistics
  ma_20 = rolling_mean(prices, 20)
  std_60 = rolling_std(prices, 60)
  sum_5 = rolling_sum(volume, 5)
  max_252 = rolling_max(prices, 252)
  min_20 = rolling_min(prices, 20)

  emit ma_20

docs.title: Cross-Sectional Functions
Mathematical Functions
docs.text:
Text Only
signal math_functions:
  // Basic math
  absolute = abs(returns)
  square_root = sqrt(variance)
  natural_log = ln(prices)
  exponential = exp(log_returns)
  power = pow(base, 2)

  // Trigonometric (rare in finance)
  sine = sin(x)
  cosine = cos(x)

  // Rounding
  rounded = round(value, 2)
  floored = floor(value)
  ceiling = ceil(value)

  emit absolute

docs.title: Statistical Functions
Basic Portfolio
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: With Constraints
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio constrained:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)

  constraints:
    gross_exposure = 2.0
    net_exposure = 0.0
    max_sector = 0.20
    max_position = 0.03

  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: With Costs
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio realistic:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  costs = tc.bps(10)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Rebalancing Frequency
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio monthly:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=21 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31  // Monthly

portfolio weekly:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=5 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31  // Weekly

docs.title: Long-Short
docs.text:
Text Only
// Equal-weighted long/short
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

// With position cap
weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2, cap=0.03)

docs.title: Long-Only
docs.text:
Text Only
// Top 20% only
weights = rank(signal).long_only(top=0.2)

// With cap
weights = rank(signal).long_only(top=0.2, cap=0.05)

docs.title: Signal-Weighted
docs.text:
Text Only
// Weights proportional to signal
weights = signal.normalize()

// Scaled to target exposure
weights = signal.normalize().scale(gross=2.0)

docs.title: Defining Parameters
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback: 60
  top_pct: 0.2
  cap: 0.03

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, lookback))

portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=top_pct, bottom=top_pct, cap=cap)

docs.title: Parameter Ranges (for Optimization)
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback: range(20, 120, 20)   // 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120
  top_pct: range(0.1, 0.3, 0.05) // 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3
  rebal: [5, 10, 21]             // Explicit list

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
Text Only
// Single line comment

/*
  Multi-line
  comment
*/

signal momentum:
  // Explain your logic
  ret_60 = ret(prices, 60)  // 60-day return
  emit zscore(ret_60)

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Text Only
/*
  Multi-Factor Strategy
  Combines momentum, value, and quality factors
*/

// Data configuration
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Parameters
params:
  mom_lookback: 60
  value_weight: 0.3
  quality_weight: 0.3
  top_bottom: 0.2

// Individual factors
signal momentum:
  // 60-day momentum, sector-neutralized
  raw = ret(prices, mom_lookback)
  emit neutralize(zscore(raw), by=sectors)

signal value:
  // Book-to-market ratio
  btm = book_value / market_cap
  emit neutralize(zscore(btm), by=sectors)

signal quality:
  // Return on equity
  roe = net_income / equity
  emit neutralize(zscore(roe), by=sectors)

// Combined signal
signal multi_factor:
  mom_weight = 1 - value_weight - quality_weight
  combined = mom_weight * momentum + value_weight * value + quality_weight * quality
  emit combined

// Portfolio construction
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(multi_factor).long_short(
    top = top_bottom,
    bottom = top_bottom,
    cap = 0.03
  )

  constraints:
    gross_exposure = 2.0
    net_exposure: [-0.1, 0.1]
    max_sector = 0.20

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest rebal=21 from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: 1. Use Meaningful Names
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good
signal momentum_12_1:
signal value_btm:

// Bad
signal s1:
signal x:

docs.title: 2. Break Down Complex Logic
docs.text:
Text Only
// Good
signal complex:
  step1 = calculation1
  step2 = calculation2(step1)
  step3 = calculation3(step2)
  emit step3

// Bad
signal complex:
  emit calculation3(calculation2(calculation1))

docs.title: 3. Comment Your Intent
docs.text:
Text Only
signal adaptive:
  // Use momentum in bull markets, mean reversion in bear
  regime = ma_50 > ma_200
  emit where(regime, momentum, mean_reversion)

docs.title: Exercises
docs.text:
1. Write a signal that computes RSI (Relative Strength Index)
  2. Create a combined signal with momentum, value, and size factors
  3. Build a portfolio with sector constraints
  4. Parameterize your strategy for optimization

docs.title: Next Chapter
docs.text: Continue to Chapter 5: Backtesting to learn proper strategy validation.

docs.title: Chapter 5: Backtesting
docs.text: Validate strategies properly with historical simulation.

docs.title: What is Backtesting?
docs.text:
Backtesting simulates how a strategy would have performed historically:

Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Output: Text Only

Sharpe: 0.85
Return: 8.2% annual
Max Drawdown: -15.3%

docs.title: Validates Ideas
docs.text: Before risking real money: - Does the signal work historically? - How does it behave in different regimes? - What's the worst-case scenario?

docs.title: Identifies Issues
docs.text:
• Implementation errors
  • Data problems
  • Unrealistic assumptions

docs.title: The Backtest Loop
docs.text:
Text Only
For each trading day:
  1. Observe today's data (NOT future)
  2. Compute signals
  3. Generate target weights
  4. Execute trades (with realistic costs)
  5. Mark-to-market
  6. Record performance

docs.title: Key Principles
docs.text:
1. No look-ahead bias: Only use data available at decision time
  2. Realistic execution: Include transaction costs
  3. Survivorship-free data: Include delisted stocks
  4. Point-in-time data: Use data as it was known then

docs.title: Simple Backtest
docs.text:
Text Only
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio simple:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: With Rebalancing
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio monthly:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest rebal=21 from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31  // Rebalance monthly

docs.title: With Transaction Costs
docs.text:
Text Only
portfolio realistic:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  costs = tc.bps(10)  // 10 basis points round-trip
  backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Return Metrics
docs.text: Metric Description Good Value Total Return Cumulative return >50% Annual Return Annualized return >5% Excess Return Return vs benchmark >0%

docs.title: Risk Metrics
docs.text: Metric Description Good Value Volatility Annualized std dev <15% Max Drawdown Largest peak-to-trough <20% VaR (95%) Value at Risk <2% daily

docs.title: Risk-Adjusted Metrics
docs.text: Metric Formula Good Value Sharpe Ratio Return / Vol >0.5 Sortino Ratio Return / Downside Vol >1.0 Calmar Ratio Return / Max DD >0.5 Information Ratio Active Return / TE >0.5

docs.title: Interpreting Results
docs.text:
Text Only
Backtest Results
================
Total Return: 85%
Annual Return: 8.2%
Annual Volatility: 9.6%
Sharpe Ratio: 0.85

Max Drawdown: -15.3%
Avg Drawdown: -4.2%
Drawdown Duration: 45 days (avg)

Turnover: 320% annual
Win Rate: 54%

docs.title: 1. Look-Ahead Bias
docs.text:
Using information not available at decision time:

Text Only
// WRONG: Using future data
signal bad:
  future_return = ret(prices, -60)  // Future returns!
  emit future_return

// CORRECT: Only past data
signal good:
  past_return = ret(prices, 60)
  emit zscore(past_return)

docs.title: 2. Survivorship Bias
docs.text:
Only testing on stocks that still exist:

Text Only
Full Universe (2015):      1000 stocks
Survivors (to 2024):        800 stocks
Survivorship bias: Testing only on 800 winners

Solution: Use survivorship-free data that includes delisted stocks.

docs.title: 3. Overfitting
docs.text:
Finding patterns that only exist in sample:

Text Only
// High risk of overfitting
params:
  p1: range(1, 100, 1)
  p2: range(1, 100, 1)
  p3: range(1, 100, 1)
  // 1,000,000 combinations tested!

Signs of overfitting: - Perfect in-sample, terrible out-of-sample - Complex, unintuitive parameters - Very different optimal params across periods

docs.title: 4. Ignoring Transaction Costs
docs.text:
Text Only
// Unrealistic: No costs
portfolio no_costs:
  weights = ...
  backtest rebal=1  // Daily rebalancing, no costs
  // Result: 20% annual return! (unrealistic)

// Realistic: Include costs
portfolio with_costs:
  weights = ...
  costs = tc.bps(10)
  backtest rebal=1
  // Result: 2% annual return (costs destroy alpha)

docs.title: 5. Data Mining
docs.text:
Testing many hypotheses until one works:

Text Only
Test 100 random signals
By chance, ~5 will be "significant" (p < 0.05)
These are false positives!

Solution: Multiple testing correction, out-of-sample validation.

docs.title: The Right Way to Test
docs.text:
Text Only
params:
  lookback: range(20, 100, 20)

portfolio validated:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  backtest walk_forward(
    train_years = 5,   // Optimize on 5 years
    test_years = 1     // Test on 1 year (unseen)
  ) from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Text Only
Period 1:
  Train: 2010-2014 → Find optimal params
  Test:  2015      → Evaluate (out-of-sample)

Period 2:
  Train: 2011-2015 → Re-optimize
  Test:  2016      → Evaluate

...

Combined test results = True expected performance

docs.title: Interpreting Walk-Forward Results
docs.text:
Text Only
Walk-Forward Results
====================
In-Sample Sharpe:  1.2
Out-of-Sample Sharpe: 0.65

Degradation: 46%
  • <30% degradation: Robust strategy
  • 30-50% degradation: Some overfitting
  • >50% degradation: Significant overfitting

docs.title: 1. Sensitivity Analysis
2. Subsample Testing
docs.text:
Text Only
// Test on different time periods
backtest from 2010-01-01 to 2014-12-31  // Period 1
backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2019-12-31  // Period 2
backtest from 2020-01-01 to 2024-12-31  // Period 3

// Results should be consistent

docs.title: 3. Universe Variations
docs.text: Test on different asset subsets: - Large cap vs small cap - Different sectors - Different geographies

docs.title: 4. Transaction Cost Sensitivity
docs.text:
Text Only
// Test different cost assumptions
costs = tc.bps(5)   // Optimistic
costs = tc.bps(10)  // Realistic
costs = tc.bps(20)  // Conservative

docs.title: Complete Backtesting Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Parameters for optimization
params:
  mom_lookback: [40, 60, 80]
  value_weight: [0.3, 0.4, 0.5]
  top_pct: [0.15, 0.20, 0.25]

// Signals
signal momentum:
  emit neutralize(zscore(ret(prices, mom_lookback)), by=sectors)

signal value:
  emit neutralize(zscore(book_to_market), by=sectors)

signal combined:
  mom_wt = 1 - value_weight
  emit mom_wt * momentum + value_weight * value

// Portfolio with constraints
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(combined).long_short(
    top = top_pct,
    bottom = top_pct,
    cap = 0.03
  )

  constraints:
    gross_exposure = 2.0
    net_exposure = 0.0
    max_sector = 0.20

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  // Walk-forward validation
  backtest walk_forward(
    train_years = 5,
    test_years = 1,
    metric = "sharpe"
  ) rebal=21 from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Run: Bash

sigc run strategy.sig --walk-forward --verbose

docs.title: Best Practices Checklist
docs.text:
• Use survivorship-free data
  • Include realistic transaction costs
  • Test out-of-sample (walk-forward)
  • Check parameter stability
  • Test across different time periods
  • Verify no look-ahead bias
  • Check for reasonable turnover
  • Compare to benchmark
  • Document assumptions

docs.title: Exercises
docs.text:
1. Backtest a simple momentum strategy with different lookback periods
  2. Add transaction costs and measure the impact
  3. Run walk-forward validation and measure degradation
  4. Test strategy on different time periods

docs.title: Next Chapter
docs.text: Continue to Chapter 6: Risk Management to learn about controlling portfolio risk.

docs.title: Chapter 6: Risk Management
docs.text: Control portfolio risk to survive and thrive.

docs.title: Why Risk Management?
docs.text:
"Rule #1: Never lose money. Rule #2: Never forget rule #1." - Warren Buffett

Even the best signals fail sometimes. Risk management:

  • Limits losses during drawdowns
  • Ensures survival through bad periods
  • Enables consistent compounding

docs.title: Market Risk
docs.text:
Exposure to overall market movements:

Text Only
signal beta:
  stock_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  market_ret = ret(market, 1)
  beta = rolling_cov(stock_ret, market_ret, 60) / rolling_var(market_ret, 60)
  emit beta

docs.title: Specific Risk
docs.text:
Risk unique to individual securities:

Text Only
signal idio_risk:
  // Risk unexplained by market
  residual = stock_returns - beta * market_returns
  idio_vol = rolling_std(residual, 60)
  emit idio_vol

docs.title: Factor Risk
docs.text:
Exposure to common factors (size, value, momentum):

Text Only
// Monitor factor exposures
factor_exposure:
  market_beta: [0.9, 1.1]  // Target range
  size: [-0.1, 0.1]        // Neutral
  value: [0.0, 0.3]        // Slight tilt

docs.title: Concentration Risk
docs.text:
Too much exposure to single positions/sectors:

Text Only
constraints:
  max_position = 0.03   // No more than 3% per stock
  max_sector = 0.20     // No more than 20% per sector

docs.title: Position Sizing
docs.text:
Limit exposure to individual stocks:

Text Only
portfolio main:
  weights = rank(signal).long_short(
    top = 0.2,
    bottom = 0.2,
    cap = 0.03  // Max 3% per position
  )

docs.title: Volatility-Based Sizing
docs.text:
Allocate less to volatile stocks:

Text Only
signal vol_adjusted_weights:
  raw_weight = rank(signal) / count(signal)
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60) * sqrt(252)
  inv_vol = 1 / vol
  adjusted = raw_weight * inv_vol
  emit adjusted / sum(adjusted)  // Normalize

docs.title: Gross Exposure
docs.text:
Total absolute exposure:

Text Only
constraints:
  gross_exposure = 2.0  // 100% long + 100% short

docs.title: Net Exposure
docs.text:
Directional market exposure:

Text Only
constraints:
  net_exposure = 0.0     // Dollar neutral
  // or
  net_exposure: [-0.1, 0.1]  // Small range

docs.title: Sector Constraints
docs.text:
Limit sector concentration:

Text Only
constraints:
  max_sector = 0.20  // Max 20% per sector

docs.title: Turnover Control
docs.text:
Limit trading activity:

Text Only
constraints:
  max_turnover = 0.25  // Max 25% turnover per rebalance

docs.title: Fixed Volatility Target
docs.text:
Scale positions to target volatility:

Text Only
portfolio vol_targeted:
  raw_weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)

  // Target 10% annual volatility
  vol_target = 0.10
  current_vol = portfolio_volatility(raw_weights)
  scale = vol_target / current_vol

  weights = raw_weights * scale

  backtest from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Dynamic Scaling
docs.text:
Reduce exposure in high volatility environments:

Text Only
signal vol_scale:
  current_vol = rolling_std(ret(market, 1), 20) * sqrt(252)
  target_vol = 0.15
  scale = clip(target_vol / current_vol, 0.5, 1.5)
  emit scale

portfolio adaptive:
  base_weights = rank(signal).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  weights = base_weights * vol_scale

docs.title: Maximum Drawdown Monitoring
docs.text:
Track underwater periods:

Text Only
signal drawdown:
  peak = rolling_max(portfolio_value, 252)
  dd = (portfolio_value - peak) / peak
  emit dd

docs.title: Drawdown-Based Deleveraging
docs.text:
Reduce exposure during drawdowns:

Text Only
signal dd_scale:
  drawdown = current_drawdown
  // Scale down as drawdown increases
  scale = where(drawdown > -0.05, 1.0,
          where(drawdown > -0.10, 0.75,
          where(drawdown > -0.15, 0.50, 0.25)))
  emit scale

docs.title: Value at Risk (VaR)
docs.text:
Maximum expected loss at confidence level:

Text Only
VaR(95%) = 1.65 × σ × √days

For a portfolio with 10% annual vol: Text Only

Daily VaR(95%) = 1.65 × (0.10 / √252) = 1.04%

docs.title: Conditional VaR (CVaR)
docs.text:
Expected loss given VaR breach:

Text Only
CVaR(95%) = Expected loss when loss > VaR(95%)

docs.title: Tracking Error
docs.text:
Volatility of returns vs benchmark:

Text Only
tracking_error = std(portfolio_return - benchmark_return) * sqrt(252)

docs.title: Complete Risk Framework
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

// Signals
signal momentum:
  emit neutralize(zscore(ret(prices, 60)), by=sectors)

signal value:
  emit neutralize(zscore(book_to_market), by=sectors)

signal combined:
  emit 0.5 * momentum + 0.5 * value

// Volatility regime
signal vol_regime:
  market_vol = rolling_std(ret(market, 1), 20) * sqrt(252)
  long_vol = rolling_std(ret(market, 1), 60) * sqrt(252)
  high_vol = market_vol > long_vol * 1.3
  emit high_vol

// Risk-aware portfolio
portfolio risk_managed:
  // Base weights
  base_weights = rank(combined).long_short(
    top = 0.2,
    bottom = 0.2,
    cap = 0.03
  )

  // Scale down in high vol regime
  vol_scale = where(vol_regime, 0.7, 1.0)
  weights = base_weights * vol_scale

  constraints:
    // Exposure limits
    gross_exposure = 2.0
    net_exposure: [-0.1, 0.1]

    // Concentration limits
    max_position = 0.03
    max_sector = 0.20

    // Factor limits
    beta: [0.8, 1.2]

    // Turnover
    max_turnover = 0.25

    // Risk limit
    max_volatility = 0.12  // 12% target vol

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest rebal=21 from 2015-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Key Metrics to Monitor
docs.text: Metric Frequency Alert Level Daily P&L Daily >2% loss Drawdown Daily >10% Volatility Daily >1.5x target Gross Exposure Intraday Outside limits Factor Exposure Daily Outside limits

docs.title: Alert Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
alerts:
  - name: "Drawdown Alert"
    condition: drawdown > 0.10
    action: notify

  - name: "Circuit Breaker"
    condition: daily_loss > 0.03
    action: halt_trading

docs.title: Equal Risk Contribution
docs.text:
Each position contributes equally to risk:

Text Only
portfolio risk_parity:
  // Weight inversely to volatility
  vol = rolling_std(ret(prices, 1), 60)
  inv_vol = 1 / vol
  weights = inv_vol / sum(inv_vol)

docs.title: Factor Risk Budgeting
docs.text:
Allocate risk to factors:

Text Only
Total Risk Budget: 10%
  Market: 5%
  Momentum: 2%
  Value: 2%
  Idiosyncratic: 1%

docs.text:
Text Only
// Start

gross_exposure = 1.5

// Increase over time as confidence grows
max_position = 0.03
gross_exposure = 2.0

docs.title: 2. Multiple Defense Layers
3. Stress Testing
docs.text: Test against historical crises: - 2008 Financial Crisis - 2020 COVID Crash - 2022 Rate Hikes

docs.text: Define maximum acceptable: - Daily loss: 3% - Monthly loss: 10% - Maximum drawdown: 20%

docs.title: Chapter 7: Going to Production
docs.text: Deploy strategies from research to live trading.

docs.title: The Production Journey
docs.text:
Text Only
Research → Validation → Paper Trading → Live Trading → Monitoring

Each stage requires different considerations.

docs.title: Strategy Validation
docs.text:
• Walk-forward Sharpe > 0.5
  • Degradation < 50%
  • Positive after realistic costs
  • Stable parameters
  • Reasonable turnover

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Data pipeline operational
  • Broker connection tested
  • Alerting configured
  • Monitoring in place
  • Backup procedures documented

docs.title: Risk Controls
docs.text:
• Position limits set
  • Exposure limits configured
  • Circuit breakers enabled
  • Maximum loss defined

docs.title: Research Code
docs.text:
Text Only
// research/momentum.sig
data:
  source = "historical_prices.parquet"

params:
  lookback: range(40, 100, 20)  // Optimize

signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, lookback))

portfolio research:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(top=0.2, bottom=0.2)
  backtest walk_forward(...) from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

docs.title: Production Code
docs.text:
Text Only
// production/momentum_live.sig
config: "config/production.yaml"

data:
  source = "s3://mybucket/live_prices/"
  format = parquet

// Fixed parameters (from optimization)
signal momentum:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 60))

portfolio live:
  weights = rank(momentum).long_short(
    top = 0.2,
    bottom = 0.2,
    cap = 0.03
  )

  constraints:
    gross_exposure = 2.0
    net_exposure = 0.0
    max_sector = 0.20
    max_position = 0.03

  costs = tc.bps(10)

docs.title: Key Differences
docs.text: Aspect Research Production Parameters Optimized Fixed Data Source Historical Live feed Execution Simulated Real orders Monitoring Manual Automated Error Handling None Comprehensive

docs.title: Main Config File
docs.text:
YAML
# config/production.yaml

# Environment
environment: production
log_level: info

# Data
data:
  source: "s3://mybucket/prices/"
  refresh_interval: 1h
  cache_dir: "/var/cache/sigc"

# Strategy parameters
params:
  lookback: 60
  top_pct: 0.20
  position_cap: 0.03

# Risk limits
risk:
  max_position: 0.03
  max_sector: 0.20
  max_daily_loss: 0.03
  max_drawdown: 0.15
  gross_exposure: 2.0
  net_exposure_range: [-0.1, 0.1]

# Transaction costs
costs:
  commission_bps: 5
  spread_bps: 5
  market_impact_bps: 2

# Execution
execution:
  broker: alpaca
  order_type: limit
  limit_offset_bps: 5
  max_order_size: 0.02

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
YAML
# config/daemon.yaml
daemon:
  enabled: true
  pid_file: "/var/run/sigc/strategy.pid"
  log_file: "/var/log/sigc/strategy.log"

  schedule:
    # Compute weights before market close
    - cron: "30 15 * * 1-5"
      action: compute_weights
      timezone: "America/New_York"

    # Execute trades
    - cron: "55 15 * * 1-5"
      action: execute_trades
      timezone: "America/New_York"

docs.title: Start Daemon
docs.text:
Bash
# Start in paper mode first
sigc daemon start \
  --strategy production/momentum_live.sig \
  --config config/production.yaml \
  --paper

# Check status
sigc daemon status

docs.title: Why Paper Trade First?
docs.text:
1. Verify execution logic
  2. Test broker integration
  3. Validate data pipeline
  4. Check alerting
  5. Build confidence

docs.title: Duration
docs.text: Minimum 2-4 weeks of paper trading before going live.

docs.title: What to Check
docs.text:
• Trades execute correctly
  • Position sizes match targets
  • Transaction costs reasonable
  • No unexpected errors
  • Performance tracks expectations

docs.title: Final Pre-Live Checklist
docs.text:
Bash
# Run pre-flight checks
sigc preflight --config config/production.yaml
Text Only
Pre-Flight Checks
=================
[✓] Strategy file valid
[✓] Data source accessible
[✓] Broker connection OK
[✓] Account has sufficient capital
[✓] Risk limits configured
[✓] Alerting configured
[✓] Logging enabled

Ready for live trading.

docs.title: Start Live Trading
docs.text:
YAML
# Update config
execution:
  paper: false  # Switch to live
Bash
# Start live
sigc daemon start \
  --strategy production/momentum_live.sig \
  --config config/production.yaml \
  --live

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text: Metric Monitor Alert Daily P&L Continuous >2% loss Positions Every trade Divergence >10% Exposure Hourly Outside limits Data freshness Hourly >2h stale System health Continuous Any failure

docs.title: Alerting
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  slack:
    webhook: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
    channel: "#trading"

  rules:
    - name: "Large Loss"
      condition: daily_loss > 0.02
      severity: warning

    - name: "Circuit Breaker"
      condition: daily_loss > 0.03
      severity: critical
      action: halt_trading

    - name: "Trade Failure"
      condition: trade_failed
      severity: critical

docs.title: Morning Routine
docs.text:
1. Check overnight status
  2. Verify positions match targets
  3. Review any alerts
  4. Check data freshness
  5. Review broker account
Bash
# Morning checks
sigc status
sigc positions --compare target
sigc alerts --last 24h

docs.title: End of Day
docs.text:
1. Review today's trades
  2. Check P&L
  3. Verify reconciliation
  4. Note any issues
Bash
# EOD checks
sigc trades --today
sigc performance --today
sigc reconcile

docs.title: Weekly Review
docs.text:
1. Performance analysis
  2. Compare to backtest
  3. Check parameter drift
  4. Review any incidents
Bash
# Weekly report
sigc report --period 1w --output reports/weekly.html

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Data Stale: Text Only

Alert: Data not updated in 2 hours
Action: Check data pipeline, S3 access

Trade Rejected: Text Only

Alert: Order rejected - insufficient buying power
Action: Check account balance, reduce position sizes

Connection Lost: Text Only

Alert: Broker connection lost
Action: Auto-retry, failover to backup

docs.title: Recovery Procedures
docs.text:
YAML
recovery:
  auto_retry:
    max_attempts: 3
    delay_seconds: 60

  failover:
    enabled: true
    backup_broker: "backup_config.yaml"

  manual_intervention:
    - circuit_breaker_triggered
    - large_position_divergence

• Daily: Quick health check
  • Weekly: Performance review
  • Monthly: Deep analysis
  • Quarterly: Strategy review

docs.title: Chapter 8: Advanced Analytics
docs.text: Factor models, attribution, and deep performance analysis.

docs.title: What are Factors?
docs.text:
Factors are systematic drivers of returns:

Text Only
Stock Return = Factor Returns + Idiosyncratic Return
             = β₁F₁ + β₂F₂ + ... + ε

docs.title: Common Factor Models
docs.text:
CAPM (1 factor): Text Only

R = α + β(Rm - Rf) + ε

Fama-French 3 Factor: Text Only

R = α + β₁(MKT) + β₂(SMB) + β₃(HML) + ε

Carhart 4 Factor: Text Only

R = α + β₁(MKT) + β₂(SMB) + β₃(HML) + β₄(MOM) + ε

docs.title: Factor Definitions
docs.text: Factor Name Construction MKT Market Market return - Risk-free SMB Size Small cap - Large cap HML Value High B/M - Low B/M MOM Momentum Winners - Losers QMJ Quality High quality - Low quality

docs.title: Calculate Factor Exposures
docs.text:
Text Only
signal market_beta:
  stock_ret = ret(prices, 1)
  market_ret = ret(market, 1)
  beta = rolling_cov(stock_ret, market_ret, 60) / rolling_var(market_ret, 60)
  emit beta

signal size_exposure:
  // Negative of market cap (small = positive exposure)
  emit -zscore(ln(market_cap))

signal value_exposure:
  emit zscore(book_value / market_cap)

signal momentum_exposure:
  emit zscore(ret(prices, 252) - ret(prices, 21))

docs.title: Returns-Based Attribution
docs.text:
Decompose returns by source:

Text Only
Total Return = Factor Returns + Selection Return
             = Σ(βᵢ × Fᵢ) + α

docs.title: Holdings-Based Attribution
docs.text:
Decompose by position:

Text Only
Portfolio Return = Σ(wᵢ × rᵢ)
                 = Σ(contribution per stock)

Total Variance = Factor Variance + Idiosyncratic Variance
               = β'Σβ + σ²ε

  2021: 0.08
  2022: 0.22
  2023: 0.05
  2024: 0.03

Correlation has decreased over time (good for diversification).

docs.text:
YAML
stress_scenarios:
  - name: "Market Crash"
    market_return: -20%
    volatility_multiplier: 2.5

  - name: "Rate Shock"
    yield_change: +200bps
    duration_impact: true

docs.title: Complete Analytics Example
docs.text:
Text Only
data:
  source = "prices_fundamentals.parquet"
  format = parquet

signal momentum:
  emit neutralize(zscore(ret(prices, 60)), by=sectors)

signal value:
  emit neutralize(zscore(book_to_market), by=sectors)

signal combined:
  emit 0.5 * momentum + 0.5 * value

portfolio analyzed:
  weights = rank(combined).long_short(
    top = 0.2,
    bottom = 0.2,
    cap = 0.03
  )

  constraints:
    gross_exposure = 2.0
    net_exposure = 0.0
    max_sector = 0.20

  costs = tc.bps(10)

  backtest rebal=21 from 2010-01-01 to 2024-12-31

  // Request full analytics
  analytics:
    factor_attribution: true
    risk_attribution: true
    regime_analysis: true
    turnover_analysis: true
    stress_tests: true
Bash
sigc run strategy.sig --full-analytics --output reports/analysis.html

docs.title: Chapter 9: Deployment and Safety
docs.text: Build robust, safe trading systems.

docs.title: Safety Philosophy
docs.text:
"Plan for failure" - every component can fail:

  • Data sources become unavailable
  • Broker connections drop
  • Strategies behave unexpectedly
  • Markets move violently

Build systems that fail safely.

docs.title: Multiple Layers of Protection
docs.text:
Text Only
Layer 1: Pre-Trade Checks
    ↓
Layer 2: Position Limits
    ↓
Layer 3: Exposure Controls
    ↓
Layer 4: Loss Limits
    ↓
Layer 5: Circuit Breakers
    ↓
Layer 6: Kill Switch

docs.title: Order Validation
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  pre_trade_checks:
    enabled: true

    checks:
      # Price reasonableness
      - name: "Price Check"
        condition: abs(order_price - last_price) / last_price < 0.05
        message: "Order price >5% from last price"

      # Size limits
      - name: "Size Check"
        condition: order_value < max_order_value
        message: "Order exceeds maximum size"

      # No penny stocks
      - name: "Price Floor"
        condition: price > 1.0
        message: "Stock price below $1"

      # Liquidity check
      - name: "Liquidity"
        condition: order_size < avg_daily_volume * 0.01
        message: "Order exceeds 1% of ADV"

docs.title: What Gets Checked
docs.text: Check Purpose Example Price No fat fingers Order within 5% of market Size Limit impact <1% of ADV Exposure Risk limit Position <3% Liquidity Executability Stock has volume Restriction Compliance Not on restricted list

docs.title: Static Limits
docs.text:
YAML
risk:
  limits:
    # Per-position
    max_position: 0.03          # 3% max per stock
    max_position_value: 50000   # $50K max

    # Sector
    max_sector: 0.20            # 20% per sector

    # Total
    max_positions: 200          # Max 200 positions

docs.title: Dynamic Limits
docs.text:
YAML
risk:
  dynamic_limits:
    # Scale down after losses
    - condition: mtd_return < -0.05
      action:
        max_position: 0.02      # Reduce to 2%

    # Scale down in high vol
    - condition: vix > 30
      action:
        gross_exposure: 1.5     # Reduce exposure

docs.title: Daily Loss Limit
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  loss_limits:
    daily:
      warning: 0.02    # 2% - send alert
      hard: 0.03       # 3% - halt trading

docs.title: Drawdown Limits
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  loss_limits:
    drawdown:
      warning: 0.10    # 10% - send alert
      hard: 0.15       # 15% - halt trading

docs.title: Actions on Breach
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  loss_limits:
    daily:
      hard: 0.03
      action:
        - halt_new_trades
        - flatten_positions     # Optional
        - notify_admin

docs.title: Automatic Halts
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  circuit_breakers:
    # Daily loss
    - name: "Daily Loss"
      condition: daily_pnl < -0.03
      action: halt_trading
      reset: next_trading_day

    # Position divergence
    - name: "Position Divergence"
      condition: max_position_divergence > 0.10
      action: halt_trading
      reset: manual

    # System error
    - name: "Error Count"
      condition: error_count_1h > 10
      action: halt_trading
      reset: manual

docs.title: Market-Wide Halts
docs.text:
Respect exchange circuit breakers:

YAML
safety:
  market_circuit_breakers:
    enabled: true
    # Pause when market halts
    resume_delay: 300  # Wait 5 min after resume

docs.title: Emergency Stop
docs.text:
Bash
# Immediate halt - no new trades
sigc kill

# Flatten all positions
sigc kill --flatten

docs.title: Remote Kill Switch
docs.text:
YAML
safety:
  kill_switch:
    enabled: true

    # Multiple triggers
    triggers:
      - type: slack_command
        command: "/kill trading"

      - type: api
        endpoint: "/api/kill"
        auth: api_key

      - type: watchdog
        timeout: 300  # No heartbeat for 5 min

docs.title: Input Validation
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  validation:
    # Freshness
    max_age: 2h
    stale_action: alert

    # Completeness
    min_assets: 100
    missing_action: warn

    # Sanity
    price_change_limit: 0.50  # 50% daily change suspicious
    price_check_action: exclude

docs.title: Anomaly Detection
docs.text:
YAML
data:
  anomaly_detection:
    enabled: true

    checks:
      # Unusual price moves
      - type: price_change
        threshold: 3  # Standard deviations
        action: flag

      # Volume spikes
      - type: volume_spike
        threshold: 10x
        action: flag

      # Missing data
      - type: gaps
        max_gap: 5  # Trading days
        action: alert

docs.title: Order Types
docs.text:
YAML
execution:
  order_settings:
    # Use limit orders
    default_type: limit
    limit_offset_bps: 5

    # Timeout for unfilled orders
    timeout_seconds: 30
    timeout_action: cancel

    # Max retries
    max_retries: 3

docs.title: Slippage Protection
docs.text:
YAML
execution:
  slippage_protection:
    max_slippage_bps: 20
    action_on_breach: cancel

docs.title: Trade Reconciliation
docs.text:
YAML
execution:
  reconciliation:
    frequency: hourly
    tolerance: 0.01  # 1% position difference

    on_mismatch:
      - alert
      - log_discrepancy
      - auto_correct  # Optional

docs.title: Health Checks
docs.text:
YAML
monitoring:
  health_checks:
    - name: "Daemon Running"
      check: process_alive
      interval: 60s

    - name: "Broker Connected"
      check: broker_ping
      interval: 30s

    - name: "Data Fresh"
      check: data_age < 2h
      interval: 300s

    - name: "Positions Match"
      check: position_reconciliation
      interval: 3600s

docs.title: Alert Escalation
docs.text:
YAML
alerting:
  escalation:
    warning:
      - channel: slack
        target: "#trading-alerts"

    critical:
      - channel: slack
        target: "#trading-alerts"
      - channel: pagerduty
        target: "trading-oncall"
      - channel: sms
        target: "+1234567890"

docs.title: What to Log
docs.text:
YAML
logging:
  events:
    # All trades
    - type: trade
      level: info
      retain: 7y

    # Signals
    - type: signal_computation
      level: debug
      retain: 30d

    # Errors
    - type: error
      level: error
      retain: 7y

    # Configuration changes
    - type: config_change
      level: info
      retain: 7y

docs.title: Audit Trail
docs.text:
YAML
audit:
  enabled: true

  capture:
    - all_orders
    - position_changes
    - config_changes
    - user_actions

  storage:
    type: immutable
    retention: 7y

docs.title: Backup Procedures
docs.text:
YAML
backup:
  # State backup
  state:
    frequency: daily
    destination: s3://backups/state/
    retain: 90d

  # Configuration
  config:
    frequency: on_change
    destination: git
    retain: forever

  # Logs
  logs:
    frequency: daily
    destination: s3://backups/logs/
    retain: 7y

docs.title: Recovery Procedures
docs.text:
1. System Failure Bash

    # Restart daemon
sigc daemon restart

# Verify positions
sigc reconcile

# Resume trading
sigc daemon resume

  2. Data Corruption Bash

    # Restore from backup
sigc restore --state latest

# Verify state
sigc status --verify

  3. Broker Issues Bash

    # Switch to backup broker
sigc broker switch --to backup

docs.title: Complete Safety Configuration
docs.text:
YAML
# config/safety.yaml

safety:
  # Pre-trade
  pre_trade_checks:
    enabled: true
    checks:
      - price_reasonableness
      - size_limit
      - liquidity
      - restricted_list

  # Limits
  limits:
    max_position: 0.03
    max_sector: 0.20
    gross_exposure: 2.0
    net_exposure: [-0.1, 0.1]

  # Loss limits
  loss_limits:
    daily:
      warning: 0.02
      hard: 0.03
    drawdown:
      warning: 0.10
      hard: 0.15

  # Circuit breakers
  circuit_breakers:
    - name: daily_loss
      trigger: daily_pnl < -0.03
      action: halt

    - name: position_divergence
      trigger: max_divergence > 0.10
      action: halt

  # Kill switch
  kill_switch:
    enabled: true
    methods: [api, slack, watchdog]

# Monitoring
monitoring:
  health_checks:
    interval: 60s
    checks: [daemon, broker, data, positions]

  prometheus:
    enabled: true
    port: 9090

# Alerting
alerting:
  channels:
    - type: slack
      webhook: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK}
    - type: pagerduty
      key: ${PAGERDUTY_KEY}

  rules:
    - condition: daily_loss > 0.02
      severity: warning
    - condition: daily_loss > 0.03
      severity: critical
    - condition: system_error
      severity: critical

# Logging
logging:
  level: info
  output: /var/log/sigc/
  retention: 7y

# Audit
audit:
  enabled: true
  events: [trades, positions, config]
  storage: immutable
